urn:contentItem:6JGP-35J3-RSH5-M0K1-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Xcel Energy reports 1.9% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:21ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Xcel Energy reports 1.9% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit

AMERICAN RESULTS

Xcel Energy (NASDAQ:XEL), announced net profit of $556m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 1.9% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 15% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 15.1% from $483.0m to $556.0m

- Total revenue was up 2.9% from $3.9b to $4.0b

- EPS growth 6% from 84.0c to 89.0c

- EBITDA to Revenues was up from 29.4% to 31.8%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 12.4% to 13.8%

- EBIT to Revenues was up from 10.8% to 12.7%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was up from 10.8% to 12.7%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was up from 18.6% to 19.1%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 6%, Revenue was up 2.9% and Net Profit was up 15.1%.

ACTIVITIES:

Xcel Energy Inc., headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota, is a leading U.S.-based utility holding company that operates primarily in the electric and natural gas sectors. As a major provider serving approximately 3.7 million electric customers and 2.1 million natural gas customers across eight states, including Colorado, Minnesota, and Wisconsin, the company's core activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas. Xcel Energy focuses on reliable energy delivery through a diversified portfolio that includes coal, nuclear, and increasingly, renewable sources. The fastest-growing segments of its business are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power, driven by strategic investments in clean energy projects and a commitment to reducing carbon emissions, with goals to achieve 100% carbon-free electricity by 2050. This shift not only supports regulatory demands and customer preferences for sustainable options but also positions the company for long-term growth in a transitioning energy market. Xcel Energy maintains a strong emphasis on operational efficiency, safety, and innovation to ensure stable returns for shareholders.

MARKET POSITION:

Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:XEL), the NASDAQ's 3rd largest Utility company by market cap, jumped $14.34 (or 21.0%) in the trailing 12 months to May 01, 2026 to close at $82.58. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 41.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -20.6%. Average daily volume of 4.4 million shares in the trailing year was 1.3 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JGG-3XS3-S7C2-R1D0-00000-00CARGILL INCORPORATED - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisSTOCKCARGILL INCORPORATED - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: CARGILL

PermID4296555324Websitehttp://www.cargill.comIndustryAgribusiness & Food ProcessingAddressPO Box 9300 Minneapolis, Minnesota 55440-9300 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

+ CARGILL [3 News That Matters]

+ SUSTAINABLE SOURCING DRIVE: CARGILL FUNDS TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION FOR INDONESIAN COCOA

Cargill has announced a Rp38.8 billion (US$2.25 million) investment to support more than 7,800 cocoa farmers in Indonesia through Rainforest Alliance certification, disclosed on 26th of April 2026. The initiative reinforces Cargill's commitment to sustainable sourcing and empowering farmers in the sector.The premium payments amount to Rp35 billion for Central Sulawesi and Rp3.8 billion for East Nusa Tenggara, designed to supplement cocoa income and ensure compliance with recognized sustainability standards.In Central Sulawesi, over 5,600 farmers participate, including more than 600 women; from May 2023 to February 2026, about 16,000 metric tonnes of Rainforest Alliance-certified cocoa beans were sourced from Parigi Moutong, Poso and North Morowali. In East Nusa Tenggara, around 2,200 farmers contributed nearly 2,000 metric tonnes from Ende, Sikka, EastManggarai and East Flores. The Rainforest Alliance certification framework supports transparency and traceability in Cargill's global supply chain, helping farmers meet international market demands.

+ CARGILL'S REGINA PLANT SETS NEW BENCHMARK IN CANADIAN CANOLA PROCESSING

Cargill has opened a new canola processing facility in Regina, Saskatchewan, expanding domestic processing to meet rising demand for food and renewable fuels. With a capacity of about 1 million metric tons per year, the plant is aimed at boosting market access for western Canadian farmers.The Regina site will turn canola into oil for food and renewable energy and high-protein meal for animal feed. Its location at the Global Transportation Hub provides strong rail links to markets across Canada and beyond, and the project is expected to create over 100 local jobs.Jeff Vassart, president of Cargill Canada, said the facility strengthens the company's ability to connect farmers to global demand. Cargill notes the project also advances value creation within Canada by reducing reliance on exports of raw seed, and it builds on existing plants in Camrose and Clavet.

+ CARGILL'S CARVE AI WINS EDISON BRONZE, DEMONSTRATING AI IN PROTEIN PROCESSING

WAYZATA, MINN. — Cargill has earned a Bronze award at the 2026 Edison Awards for its CarVe AI-driven computer vision system, which measures red meat yield in real time. The award was announced on the 22nd of April 2026.CarVe improves beef-cutting accuracy and provides video footage to reduce waste and boost efficiency across Cargill's protein operations, according to the company.Earlier this year, Cargill also received the 2026 BIG AI Award, which highlighted its AI technology applications across operations, with CarVe noted as a leading example in protein processing. Fort Morgan, Colorado remains the first site using CarVe, with plans to extend to High River in Ontario as part of a broader push to advance technology across the business.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ INGREDION [3 News That Matters]

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) PARTNERS WITH LANTMANNEN TO EXPAND PLANT-BASED PROTEIN IN EUROPE AT VITAFOODS EUROPE 2026

Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) Manchester, UK – Ingredion will showcase its protein fortification, texture enhancement, and sugar reduction capabilities at Vitafoods Europe 2026 in Barcelona, with the date of this announcement 23rd April 2026.The display will feature VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD for cold-pressed applications, offering texture improvement and protein fortification while maintaining softness over shelf life. The product also demonstrates versatility in bakery applications, enabling protein enrichment without compromising quality or taste.Ingredion continues its collaboration with Lantmannen, first announced in 2024, to expand European-sourced, plant-based protein solutions and strengthen regional sourcing for customers in Europe.Visit stand #3D252 at Vitafoods Europe in Barcelona, Spain, to learn more about Ingredion’s ingredient solutions.

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) GAINS EDGE IN AGRIBUSINESS AI PUSH BARCLAYS FINDS

Barclays Reports On AI in Agribusiness in the Americas, Highlighting Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) as positioned to gain from an AI-enabled pivot. The note, published on 22nd April 2026, states Ingredion is moving from a commodity-focused model toward a solutions orientation, leveraging its ingredient library to address unmet customer needs and monetize AI more quickly given its solid balance sheet. The bank flags seeds and crop chemicals as sectors likely to see AI-driven progress, with Ingredion and Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) identified as early adopters; FMC (NYSE:FMC) could see delays due to restructuring. Barclays also mentions AI could improve yields in protein processing, reduce recalls, and support profitability. Overall, AI is expected to reinforce leaders and drive efficiency across agriculture, with Ingredion positioned to benefit as the market evolves

+ INGREDION APPOINTS INTERIM CFO AS IT EXPANDS PARTNERSHIPS AND BOARD CHANGES

Ingredion has appointed Jason Payant as Interim Chief Financial Officer, effective April 1, following the resignation of James D. Gray as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer on 31st March 2026.Payant, 55, has been with Ingredion since 2012 and has led finance teams across the company. He has been the Vice President, Finance for Global Texture & Healthful Solutions since November 2025 and will continue in that role while serving as interim CFO.The company noted there are no arrangements or disclosures between Payant and others about the appointment, and no material interests involving Payant or his immediate family. He will receive a monthly cash compensation of $25,000 in addition to his existing pay and benefits during the interim period.Other news includes a quarterly dividend of $0.82 per share payable on 21st April 2026 to stockholders of record as of 1st April 2026, a new collaboration with Shiru to develop functional proteins and prebiotics using Shiru's AI platform, and Univar Solutions LLC becoming the exclusive distributor for Ingredion Pharma Solutions in the United States and Canada. The company also disclosed board changes with Gregory B. Kenny retiring after more than 21 years on the board and Siobhan Talbot joining effective 1st April 2026.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KOCH [3 News That Matters]

+ KOCH FERTILIZER ATTAINS ENERGY STAR STATUS ACROSS FIVE SITES, DEMONSTRATING ENERGY EFFICIENCY

Koch Fertilizer has earned ENERGY STAR certifications for five plants, as announced on 23rd April 2026, underscoring the company's commitment to energy efficiency and environmental stewardship.The five facilities—Enid, Oklahoma; Wever, Iowa; Fort Dodge, Iowa; Beatrice, Nebraska; and Brandon, Manitoba—have earned 25 ENERGY STAR certifications since 2019, with each site operating in the top 25% for energy performance.Beatrice Plant Manager Jason Stowell said the recognition reflects the teamwork behind Koch Fertilizer and that 'strong site ownership, disciplined execution, and a culture that values both operational excellence and environmental stewardship' are essential. Ev Shishkin, the Wever plant manager, added that ENERGY STAR certification goes beyond benchmarks and signals year-over-year efficiency improvements and disciplined operations. 'We focus on reducing waste, improving reliability, and investing in upgrades that help us produce essential products while being mindful of our footprint,' Shishkin explained.The ENERGY STAR program recognizes facilities that meet stringent energy performance standards and underscores Koch Fertilizer's ongoing drive to embed sustainability into its operations.

+ CPH2 PARTNERS WITH KOCH MODULAR TO ASSESS 100 MW LICENSING OPPORTUNITY FOR MFE IN NA MARKETS

CPH2 (AIM:CPH2) has disclosed a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding with Koch Modular Process Systems to study the feasibility of manufacturing and licensing its Membrane-Free Electrolyser hydrogen technology in North America. The agreement, signed on 14th April 2026, covers an initial 24-month window to evaluate technical, commercial, and operational considerations, including localization and supply-chain logistics, with a view to a potential binding deal for up to 100 MW of modular hydrogen capacity in the region.Markets targeted under the initiative include Mexico, the United States, and Canada, with discussions focusing on deploying CPH2's technology through licensing and potential manufacturing arrangements. CEO Jon Duffy noted that the collaboration will involve feasibility studies and supply-chain assessments, supported by Koch Modular's expertise in process and project delivery, to advance licensing opportunities across North America. Koch Modular Vice President Brian Loftus highlighted the strategic value of CPH2's patented MFE and said the partnership could leverage Koch Modular's design and manufacturing capabilities to broaden access to hydrogen in the North American market.CPH2 operates as the holding company for Clean Power Hydrogen Group Limited, which has accumulated a decade of R&D and holds several patents in hydrogen and oxygen production technologies. The group aims to achieve the lowest lifetime LCoH in decentralized applications across wastewater treatment, curtailed wind power capture, data center backup, medical and life sciences, and heavy-duty mobility.

+ BARBER SPRINT CHALLENGE HIGHLIGHTS RISING TALENT, KOCH MOMENTUM GROWS

Barber Motorsports Park hosted the Porsche Sprint Challenge North America by Yokohama on the Mar. 30 weekend, featuring 15-year-old Karel Staut and Ford Koch delivering notable performances for Koch Motorsports as the series returns to the Children's of Alabama Indy Grand Prix. Staut claimed pole and then won both GT3 Cup Pro-Am races, extending his streak with margins of 15 seconds in Race 1 and six seconds in Race 2, underscoring his potential in the Porsche-backed lineup. Koch joined the action late but made a strong impression in the Cayman GT4 RS Clubsport class, taking Race 1 by a flag-to-flag margin and second in the day’s other race before reclaiming the lead in Race 2 to finish two seconds ahead. The GT3 Cup Masters also featured drama, with Scalzo moving from fifth to the top in the first race and Boden adding a Race 2 victory in a celebratory return to form.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) PROMOTES MINDFUL SNACKING WITH EDUCATION CAMPAIGN UNDER SNACK NA SWAK IN THE PHILIPPINES

Mondelez International Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) launched its Snack na Swak initiative on Apr. 27, 2026, to reshape Filipino snacking by promoting mindful choices within a 250-calorie range. The initiative recognizes snacking as a cultural practice and aims to transform it into a mindful act through education and accessibility. Caitlin Nina Punzalan, Corporate and Government Affairs Lead at Mondelez International, explains the campaign addresses obesity and diabetes concerns and supports a balanced diet that includes indulgences when mindful. The program emphasizes education and accessibility, encouraging consumers to understand nutrition labels and portion sizes. During an event at Common Ground in Rockwell, Jo Sebastian guided participants through the Portion Puzzle, while Arlene Reario urged clearer labeling and portion-controlled options; Mondelez International says it will promote portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier snacks and reframe Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) ELEVATES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VP OF MARKETING

On 22nd April 2026, Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) elevated Anil Viswanathan to VP of Marketing after nearly eight years with the company, most recently as Senior Director of Marketing. Viswanathan will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the India brand portfolio, including brand equity, innovation, and activation, and will lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice in partnership with India leadership and Global Technology Leadership. His career began at Cadbury India in 2008 as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business, followed by VP Marketing – Chocolates at Cadbury Kraft India from 2011 to 2013. He joined Mondelez International in 2014, relocating to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, returning to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA and advancing to Senior Director of Marketing in 2020. This appointment reinforces Mondelez’s focus on strengthening its India market position in a digitized consumer environment

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) MIXED VIEW AS UBS CUTS TARGET WHILE BOFA LIFTS OUTLOOK

Bank of America raises Mondelez price target to $65 from $62 and maintains a Buy rating after constructive management updates from March meetings; the upgrade is seen as a positive signal for the snack giant's growth trajectory. On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterates its optimism amid ongoing talks with European chocolate retailers, suggesting potential expansion of Mondelez's footprint in EuropeUBS lowers its price target to $62 from $63 and maintains Neutral, forecasting a modest improvement in first-quarter results for consumer staples names, but flagging inflation as a risk for 2026 earningsMondelez operates in chocolate, biscuits and baked snacks and has exposure to candy, cheese, grocery items and powdered beverages, reinforcing a diversified portfolio that supports its dividend appeal among NASDAQ peers. The stock remains among the NASDAQ stocks with the highest dividends

+ TYSON FOODS [3 News That Matters]

+ TYSON FOODS, INC. (NYSE:TSN) DEPLOYS MEALS THAT MATTER TEAM TO AID NEW LONDON FLOOD VICTIMS

Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE:TSN) deploys its Meals That Matter disaster relief team to serve hot meals to New London flood victims at 3620 County Road D, in the lower parking lotFrom 11 a.m. to 4 p.m., through April 22, the effort will operate at the New London facility, which employs over 400 local residents. The Meals That Matter crew is expected to deliver between 2,000 and more than 10,000 meals daily, depending on needMichelle Mullins, director of food business operations, food loss and waste at Tyson Foods, noted, 'When communities are facing hardship, we believe one of the most powerful things we can do is show up with a hot meal.' Since its launch in 2012, Meals That Matter has supported more than 30 natural disaster sites across hurricanes, floods, and tornadoesTyson Foods remains committed to aiding those affected and maintaining its role as a community partner during challenging times

+ TYSON FOODS, INC. (NYSE:TSN) BROADENS SAVORY SNACK LINE WITH STAR, TROPHY AND BALL SHAPED NUGGETS

Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE:TSN) Introduces Game Time nuggets shaped as a star, a trophy and a ball to broaden its sports-themed offerings. On 20th April 2026, Tyson details that the nuggets are made from 100% all-natural white meat chicken, lightly breaded and fully cooked, with 20 grams of protein per serving.Last year’s NFL-licensed nuggets demonstrated the company’s ability to align with fan-favorite teams such as the Denver Broncos and the Dallas Cowboys, and this year Tyson extends the approach to a wider audience. Available now at select retailers nationwide, the Game Time lineup sits alongside other innovations like Restaurant Style Waffle Flavored Breaded Chicken Breast Bites and Chicken Dip Wedges.Tyson positions the product as part of a broader effort to improve the game-day experience for fans during the busy summer sports period

+ TYSON FOODS JOINS RAZORBACKS IN HISTORIC SPONSORSHIP, JERSEY PATCH DEBUTS IN COLLEGIATE ATHLETICS

Tyson Foods is expanding its partnership with the University of Arkansas Department of Intercollegiate Athletics, with the 2026-2027 season marking a broader brand role for the company. Tyson will become the Official Protein of the Razorbacks and will have jersey patches across all 19 varsity teams, one of the first jersey-patch sponsorships in college sports. This announcement was made on the 4th of March, 2026.The broader agreement includes extensive brand visibility across Razorback Athletics—jersey patches, logos on fields and courts, and media backdrops at press events—along with brand ambassador programs that engage Arkansas student-athletes in community and performance initiatives. Tyson Foods’ leadership described the partnership as transformative for Razorback Athletics and a strategic way to connect with the next generation of consumers through sports and protein that fuels performance. The deal is facilitated in collaboration with Learfield’s Razorback Sports Properties.

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (SGX:F34) REAFFIRMS ASIA AND AFRICA FOCUS AMID GEOPOLITICAL UNCERTAINTY

Wilmar International Limited (SGX:F34) Posts Indirect Impacts From Middle East Conflict, Maintaining Structural Resilience. The 21st April 2026 announcement shows Wilmar anticipates indirect operational effects from the conflict and says its corporate structure remains resilient to market fluctuations. Direct exposure to the Middle East remains limited, with regional contributions representing a single-digit percentage of annual revenue, while indirect effects include higher costs for insurance, freight, and fertilizers, and volatility in commodity prices.The agribusiness group emphasizes that it has managed the shocks in recent years and does not expect a material change to its framework. Its core investments and operations lie in Asia and Africa, and long-term capital allocation decisions have not changed due to uncertainty about the conflict duration. The company plans to address questions at the April 23 AGM, following inquiries from the Securities Investors Association (Singapore).Wilmar also underscores its commitment to compliance, noting investigations in Indonesia and China. It cites a 2025 Chinese court ruling involving contract fraud at a local unit and charges against a general manager in Indonesia, adding that any staff found to breach instructions will face disciplinary action. Shares stood at S$3.84, down 0.3% prior to the disclosure.

+ WILMAR ADVANCES FINANCING STRATEGY WITH $1.5 BILLION SYNDICATED LOAN HIGHLIGHTING ESG FOCUS

Wilmar International Limited has secured a $1.5 billion syndicated loan facility, announced on 31st March 2026, including a greenshoe option.The facility carries a competitive interest rate and reflects Wilmar's robust financial health and market position.The greenshoe option provides additional borrowing capacity for potential acquisitions or investments in technologies that support the company's sustainability goals, complemented by a favorable MSCI ESG score.As Wilmar pursues continued operational efficiency and market expansion, investors will monitor execution and its impact on future performance.

+ PAKISTAN GOVERNANCE CONCERNS WEIGH ON WILMAR'S UNITY FOODS STAKE; $150 MILLION LOSS

Wilmar International has reported a $150 million loss tied to its Unity Foods stake in Pakistan, disclosed on 2nd March 2026, due to Unity Foods’ incomplete disclosures that affected the stake’s valuation.Unity Foods reportedly did not respond to several requests for clarification on key financial and operational data, raising questions about governance in Pakistan's food and agribusiness sectors and potentially dampening foreign investor confidence.The episode illustrates the risks of cross-border investments in emerging markets where transparency and regulatory practices may diverge from global standards.Industry analysts will monitor the implications for Wilmar's investment strategy and for broader investor sentiment toward Pakistan's market as governance-related concerns persist.

+ GENERAL MILLS [3 News That Matters]

+ GENERAL MILLS, INC. (NYSE:GIS) TRIPLING E-COMMERCE GROWTH HIGHLIGHTS ONLINE-OFFLINE INTEGRATION

General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) positions omnichannel execution at the heart of its growth framework as it pursues more consistent engagement across channels. Apr. 14, 2026 marked another milestone in its online and offline integration.The company leverages portfolio scale and high-impact marketing events to drive differential retail execution and has increased retail media investments to attract convenience- and value-seeking shoppers.This approach has produced a tripling of e-commerce growth across its top five e-commerce retail partners, while household penetration rose in seven of the top ten North America retail categories through the fiscal third quarter.With the omnichannel framework strengthening retailer partnerships, General Mills expects the strategy to support long-term growth as consumer shopping shifts toward digital and in-store experiences.

+ GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) REPORTS €1.7 BILLION OF JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES ISSUANCE WITH FIXED-TO-FIXED RATE STRUCTURE

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) is issuing €1.7 billion of junior subordinated notes to strengthen its capital structure. Series A comprises €1.0 billion at 4.750% until 16 July 2031, then resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points. Series B comprises €700 million at 5.250% until 16 July 2034, then resets to the swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series include fixed-to-fixed rate structures with step-up margins of 25 basis points after five years from the first reset and 75 basis points after 20 years. Interest payments occur annually on 16 July beginning in 2026, and the company retains the right to redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. The notes are classified as junior subordinated debt with a lower claim on assets in liquidation, reflecting a strategic use of capital structure to support future growth initiatives.

+ GENERAL MILLS REVAMPS CEREAL OFFERING TO BOOST RELEVANCE AND GROWTH

General Mills unveiled two new cereals on 7th April 2026: Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney's Moana, as part of a strategic refresh of its cereal line.The company emphasizes removing artificial colors and expanding flavor options through collaborations, aiming to respond to consumer preference for natural ingredients and storytelling in packaging.The move comes amid attention on the firm's financial posture, with shares at 37.67 dollars, roughly 7.9 percent below a 40.89 target, and a recent pattern of stock weakness.Analysts note a cautious stance on near term profitability, with earnings projected to decline about 6.3 percent per year, and valuation concerns persist as shares sit well below estimated fair value and the price-to-earnings ratio sits around 9.1; debt levels invite scrutiny. Investors will monitor whether the new cereals help bolster cereal segment revenue and support a more favorable long term trajectory.

Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE:CAG) Posts a 550 million pesos investment to upgrade Irapuato plant facilities and increase capacity to meet growing demand, with packaging enhancements and process optimization as of Apr. 22, 2026 (about $31.9 million).The Irapuato facility, a strategic pillar for Conagra Brands Mexico, produces ACT II, Del Monte and Hunt's products and accounts for 94% of the company's Mexico sales volume. + CONAGRA EXPANDS FAYETTEVILLE FACILITY IN $220 MILLION INVESTMENT, BOOSTING LOCAL ECONOMY

Conagra Brands will expand its Fayetteville facility to boost chicken production capacity as part of a $220 million, multi-year investment. The plan was disclosed by the Arkansas Economic Development Commission in a release dated 6th March 2026.Around 100 new jobs are expected over the next five years, contributing to Fayetteville’s economy and workforce.Construction is slated to begin in late 2026, marking a key milestone for Conagra as it expands its presence in the local market and broadens its protein portfolio.

+ BANQUET MEGA BREAKFAST BOWLS HIT SHELVES, TARGETING BUSY MORNINGS WITH HIGH-PROTEIN OPTION

On 11th February 2026, Conagra Brands introduced Banquet MEGA Breakfast Bowls, offering 30 grams of protein per bowl to tap the $626 million breakfast bowls category.The bowls use tray-in-tray steaming technology to maintain quality during cooking and are designed for quick prep. They come in four varieties: Meat Lovers, Bacon, Sausage Bowl, and Sausage & Gravy, each featuring eggs, potatoes, and a cheese sauce or gravy option.As a market leader in single-serve frozen meals, breakfast pot pies, and frozen cooked sausage links, Conagra plans a February rollout to grocery and mass retail stores, expanding protein-packed breakfast options for busy mornings.

HENDERSON, Ky. — On April 27, 2026, Perdue Farms distributed a truckload of chicken products to Ohio County Healthcare for 1,000 workers and first responders. As a proud supporter of the statewide Food Is Medicine initiative, OCH values the alignment between healthcare and agriculture in expanding access to nutritious food.\"

+ PERDUE FARMS ELEVATES ANDY URBAN TO CHIEF COMMERCIAL OFFICER

Perdue Foods named Andy Urban as chief commercial officer, effective 8th April 2026, to oversee sales, marketing, customer development, revenue management and innovation. Urban brings more than 35 years of experience in multi-channel consumer packaged goods across retail, food service and international markets, and has been with Perdue since 2009 in various senior roles, most recently leading the Transformation Office. Kevin McAdams, Perdue Farms president and CEO, praised Urban as a proven commercial leader with a deep understanding of the business, customers and culture, noting his track record for driving growth and building high-performing teams. "As CCO, Urban will guide the commercial organization to strengthen customer partnerships and deliver competitive, sustainable growth," he said. Urban added, "I'm honored to step into this role at a pivotal time for Perdue."

+ DELVAL RECEIVES MAJOR GIFT FROM PERDUE TO BUILD POULTRY SCIENCE CENTER

Delaware Valley University received a $100,000 gift from Perdue Farms and the Franklin P. and Arthur W. Perdue Foundation to support construction of a new Poultry Science Center on the main campus. The gift was announced on 9th February 2026 and aligns with Perdue Delivering Hope To Our Neighbors initiative that backs community well being and agricultural education.The grant was presented to DelVal President Dr. Benjamin E. Rusiloski and Dean of Agriculture and Environmental Science Dr. Broc Sandelin during a ceremony on Wednesday. Sandelin said the poultry industry is growing and the center will provide hands on learning and help launch a dedicated Poultry Science academic program at the university.DelVal described the planned facility as featuring a broiler building, a layer building and a pen research trials building, along with laboratories, classrooms and demonstration spaces. A study room within the center will bear the Perdue name in recognition of the company ongoing support for poultry science education. Mike Levengood, Perdue Chief Animal Care Officer, noted that investing in education strengthens hands on learning and prepares students for careers in the poultry industry.DelVal is actively pursuing industry partnerships to finalize the project and invites interested parties to contact the Office of Development and Alumni Affairs at development@delval.edu or 215.489.2397. The Perdue Foundation, established in 1957, supports community initiatives on behalf of Perdue Farms as part of its commitment to responsible food and agriculture.

MONTREAL, 15th April 2026 /CNW/ ADM Aeroports de Montreal Posts a strategic partnership with Veriforce Canada to bolster contractor compliance and training management across its operations. The SafeContractor Canada platform will provide ADM with real-time visibility into contractor qualifications, certifications, and training records, helping streamline onboarding and site-access processes. Jean Charron, Deputy Director of Occupational Health and Safety (Construction) at ADM, notes that strict compliance is central to protecting airport users and maintaining operational integrity. + ADM BROADENS BOARD TO ACCELERATE SOUTH AMERICAN OPERATIONS

Archer-Daniels-Midland Co (NYSE:ADM) has added Michael McMurray to its board, a veteran with more than 30 years of experience in the industry. The move, completed on 31st March 2026, is designed to bolster ADM 's South American growth strategy as the company seeks to strengthen governance and execution capability.McMurray's background includes senior roles at LyondellBasell Industries and Royal Dutch Shell, bringing expertise in financial management and strategic leadership that the company says will support its regional objectives. Juan Luciano, the CEO and board chair, stressed that McMurray's proven capability in business strategy and mergers and acquisitions will be valuable as ADM pursues expansion in the region.Beyond the board change, ADM has been active in engaging investors and industry stakeholders about its strategic initiatives. The company highlighted opportunities and challenges in the agricultural sector during participation at industry events such as the Bank of America 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference, emphasizing its commitment to transparency and collaboration as it strengthens its market position in South America. + ADM ANNOUNCES MOVE TOWARD GLOBAL HQ, EXPANDS DECATUR TIES AND COMMUNITY SUPPORT

ADM announced on 30th March 2026 that it is exploring a new global headquarters and customer center to enhance its global presence and customer-focused operations.Locations are being evaluated with public officials and advisors; no site has been disclosed. The plan includes relocating a small corporate team with about 100 job transfers and establishing a new IT center with around 100 roles in the coming years, with no layoffs.ADM will keep its 4,400-employee Decatur workforce, and the Decatur office will be designated the North American headquarters. The company also outlined multi-year commitments to the Decatur community: $250,000 annually for three years for a unified marketing campaign via the Economic Development Corporation of Decatur & Macon County; $500,000 per year for five years to Decatur Public School District 61; and $1 million per year in community support for at least the next decade.Looking ahead, Woertz expressed optimism about a strong North American harvest and the GrainCorp acquisition, with the new center positioning ADM for continued growth in the agricultural sector.

+ BUNGE GLOBAL SA (NYSE:BG) REPORTS EXPANSION OF SECURITIZATION FACILITY TO $2 BILLION ALONGSIDE VITERRA INTEGRATION

Bunge Global SA (NYSE:BG) raises its securitization capacity to $2 billion from $1.5 billion, with Finacity guiding the expansion, on April 13th, 2026. The integration of Viterra Limited into the program increases capacity and strengthens receivables management for Bunge. The company operates in more than 50 countries with a workforce of about 34,000, covering grain origination, storage, distribution and oilseed processing. This expansion supports Bunge's ongoing efforts to meet evolving demand in the agribusiness sector while advancing sustainability initiatives. Finacity, a White Oak Global Advisors Company, has structured over $200 billion in annual receivables volume and provides expertise in off-balance-sheet securitizations under US GAAP and IFRS, underscoring its essential role in Bunge's capital strategy.

+ ANALYSTS LIFT BUNGE GLOBAL RATING ON MIDDLE EAST TENSIONS AND MARGIN STRENGTH

Bunge Global SA has secured a rating upgrade, aided by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East that have lifted oil prices and margins for soybean oil and other essential commodities.Demand for soy oil, a feedstock for biodiesel, has risen, bolstering Bunge Global's positioning in the agricultural sector amid the current environment.An analysis dated 26th March 2026 notes that while the tensions may serve as a short term catalyst, the company is focused on long term execution to sustain growth, with an EPS target of $15.Analysts have moved the rating from Hold to Buy, citing potential upside from 74% to 185%, and the company expects to capitalize on strategic synergies through innovation and efficiency in sourcing and production as it navigates market volatility.

+ BUNGE GETS UPGRADES AS INVESTOR DAY SIGNALS EARNINGS PATH

Bank of America raised Bunge Global SA's price target to $130 from $125 and reiterated a Buy rating after its Investor Day, noting midcycle EPS of $13-$15 and stronger synergy and margin opportunities.On the same day, BMO Capital increased its target to $135 from $130 and kept an Outperform rating, citing a clear plan to reach the $15 mid-cycle EPS target by 2030.Bunge, a global agribusiness with brands such as Green Giant and Mrs. Dash, aims to translate Investor Day insights into improved earnings and longer-term growth as it scales its platform and technology.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-S5D3-RTDH-2467-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across central Nebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION U.S. CONSTRUCTION DEMAND REMAINS STRONG AMID SHIFTING MARKET CONDITIONS

Turner Construction Company announced that the First Quarter 2026 Turner Building Cost Index-which measures costs in the non-residential building construction market in the United States-increased to the value of 1530. This represents a 1.32% quarterly increase from the Fourth Quarter 2025 and a 4.87% yearly increase from the First Quarter 2025.

"Demand across the U.S. construction market remains solid, though conditions vary by region and sector. High-growth areas such as data centers, manufacturing, and semiconductor facilities are driving strong demand, particularly in the Midwest and Southeast, while traditional commercial construction has softened in several markets. At the same time, architecture and engineering activity appears to be stabilizing. Material costs such as steel, aluminum, and copper have experienced increases during the first quarter," said Attilio Rivetti, the Turner vice president responsible for compiling the Cost Index. "We are closely monitoring rising oil prices, which could begin to impact transportation and material costs in the coming quarter."

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION STRONGER MINDS, STRONGER TEAMS: EMPOWERING THE BIAL TEAM

Investing in people is the cornerstone of high-performance culture. We recently concluded an intensive training program for the Bengaluru International Airport team (BIAL), centered on the theme "Stronger Minds, Stronger Teams!!"

the session delved deep into performance excellence and the attitudes that drive successful outcomes. From mastering self-direction to understanding the emotional drivers behind decision-making, the day was packed with actionable insights designed to elevate both personal and professional growth.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

SECTION 1: BIOGRAPHY: PATRICK R. GRUBER, CEO AND DIRECTOR

Patrick R. Gruber, age 56, has served as Chief Executive Officer and a director of the Company since 2007. Prior to joining the Company, from 2005 to 2007, Mr. Gruber was President and Chief Executive Officer of Outlast Technologies, Inc. ("Outlast Technologies"), a technology and marketing company primarily serving the textile industry, where he was responsible for all aspects of Outlast Technologies' business. Previously, Mr. Gruber co-founded NatureWorks LLC (formerly Cargill Dow, LLC) ("NatureWorks") and served as Vice President, Technology and Operations, and Chief Technology Officer from 1997 to 2005, where he was responsible for all aspects of the business' project, application and process technology development. Mr. Gruber is a member of the Bioenergy Technical Advisory Committee for the Energy Future Coalition. From 2007 to May 2012, Mr. Gruber served on the board of directors of Segetis, Inc. From 2007 to January 2012, Mr. Gruber served on the board of directors of Green Harvest Technologies, LLC and from 2007 to 2008, he served on the board of directors of Outlast Technologies. In 2011, Mr. Gruber was awarded the University of Minnesota Outstanding Achievement Award. In 2008, Mr. Gruber was awarded the first ever George Washington Carver Award, recognizing significant contributions by individuals in the field of industrial biotechnology and its application in biological engineering, environmental science, biorefining and biobased products.

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT $71.76), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, has decreased $2.30 (or 3.1%) in the past week to close at $71.76. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 1,524.6 points (or 5.5%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -8.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $590.2 million to $18.4 billion.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading per week of 10.6 million shares.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

May 07 2026: Jefferies Adjusts Alliant Energy Price Target to $85, Signaling Confidence

Jefferies has raised its price target for Alliant Energy Corporation to $85 from $84, reflecting a sustained confidence in the company's growth trajectory. This adjustment, made on 8th May 2026, comes as the firm evaluates the utility's performance amid favorable market conditions and robust operational fundamentals.

The updated price target is backed by a composite rating system that considers several key metrics, including valuation, earnings per share (EPS) revisions over four months, and overall visibility. Alliant Energy's ratings indicate a solid standing in its sector, which is essential for attracting investor interest in a competitive market.

Investors may find reassurance in Alliant Energy's strong composite ratings, which aggregate various aspects of the company's financial health and market performance. Jefferies' analysis suggests that Alliant is well-positioned for future growth, benefiting from sound fundamentals and profitability metrics that surpass many of its peers.

Furthermore, Alliant Energy's commitment to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices plays a crucial role in its appeal. The company's MSCI ESG score reflects its dedication to sustainability, allowing investors to align their portfolios with socially responsible investments. This focus on ESG factors is increasingly relevant in today's investment landscape, where environmental considerations are becoming a priority for many investors.

As Alliant Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy sector, the revised $85 price target from Jefferies serves as a reminder of the company's resilience and potential for long-term success. Investors will be keen to monitor Alliant's performance in the coming quarters, particularly in light of its robust ratings and the broader market dynamics.

SECTION 9 CORPORATE PROFILE

9.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

value.Title: This Week In Climate Action - May 8, 2026

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-35W3-SHP5-H47V-00000-00This Week In Climate Action - May 8, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:42ZLexisNexisThis Week In Climate Action - May 8, 2026Targeted News ServiceWASHINGTON

[Category: Environment] -- The League of Conservation Voters issued the following news wrap up, This Week in Climate Action - May 8, 2026:

* * *

Your weekly resource to learn what the environmental movement is saying about the news of the day and the political fight of our generation. This week, we're covering rising energy costs, clean energy wins in our states, and the Trump administration's latest attacks on our climate and public lands.

Be sure to follow LCV on Facebook, Instagram, BlueSky, and TikTok.

QUOTES OF THE WEEK:

"We are demanding a real plan. Not silence, not delay, not vague promises or half-measures. We will not be satisfied with small or symbolic action. We are asking for solutions bold enough to actually solve the problem because our rights cannot wait any longer."

-- LCV Senior Director of Judiciary and Democracy Doug Lindner calling on Congress to take bold action to make our democracy and courts work for the people following the Supreme Court's ruling in Louisiana v. Callais, in which the MAGA Justices demolished what was left of the Voting Rights Act, the most important civil rights law in the nation's history.

"The American people deserve a Department of Justice that fights for us, and it's a tremendous shame that Trump's DOJ would rather sell us out to Big Oil."

-- Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison on Trump's DOJ filing a lawsuit to block Minnesota's effort to hold fossil fuel companies accountable for contributing to climate change.

"Renewable energy provides strong benefits across our communities, but our systems and programs need to be updated regularly, making sure we're delivering benefits to all, not just some. This legislation takes a detailed view of our solar outlook and makes significant adjustments preserving current programs and extending their availability and opportunities further across the state."

-- Connecticut State Senator Norm Needleman (CT-33) on state lawmakers passing House Bill 5340 that will extend and expand state solar programs to increase access to affordable, renewable energy, especially for low-income households.

TOP NEWS IN ENERGY AFFORDABILITY

STATE NEWS

MICHIGAN UTILITIES SPENT HEAVILY IN POLITICAL CAMPAIGNS: An analysis from Michigan LCV found that Consumers Energy and DTE Energy contributed more than $156,000 to Michigan lawmakers' campaigns in the first four months of the year. These political donations have enabled utility companies to sidestep criticism and accountability over rising energy costs. Michigan LCV is advocating to pass a Ratepayer Bill of Rights as well as to ban utility companies from contributing to political campaigns to ensure they are held accountable for their role in driving up people's costs.

MLCV TAKE: Michigan LCV Energy Accountability Manager Alex Kellogg said, "The latest campaign finance figures show yet again that it's long past time to get utility money out of politics. While DTE and Consumers make billions of dollars in profits, we pay higher and higher energy bills. Our bills are too damn high, and it's time state leaders step up to rein in corporate utility greed. Working Michigan families need a Ratepayer Bill of Rights to protect consumers from rising prices, poor service, unfair outage compensation practices, and excessive utility CEO compensation and perks."

CONNECTICUT PASSES SOLAR ENERGY BILL: On the last day of the legislative session, Connecticut lawmakers approved a bill authorizing balcony solar, extending state solar programs and incentives, improving residential solar permitting, increasing solar access for low-income households, and more.

CTLCV TAKE: Connecticut LCV Deputy Director Connor Yakaitis said, "We applaud final action on HB 5340, a measure that demonstrates legislative commitment to stabilizing costs and expanding renewable and reliable energy for our state's ratepayers. We are proud to support their efforts in promoting locally generated power that will help pave the way for a cleaner and more affordable energy future for our residents and businesses across Connecticut. Thank you to State and House Leadership, as well as Energy & Technology Committee Co-Chairs Rep. Steinberg and Sen. Needleman for shining sunlight on this critical legislation."

CALIFORNIA REGULATORS DENY UTILITY PERMISSION TO CHARGE CUSTOMERS FOR PIPELINE: The California Public Utilities Commission has denied Southern California Gas permission to charge utility customers $266 million for a proposed hydrogen pipeline project. Regulators deemed it unreasonable to pass the high costs to ratepayers before the project demonstrates clear benefits.

NATIONAL NEWS

GAS REACHES HIGHEST PRICE SINCE 2022: New data shows the average price of gas nationwide reached over $4.50 a gallon this week, the highest average price since July 2022, as Trump's war with Iran and uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz continue to disrupt global oil markets. In California, there are growing concerns that gas could rise another $2/gallon on top of prices already over $6/gallon in some areas, impacting daily commutes and upcoming summer travel plans. Diesel prices have also risen to near record levels. The average cost of diesel has reached $5.66 a gallon, disrupting industries nationwide, raising costs on everyday goods like food and clothing, and contributing to high energy bills.

CLIMATE AND ENVIRONMENT ATTACKS FROM THE TRUMP ADMINISTRATION

DOJ SUES MINNESOTA TO BLOCK CLIMATE LAWSUIT: Trump's Department of Justice has filed a federal lawsuit seeking to block Minnesota's efforts to hold fossil fuel companies accountable for damages related to climate change. Minnesota's case is one of many nationwide aiming to hold the fossil fuel industry liable for contributing to climate change.

PENTAGON BLOCKS RENEWABLE ENERGY PROGRESS: Trump's Department of Defense is delaying the approval of 160 wind energy projects nationwide, according to the American Clean Power Association. This is part of Trump's ongoing attack on affordable clean energy that could bring down costs, and planet-warming and health-harming pollution. The Trump administration is currently blocking projects that are worth $54 billion in clean energy investments and have the potential to power 8.5 million homes combined.

SOIL FROM WHITE HOUSE DEMOLITION CONTAMINATED WITH TOXINS: Preliminary tests show soil from the White House East Wing demolition dumped at the East Potomac Golf Links, public land managed by the National Park Service, contains elevated levels of hazardous chemicals including lead and arsenic. Such high levels of toxins pose a potential threat to public health and environmental contamination.

INTERIOR DEPARTMENT GIVES AWAY FEDERAL PUBLIC LANDS TO ALASKA: Trump's Interior Department has given away 1.4 million acres of federal public lands to Alaska, leaving the lands vulnerable to being sold off to fossil fuel and mining corporations. The transfer advances the Trump administration's efforts to dismantle federal protections of public lands and sell them off to fossil fuel CEOs and other billionaires. The Trump administration has also pushed forward the sale of oil and gas leases in the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

ICYMI: STATE NEWS

VIRGINIA SUPREME COURT BLOCKS REDISTRICTING MEASURE: The Virginia Supreme Court blocked a recently approved redistricting ballot measure that would have redrawn the Commonwealth's Congressional districts map ahead of the upcoming midterm elections. Virginia's successful ballot measure aimed to counter President Trump's efforts to force mid-decade redistricting in multiple states to create more safe Republican seats. The ruling is a major blow to the fight to preserve free and fair elections and representative democracy.

OEC ENDORSED CANDIDATES WIN PRIMARY ELECTIONS: The Ohio Environmental Council Action Fund has announced 33 of their endorsed candidates have won their primary elections and will be on the ballot for the November elections. The candidates have promised to put the fight for clean energy, energy affordability, and protecting public lands at the forefront of their agendas.

OEC TAKE: Ohio Environmental Council Action Fund Vice President of Government Affairs Spencer Dirrig said, "Ohioans are tired of paying higher energy bills while polluters get a pass. Ohioans are tired of our clean water being used as a bargaining chip. Ohioans made their voices heard on Tuesday, advancing 33 environmental champions through their primaries. Our endorsed candidates are running on common sense solutions to the issues facing Ohio families. These candidates will fight to lower energy bills, protect clean drinking water, and oppose degradation of our public lands. Ohio is at a crossroads, and we are proud of these victories and excited to finish the job in November."

ENVIROVOTERS RALLY TO PROTECT CALIFORNIA FORESTS: California Environmental Voters hosted a rally on Thursday to protect millions of acres of California forests as the Trump administration moves to dismantle the Roadless Area Conservation Rule which bans construction and logging in 60 million acres of undeveloped national forests.

ENVIROVOTERS TAKE: California Environmental Voters said in a post advertising the event, "4.4 million acres of irreplaceable old growth forests in California are all protected by the Roadless Rule. For more than 20 years, the Roadless Rule has protected forests across the country with a simple decree: If there weren't already roads through the forest, you can't build new ones for land development. Now, the Trump administration is trying to open these forests up for mining and logging as a giveaway to Trump's corporate polluter friends."

LICENSE APPROVED FOR CONTROVERSIAL NEW JERSEY PIPELINE: The New Jersey Tidelands Resource Council approved a key license for the Northeast Supply Enhancement (NESE) pipeline despite efforts from advocacy organizations like New Jersey LCV and local elected officials who fought to stop the pipeline. The project has faced years of opposition over concerns it would pollute water and pose a risk to public health.

NJLCV TAKE: New Jersey LCV Interim Executive Director Allison McLeod said, "We are extremely disappointed that the Tidelands Resource Council has allowed the unnecessary and dangerous NESE pipeline to move forward. This is a step backward for New Jersey. This project will result in dirtier air and polluted water in the Raritan Bay, with no benefits to our state. We know that the best way to lower energy costs and protect our environment is through clean, renewable energy -- not by doubling down on dirty fossil fuel infrastructure that won't even serve New Jersey ratepayers. This project moves us away from our objective of a cleaner, cheaper New Jersey and ignores the clear scientific and economic evidence that gas pipelines are a bad deal for our state. In response to this decision, it is even more essential that Governor Sherrill double down on clean energy projects that will truly lower costs for New Jersey families. This fight is far from over. We will continue to challenge this permit through the courts to protect our communities."

TRIBES SUE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT TO PROTECT SACRED SITE: Nine Native American tribes in South Dakota, North Dakota and Nebraska have sued the federal government to block an exploratory graphite drilling project near a sacred site in the Black Hills. The lawsuit states the U.S. Forest Service and the U.S. Department of Agriculture violated environmental and historical preservation laws by approving the project without tribal consultation.

IOWA ATTEMPTS TO RESTRICT CLIMATE LAWSUITS: Gov. Kim Reynolds of Iowa signed legislation to shield polluters from lawsuits seeking to hold them accountable for their contributions to climate change that is driving up costs for families and businesses for insurance, food, energy, and other goods. Iowa is the fourth state to pass legislation restricting lawsuits against the fossil fuel industry.

COLORADO LAWMAKERS CONSIDER KEEPING COAL PLANT OPEN: Colorado lawmakers are pushing to keep the Ray Nixon Power Plant, which was originally scheduled to be closed by the end of 2029, open until 2032. While the legislators' proposal requires the utility company operating the plant to cut its greenhouse gas emissions 95% by 2040, keeping the coal plant open could raise energy rates for consumers and increase emissions in the short term.

ICYMI: NATIONAL NEWS

LCV AND ENVIROVOTERS JOIN REPS. KAMLAGER DOVE AND CHU TO UNVEIL MURAL ADVOCATING TO PROTECT PUBLIC LANDS: California Environmental Voters and LCV were joined by Representatives Judy Chu (CA-28), Sydney Kamlager-Dove (CA-37), and Culver City Mayor Freddy Puza to unveil a new mural dedicated to celebrating and calling for the protection of California's public lands. Dozens of community members came to the Ima Members Lounge, where the mural is displayed alongside Ballona Creek bikepath, to celebrate local art and California's iconic public lands.

OUR TAKE: LCV Conservation Program Director America Fitzpatrick said, "California is leading in protecting public lands like the San Gabriel Mountains National Monument. It is home to more national monuments than any other state. And that's all thanks to incredible leadership by public lands champions like Congresswomen Chu and Kamlager-Dove. We must fight to ensure they endure for generations to come."

ENVIROVOTERS TAKE: California Environmental Voters Federal Advocacy Coordinator Aaron McCall said, "Right now, Republicans in Congress are doing everything they can to either sell off our public lands or cut funding for programs that make them safe and available for all of us. We want this mural to inspire people to celebrate and fight for our public lands."

CONSERVATION CHAMPION TED TURNER DIES AT 87: Lifelong conservationist and founder of CNN Ted Turner has passed away at 87. Turner was a dedicated environmentalist who helped found LCV's network of over 30 state affiliates and was the recipient of LCV's Lifetime Achievement award in 2019. We are committed to living and preserving his conservation legacy.

REPRESENTATIVE INTRODUCES BILL ELEVATING YOUTH VOICES IN CLIMATE POLICY: Rep. Maxwell Frost (FL-10) has reintroduced the Youth Climate Leadership Act, aiming to create and formalize youth advisory councils within key federal agencies to shape environmental policy and engage young people in the policy making process.

OUR TAKE: LCV Vice President of Federal Policy Matthew Davis said, "We appreciate Rep. Frost's leadership introducing legislation to make sure young people have a seat at the table in federal environmental decisions that will affect their future. We will continue fighting alongside climate champions like the Congressman to reinforce our democratic processes and safeguard a livable climate."

SENATE DEMOCRATS INTRODUCE THE PUBLIC LANDS INTEGRITY ACT: A group of Senate Democrats led by Senators Michael Bennet (CO), Martin Heinrich (NM), Jeff Merkley (OR), and Ron Wyden (OR) have introduced the Public Lands Integrity Act to prevent the sale of federal lands through fast-tracked budget bills. The legislation would bar the inclusion of public lands in budget reconciliation bills and protect them from being sold off to fund budget spending.

NEW ON THE POWER SOURCE BLOG AND SOCIALS:

ON THE BLOG: LCV joined California Environmental Voters and Representatives Judy Chu (CA-28), Sydney Kamlager-Dove (CA-37), and Culver City Mayor Freddy Puza to unveil a new mural celebrating California's public lands and calling for action to protect them. And, we're getting to the bottom of why our gas prices are so high, and highlighting what you can do to help.

ON OUR SOCIALS: We're celebrating the life and legacy of Ted Turner, a lifelong champion of environmental activism and a cofounder of our network of state affiliates. His vision helped shape the fight for a healthier planet. With Trump paying billions to kill clean energy, selling off our protected public lands to Big Oil as they poison our communities, and making our gas prices soar to fuel Big Oil profits, we're reminded of how urgent this fight is. We're inspired to carry on Turner's bold commitment to protecting our planet for generations to come.

COMING UP:

MAY 10: Mother's Day.

MAY 12: Nebraska state primary election.

MAY 12: West Virginia state primary election.

MAY 25: Memorial Day.
