value.Title: THIS WEEK IN CLIMATE ACTION MAY 8. 2026

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ2-CVX3-RWDG-74YX-00000-00THIS WEEK IN CLIMATE ACTION MAY 8. 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:42ZLexisNexisTHIS WEEK IN CLIMATE ACTION MAY 8. 2026States News ServiceWASHINGTON, DC

The following information was released by the League of Conservation Voters (LCV):

Your weekly resource to learn what the environmental movement is saying about the news of the day and the political fight of our generation. This week, we're covering rising energy costs, clean energy wins in our states, and the Trump administration's latest attacks on our climate and public lands.

Be sure to follow LCV on Facebook, Instagram, BlueSky, and TikTok.

QUOTES OF THE WEEK:

"We are demanding a real plan. Not silence, not delay, not vague promises or half-measures. We will not be satisfied with small or symbolic action. We are asking for solutions bold enough to actually solve the problem because our rights cannot wait any longer."

LCV Senior Director of Judiciary and Democracy Doug Lindner calling on Congress to take bold action to make our democracy and courts work for the people following the Supreme Court's ruling in Louisiana v. Callais, in which the MAGA Justices demolished what was left of the Voting Rights Act, the most important civil rights law in the nation's history.

"The American people deserve a Department of Justice that fights for us, and it's a tremendous shame that Trump's DOJ would rather sell us out to Big Oil.

Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison on Trump's DOJ filing a lawsuit to block Minnesota's effort to hold fossil fuel companies accountable for contributing to climate change.

"Renewable energy provides strong benefits across our communities, but our systems and programs need to be updated regularly, making sure we're delivering benefits to all, not just some. This legislation takes a detailed view of our solar outlook and makes significant adjustments preserving current programs and extending their availability and opportunities further across the state."

Connecticut State Senator Norm Needleman (CT-33) on state lawmakers passing House Bill 5340 that will extend and expand state solar programs to increase access to affordable, renewable energy, especially for low-income households.

TOP NEWS IN ENERGY AFFORDABILITY

STATE NEWS

MICHIGAN UTILITIES SPENT HEAVILY IN POLITICAL CAMPAIGNS: An analysis from Michigan LCV found that Consumers Energy and DTE Energy contributed more than $156,000 to Michigan lawmakers' campaigns in the first four months of the year. These political donations have enabled utility companies to sidestep criticism and accountability over rising energy costs. Michigan LCV is advocating to pass a Ratepayer Bill of Rights as well as to ban utility companies from contributing to political campaigns to ensure they are held accountable for their role in driving up people's costs.

MLCV TAKE: Michigan LCV Energy Accountability Manager Alex Kellogg said, "The latest campaign finance figures show yet again that it's long past time to get utility money out of politics. While DTE and Consumers make billions of dollars in profits, we pay higher and higher energy bills. Our bills are too damn high, and it's time state leaders step up to rein in corporate utility greed. Working Michigan families need a Ratepayer Bill of Rights to protect consumers from rising prices, poor service, unfair outage compensation practices, and excessive utility CEO compensation and perks."

CONNECTICUT PASSES SOLAR ENERGY BILL: On the last day of the legislative session, Connecticut lawmakers approved a bill authorizing balcony solar, extending state solar programs and incentives, improving residential solar permitting, increasing solar access for low-income households, and more.

CTLCV TAKE: Connecticut LCV Deputy Director Connor Yakaitis said, "We applaud final action on HB 5340, a measure that demonstrates legislative commitment to stabilizing costs and expanding renewable and reliable energy for our state's ratepayers. We are proud to support their efforts in promoting locally generated power that will help pave the way for a cleaner and more affordable energy future for our residents and businesses across Connecticut. Thank you to State and House Leadership, as well as Energy and Technology Committee Co-Chairs Rep. Steinberg and Sen. Needleman for shining sunlight on this critical legislation.

CALIFORNIA REGULATORS DENY UTILITY PERMISSION TO CHARGE CUSTOMERS FOR PIPELINE: The California Public Utilities Commission has denied Southern California Gas permission to charge utility customers $266 million for a proposed hydrogen pipeline project. Regulators deemed it unreasonable to pass the high costs to ratepayers before the project demonstrates clear benefits.

NATIONAL NEWS

GAS REACHES HIGHEST PRICE SINCE 2022: New data shows the average price of gas nationwide reached over $4.50 a gallon this week, the highest average price since July 2022, as Trump's war with Iran and uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz continue to disrupt global oil markets. In California, there are growing concerns that gas could rise another $2/gallon on top of prices already over $6/gallon in some areas, impacting daily commutes and upcoming summer travel plans. Diesel prices have also risen to near record levels. The average cost of diesel has reached $5.66 a gallon, disrupting industries nationwide, raising costs on everyday goods like food and clothing, and contributing to high energy bills.

CLIMATE AND ENVIRONMENT ATTACKS FROM THE TRUMP ADMINISTRATION

DOJ SUES MINNESOTA TO BLOCK CLIMATE LAWSUIT: Trump's Department of Justice has filed a federal lawsuit seeking to block Minnesota's efforts to hold fossil fuel companies accountable for damages related to climate change. Minnesota's case is one of many nationwide aiming to hold the fossil fuel industry liable for contributing to climate change.

PENTAGON BLOCKS RENEWABLE ENERGY PROGRESS: Trump's Department of Defense is delaying the approval of 160 wind energy projects nationwide, according to the American Clean Power Association. This is part of Trump's ongoing attack on affordable clean energy that could bring down costs, and planet-warming and health-harming pollution. The Trump administration is currently blocking projects that are worth $54 billion in clean energy investments and have the potential to power 8.5 million homes combined.

SOIL FROM WHITE HOUSE DEMOLITION CONTAMINATED WITH TOXINS: Preliminary tests show soil from the White House East Wing demolition dumped at the East Potomac Golf Links, public land managed by the National Park Service, contains elevated levels of hazardous chemicals including lead and arsenic. Such high levels of toxins pose a potential threat to public health and environmental contamination.

INTERIOR DEPARTMENT GIVES AWAY FEDERAL PUBLIC LANDS TO ALASKA: Trump's Interior Department has given away 1.4 million acres of federal public lands to Alaska, leaving the lands vulnerable to being sold off to fossil fuel and mining corporations. The transfer advances the Trump administration's efforts to dismantle federal protections of public lands and sell them off to fossil fuel CEOs and other billionaires. The Trump administration has also pushed forward the sale of oil and gas leases in the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

ICYMI: STATE NEWS

VIRGINIA SUPREME COURT BLOCKS REDISTRICTING MEASURE: The Virginia Supreme Court blocked a recently approved redistricting ballot measure that would have redrawn the Commonwealth's Congressional districts map ahead of the upcoming midterm elections. Virginia's successful ballot measure aimed to counter President Trump's efforts to force mid-decade redistricting in multiple states to create more safe Republican seats. The ruling is a major blow to the fight to preserve free and fair elections and representative democracy.

OEC ENDORSED CANDIDATES WIN PRIMARY ELECTIONS: The Ohio Environmental Council Action Fund has announced 33 of their endorsed candidates have won their primary elections and will be on the ballot for the November elections. The candidates have promised to put the fight for clean energy, energy affordability, and protecting public lands at the forefront of their agendas.

OEC TAKE: Ohio Environmental Council Action Fund Vice President of Government Affairs Spencer Dirrig said, "Ohioans are tired of paying higher energy bills while polluters get a pass. Ohioans are tired of our clean water being used as a bargaining chip. Ohioans made their voices heard on Tuesday, advancing 33 environmental champions through their primaries. Our endorsed candidates are running on common sense solutions to the issues facing Ohio families. These candidates will fight to lower energy bills, protect clean drinking water, and oppose degradation of our public lands. Ohio is at a crossroads, and we are proud of these victories and excited to finish the job in November."

ENVIROVOTERS RALLY TO PROTECT CALIFORNIA FORESTS: California Environmental Voters hosted a rally on Thursday to protect millions of acres of California forests as the Trump administration moves to dismantle the Roadless Area Conservation Rule which bans construction and logging in 60 million acres of undeveloped national forests.

ENVIROVOTERS TAKE: California Environmental Voters said in a post advertising the event, "4.4 million acres of irreplaceable old growth forests in California are all protected by the Roadless Rule. For more than 20 years, the Roadless Rule has protected forests across the country with a simple decree: If there weren't already roads through the forest, you can't build new ones for land development. Now, the Trump administration is trying to open these forests up for mining and logging as a giveaway to Trump's corporate polluter friends."

LICENSE APPROVED FOR CONTROVERSIAL NEW JERSEY PIPELINE: The New Jersey Tidelands Resource Council approved a key license for the Northeast Supply Enhancement (NESE) pipeline despite efforts from advocacy organizations like New Jersey LCV and local elected officials who fought to stop the pipeline. The project has faced years of opposition over concerns it would pollute water and pose a risk to public health.

NJLCV TAKE: New Jersey LCV Interim Executive Director Allison McLeod said, "We are extremely disappointed that the Tidelands Resource Council has allowed the unnecessary and dangerous NESE pipeline to move forward. This is a step backward for New Jersey. This project will result in dirtier air and polluted water in the Raritan Bay, with no benefits to our state. We know that the best way to lower energy costs and protect our environment is through clean, renewable energy not by doubling down on dirty fossil fuel infrastructure that won't even serve New Jersey ratepayers. This project moves us away from our objective of a cleaner, cheaper New Jersey and ignores the clear scientific and economic evidence that gas pipelines are a bad deal for our state. In response to this decision, it is even more essential that Governor Sherrill double down on clean energy projects that will truly lower costs for New Jersey families. This fight is far from over. We will continue to challenge this permit through the courts to protect our communities."

TRIBES SUE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT TO PROTECT SACRED SITE: Nine Native American tribes in South Dakota, North Dakota and Nebraska have sued the federal government to block an exploratory graphite drilling project near a sacred site in the Black Hills. The lawsuit states the U.S. Forest Service and the U.S. Department of Agriculture violated environmental and historical preservation laws by approving the project without tribal consultation.

IOWA ATTEMPTS TO RESTRICT CLIMATE LAWSUITS: Gov. Kim Reynolds of Iowa signed legislation to shield polluters from lawsuits seeking to hold them accountable for their contributions to climate change that is driving up costs for families and businesses for insurance, food, energy, and other goods. Iowa is the fourth state to pass legislation restricting lawsuits against the fossil fuel industry.

COLORADO LAWMAKERS CONSIDER KEEPING COAL PLANT OPEN: Colorado lawmakers are pushing to keep the Ray Nixon Power Plant, which was originally scheduled to be closed by the end of 2029, open until 2032. While the legislators' proposal requires the utility company operating the plant to cut its greenhouse gas emissions 95% by 2040, keeping the coal plant open could raise energy rates for consumers and increase emissions in the short term.

ICYMI: NATIONAL NEWS

LCV AND ENVIROVOTERS JOIN REPS. KAMLAGER DOVE AND CHU TO UNVEIL MURAL ADVOCATING TO PROTECT PUBLIC LANDS: California Environmental Voters and LCV were joined by Representatives Judy Chu (CA-28), Sydney Kamlager-Dove (CA-37), and Culver City Mayor Freddy Puza to unveil a new mural dedicated to celebrating and calling for the protection of California's public lands. Dozens of community members came to the Ima Members Lounge, where the mural is displayed alongside Ballona Creek bikepath, to celebrate local art and California's iconic public lands.

OUR TAKE: LCV Conservation Program Director America Fitzpatrick said, "California is leading in protecting public lands like the San Gabriel Mountains National Monument. It is home to more national monuments than any other state. And that's all thanks to incredible leadership by public lands champions like Congresswomen Chu and Kamlager-Dove. We must fight to ensure they endure for generations to come."

ENVIROVOTERS TAKE: California Environmental Voters Federal Advocacy Coordinator Aaron McCall said, "Right now, Republicans in Congress are doing everything they can to either sell off our public lands or cut funding for programs that make them safe and available for all of us. We want this mural to inspire people to celebrate and fight for our public lands."

CONSERVATION CHAMPION TED TURNER DIES AT 87: Lifelong conservationist and founder of CNN Ted Turner has passed away at 87. Turner was a dedicated environmentalist who helped found LCV's network of over 30 state affiliates and was the recipient of LCV's Lifetime Achievement award in 2019. We are committed to living and preserving his conservation legacy.

REPRESENTATIVE INTRODUCES BILL ELEVATING YOUTH VOICES IN CLIMATE POLICY: Rep. Maxwell Frost (FL-10) has reintroduced the Youth Climate Leadership Act, aiming to create and formalize youth advisory councils within key federal agencies to shape environmental policy and engage young people in the policy making process.

OUR TAKE: LCV Vice President of Federal Policy Matthew Davis said, "We appreciate Rep. Frost's leadership introducing legislation to make sure young people have a seat at the table in federal environmental decisions that will affect their future. We will continue fighting alongside climate champions like the Congressman to reinforce our democratic processes and safeguard a livable climate."

SENATE DEMOCRATS INTRODUCE THE PUBLIC LANDS INTEGRITY ACT: A group of Senate Democrats led by Senators Michael Bennet (CO), Martin Heinrich (NM), Jeff Merkley (OR), and Ron Wyden (OR) have introduced the Public Lands Integrity Act to prevent the sale of federal lands through fast-tracked budget bills. The legislation would bar the inclusion of public lands in budget reconciliation bills and protect them from being sold off to fund budget spending.

NEW ON THE POWER SOURCE BLOG AND SOCIALS:

ON THE BLOG: LCV joined California Environmental Voters and Representatives Judy Chu (CA-28), Sydney Kamlager-Dove (CA-37), and Culver City Mayor Freddy Puza to unveil a new mural celebrating California's public lands and calling for action to protect them. And, we're getting to the bottom of why our gas prices are so high, and highlighting what you can do to help.

ON OUR SOCIALS: We're celebrating the life and legacy of Ted Turner, a lifelong champion of environmental activism and a cofounder of our network of state affiliates. His vision helped shape the fight for a healthier planet. With Trump paying billions to kill clean energy, selling off our protected public lands to Big Oil as they poison our communities, and making our gas prices soar to fuel Big Oil profits, we're reminded of how urgent this fight is. We're inspired to carry on Turner's bold commitment to protecting our planet for generations to come.

COMING UP:

MAY 10: Mother's Day.

MAY 12: Nebraska state primary election.

MAY 12: West Virginia state primary election.

MAY 25: Memorial Day.

FERGUS FALLS, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Otter Tail Corporation's (NASDAQ: OTTR $89.70) stock price extended its gains on Tuesday, rising an additional $1.41 (1.6%) to close at $89.70.

This brought its 3-day rise to 2.1%; compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 500.9 points (1.8%) in the three days for a relative price increase of 0.4%.

Cumulative volume in the 3 days was 1.2 times the average 3-day volume of 224,590 shares. There were 271,600 shares worth $24.4 million traded today.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Otter Tail Corporation, headquartered in Fergus Falls, Minnesota, is a diversified holding company primarily engaged in electric utility operations, manufacturing, and plastics production. Through its subsidiary Otter Tail Power Company, it generates and distributes electricity to customers across the Midwest, emphasizing reliable service and renewable energy integration. The company also operates in manufacturing, producing custom metal fabrications and infrastructure components via BTD Manufacturing, and in plastics, creating piping and related products that support construction and water management needs. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been manufacturing and plastics, driven by increasing demand for infrastructure projects and sustainable materials, contributing to overall revenue expansion. Otter Tail's strategic focus on diversification and operational efficiency positions it as a resilient player in dynamic markets, with a commitment to innovation and community investment. It is the NASDAQ's 10th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

August 04 2025: Otter Tail Corporation Announces Second Quarter Earnings and Increases Annual Earnings Guidance

FERGUS FALLS, Minn.-Otter Tail Corporation (Nasdaq: OTTR) today announced financial results for the quarter ended June 30, 2025.

SUMMARY

Produced diluted earnings per share of $1.85 in the second quarter of 2025. Increased midpoint of 2025 earnings per share guidance by $0.38 to $6.26 per share. Return on equity of 17% over the trailing twelve months. CEO OVERVIEW

"We are pleased with our second quarter financial results," said President and CEO Chuck MacFarlane. "Across our businesses, our team members remain committed to our mission - delivering value through building strong electric and manufacturing platforms - amidst dynamic market conditions.

"During the second quarter, severe weather moved through Otter Tail Power's service territory, resulting in significant property and infrastructure damage, as well as tree loss. Approximately 30 percent of our customers experienced an interruption in electric service due to the storms. Our employees worked tirelessly to restore power to our customers as quickly and safely as possible.

"Beyond our storm response, Otter Tail Power continues to perform well, executing on our significant capital investment plan and regulatory priorities. We secured approval to directly assign and recover the capital investment for our two solar development projects from the Minnesota and South Dakota Commissions. We look forward to adding 345 MW of cost-effective solar generation to our portfolio to better serve our customers. Additionally, for the first time since 2018, we filed a request with the South Dakota Public Utilities Commission for permission to increase our electric rates by approximately $5.7 million.

"Our Manufacturing segment continues to navigate soft end market demand but remains well positioned to respond when market conditions improve.

value.Title: -SunOpta Marks Another Year of Progress with 2025 Sustainability Report

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-FXG3-RYWP-53YH-00000-00-SunOpta Marks Another Year of Progress with 2025 Sustainability Report2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexis-SunOpta Marks Another Year of Progress with 2025 Sustainability Report

May 1, 2026

Release date- 30042026 - MINNEAPOLIS - SunOpta (Nasdaq:STKL) (TSX:SOY) - the company that delivers customized supply chain solutions and innovation for top brands, retailers and foodservice providers across a broad portfolio of beverages, broths and better-for-you snacks - is proud to release its 2025 Sustainability Report.



This annual update outlines sustainability progress made in the 2025 fiscal year across four key areas: planet, product, people and governance.

'Building a more sustainable future has grounded SunOpta for over 50 years and counting as we fuel our customers' growth. Today, we continually seek better and more sustainable solutions to manufacture products for people and the planet,' said Brian Kocher, CEO of SunOpta.

SunOpta continues its journey toward sustainability goals, while communicating transparently about its progress and challenges. Highlights of the 2025 sustainability report include:

Planet

Consistent focus on load mode optimization resulting in 733 metric tons of carbon emissions savings, a 49% increase in savings year over year

Saved 48.4 billion gallons of water by producing plant-based milk compared to dairy milk

Sourced 99% recyclable packaging (by weight)

Maintained zero-waste-to-landfill status at five manufacturing plants and achieved zero waste at our headquarters, for a total of six zero-waste sites

Donated more than 375,000 pounds of food (equivalent to nearly 314,000 meals)

Produced 275,503 kilowatt hours (kWh) of renewable energy through solar panels at its headquarters location, reducing 115.77 tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (tCO2e) emissions and generate over 25% of the facility's annual energy needs

Product

Advanced work on food loss and food waste reduction by updating policy and finalizing Standard Operating Procedures with training in plants planned for 2026

Recognized with a 2025 Progressive Grocer Impact Award in Ethical Sourcing and Supply Chain Transparency

Achieved 100% sourcing from GFSI-certified suppliers

Earned $ 30.8 million in revenue from non-GMO-labeled products

Offered 50 own branded certified organic products and 30 own branded non-GMO Project products

People

Connected with 83% of top suppliers on an ethical supply chain platform

Accepted the Governor's Gold Achievement Award for Excellence in Occupational Injury Prevention (on behalf of Alexandria, Minn. employees) at the 2025 Minnesota Safety and Health Conference

Recorded 1,052 volunteer hours

Provided $ 20,000 in annual scholarships

Governance

Maintained a robust cybersecurity program built on advanced technologies and modern protection strategies

Evaluated roadmaps, emerging technologies, threat trends, KPIs, and metrics to maintain a strong security posture without hindering productivity

'We remained focused and consistent in advancing our sustainability efforts this year,' said Stacy Seidel, Head of Sustainability at SunOpta. 'In 2025, informed by our most recent Materiality Assessment, we refined our strategy and goals, continuing to make progress today while maintaining a clear approach for the future.'

About SunOpta, Inc.

SunOpta (Nasdaq: STKL) (TSX: SOY) delivers customized supply chain solutions and innovation for top brands, retailers and foodservice providers across a broad portfolio of beverages, broths and better-for-you snacks. With over 50 years of expertise, SunOpta fuels customers' growth with high-quality, sustainability-forward solutions distributed through retail, club, food service and e-commerce channels across North America. For more information, visit www.sunopta.com or follow us on LinkedIn.

SunOpta has adopted a zero-waste definition as 90% diversion of waste from landfill.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-4CF3-RY7C-055V-00000-00Antofagasta PLC (L:ANTO) Seizes Path to Copper Project After Senate Lifts Boundary Waters Mining Ban2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSAntofagasta PLC (L:ANTO) Seizes Path to Copper Project After Senate Lifts Boundary Waters Mining Ban

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

05 May 2026 15:30 BST

On 1st May 2026, the U.S. Senate voted to repeal a federal order that barred mining across more than 200,000 acres of the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness, clearing a path for Twin Metals Minnesota LLC, an Antofagasta PLC (L:ANTO) unit, to advance its copper-nickel project. The 50-49 vote followed years of regulatory and environmental debate. Antofagasta PLC, controlled by the Luksic family whose wealth is estimated at about $56 billion, would benefit if Twin Metals wins permits and proceeds with development. Analysts say permitting could accelerate under the current administration, potentially improving the project s timing and economics. Environmental groups criticize the move, warning of potential ecological impacts in the Boundary Waters region. The decision underscores the broader tug of war between domestic resource development and conservation, a dynamic that will influence the project s prospects and Antofagasta's copper strategy in the years ahead

SECTION 2 ANTOFAGASTA ACTIVITIES

Activities

Antofagasta PLC is a leading mining company headquartered in the United Kingdom, with primary operations centered in Chile. Its core business activities revolve around the exploration, development, and production of copper, alongside other minerals such as gold and molybdenum. The company operates several key mines, including Los Pelambres and Centinela, which form the backbone of its production capabilities. In recent years, the copper segment has emerged as the fastest-growing area, fueled by surging global demand driven by the transition to renewable energy, electric vehicles, and infrastructure projects. This growth is supported by strategic investments in operational efficiency and expansion, including enhancements in ore processing and new project developments. Additionally, Antofagasta maintains diversified interests in railway transportation and water supply, which bolster its mining operations and contribute to sustainable resource management, ensuring long-term competitiveness in the global metals market. It is 6th largest Mining company by market capitalisation in the United Kingdom.

06 May 2026 03:30 BST

On 1st May 2026, the U.S. Senate repealed a federal order blocking mining in the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness across more than 200,000 acres, by a 50-49 margin. The move opens a potential path for Twin Metals Minnesota LLC, part of Antofagasta PLC (L:ANTO), to pursue its copper-nickel project. Twin Metals Minnesota LLC could gain access to environmental and mining permits that have been elusive, accelerating activity as the project moves forward. Antofagasta PLC, controlled by the Luksic family whose wealth is estimated at about $56 billion, invests in this pathway to expand its copper footprint. Environmental groups criticize the decision, warning about ecological impacts in the Boundary Waters region. The broader debate between domestic resource development and conservation will shape the project’s prospects and the company’s copper strategy.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-5083-RRTS-00SG-00000-00Antofagasta PLC (L:ANTO) Twin Metals Timeline Gains Momentum as Boundary Waters Ban Is Repealed2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSAntofagasta PLC (L:ANTO) Twin Metals Timeline Gains Momentum as Boundary Waters Ban Is Repealed

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

06 May 2026 09:30 BST

On 1st May 2026, the U.S. Senate repealed a federal order that blocked mining across more than 200,000 acres of the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness, with a 50-49 margin. Twin Metals Minnesota LLC, an Antofagasta PLC (L:ANTO) unit, could now pursue permits that unlock the copper-nickel project. The Luksic family, whose wealth is about $56 billion, stands to benefit as permitting and development move forward under this political shift. Analysts expect permitting to speed up under the current administration, potentially strengthening Antofagasta's copper market position and revenue outlook in the coming years

SECTION 2 ANTOFAGASTA ACTIVITIES

Activities

Antofagasta PLC is a leading mining company headquartered in the United Kingdom, with primary operations centered in Chile. Its core business activities revolve around the exploration, development, and production of copper, alongside other minerals such as gold and molybdenum. The company operates several key mines, including Los Pelambres and Centinela, which form the backbone of its production capabilities. In recent years, the copper segment has emerged as the fastest-growing area, fueled by surging global demand driven by the transition to renewable energy, electric vehicles, and infrastructure projects. This growth is supported by strategic investments in operational efficiency and expansion, including enhancements in ore processing and new project developments. Additionally, Antofagasta maintains diversified interests in railway transportation and water supply, which bolster its mining operations and contribute to sustainable resource management, ensuring long-term competitiveness in the global metals market. It is 6th largest Mining company by market capitalisation in the United Kingdom.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Otter Tail Corporation, headquartered in Fergus Falls, Minnesota, is a diversified holding company primarily engaged in electric utility operations, manufacturing, and plastics production. Through its subsidiary Otter Tail Power Company, it generates and distributes electricity to customers across the Midwest, emphasizing reliable service and renewable energy integration. The company also operates in manufacturing, producing custom metal fabrications and infrastructure components via BTD Manufacturing, and in plastics, creating piping and related products that support construction and water management needs. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been manufacturing and plastics, driven by increasing demand for infrastructure projects and sustainable materials, contributing to overall revenue expansion. Otter Tail's strategic focus on diversification and operational efficiency positions it as a resilient player in dynamic markets, with a commitment to innovation and community investment. It is the NASDAQ's 9th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

August 04 2025: Otter Tail Corporation Announces Second Quarter Earnings and Increases Annual Earnings Guidance

FERGUS FALLS, Minn.-Otter Tail Corporation (Nasdaq: OTTR) today announced financial results for the quarter ended June 30, 2025.

SUMMARY

Produced diluted earnings per share of $1.85 in the second quarter of 2025. Increased midpoint of 2025 earnings per share guidance by $0.38 to $6.26 per share. Return on equity of 17% over the trailing twelve months. CEO OVERVIEW

"We are pleased with our second quarter financial results," said President and CEO Chuck MacFarlane. "Across our businesses, our team members remain committed to our mission - delivering value through building strong electric and manufacturing platforms - amidst dynamic market conditions.

"During the second quarter, severe weather moved through Otter Tail Power's service territory, resulting in significant property and infrastructure damage, as well as tree loss. Approximately 30 percent of our customers experienced an interruption in electric service due to the storms. Our employees worked tirelessly to restore power to our customers as quickly and safely as possible.

"Beyond our storm response, Otter Tail Power continues to perform well, executing on our significant capital investment plan and regulatory priorities. We secured approval to directly assign and recover the capital investment for our two solar development projects from the Minnesota and South Dakota Commissions. We look forward to adding 345 MW of cost-effective solar generation to our portfolio to better serve our customers. Additionally, for the first time since 2018, we filed a request with the South Dakota Public Utilities Commission for permission to increase our electric rates by approximately $5.7 million.

"Our Manufacturing segment continues to navigate soft end market demand but remains well positioned to respond when market conditions improve.

4.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Total Liabilities/EBITDA of 2.6 is less than 5, this compares favourably with the Joseph Piotroski benchmark of 5.

- Return on Assets of 7% versus sector average of 5.0%.

- Return on Capital Employed of 8.9% versus sector average of 4.5%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.9. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 21.9% in the last 3 years. This is considered superior and suggests a high margin of safety.

urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W29X-00000-00Alliant Energy Corporation's (NASDAQ:LNT), outlook for Thursday - A potential comeback?2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisAlliant Energy Corporation's (NASDAQ:LNT), outlook for Thursday - A potential comeback?

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

MADISON, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Alliant Energy Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, decreased 0.4% during its previous trading session to close at $72.30.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

MADISON, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Alliant Energy Corporation has shown some strength in recent days. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 0.2% during its previous trading session to close at $72.59.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 315 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

MADISON, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Alliant Energy Corporation has shown some strength in recent days. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 1.0% during its previous trading session to close at $72.47.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

MADISON, Friday, May 08 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Alliant Energy Corporation closed higher the previous trading day. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 0.1% during its previous trading session to close at $71.76.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 315 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 321 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Sets Sights on Data Center Demand Amid Utility Stability

Alliant Energy Corp. is strategically positioning itself to address the surging electricity demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure across the Midwest. As of 15th April 2026, the utility provider serves over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its primary subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company in Iowa and Wisconsin Power and Light Company in Wisconsin.

With a regulated utility model, Alliant Energy earns returns that are approved by state commissions, providing a buffer against volatile economic cycles. The company currently manages its energy generation through a balanced portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. Ongoing capital plans are focused on grid modernization and expanding renewable energy resources to comply with clean energy mandates and capitalize on federal decarbonization incentives.

The demand for steady power is projected to rise significantly, driven by an anticipated 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. According to industry experts, utilities like Alliant Energy anchor their portfolios by concentrating on essential infrastructure, particularly during periods of market uncertainty. The emergence of hyperscaler-driven data centers in the Upper Midwest necessitates reliable baseload power, a demand that aligns with Alliant Energy's regional capabilities.

This structural shift in energy demand supports Alliant Energy's disciplined capital approach, which aims for an annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. Analysts generally hold a favorable view of the company due to its rate base growth and a consistent track record of dividends. Despite concerns regarding interest rate sensitivity, Alliant Energy's low-leverage strategy is designed to outpace inflation while ensuring service reliability, further solidifying its position in the market.

As Alliant Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, its focus on data center growth not only enhances its utility stability but also positions the company for sustainable long-term success. With a commitment to meeting the energy needs of the future, Alliant Energy is poised to play a critical role in the region's economic development.

Today's Volatility:

The 5% discount to 12-month high of $75.76 against the 26% premium to 12-month low of $57.18 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 1.1% was 0.7 times the average daily volatility of 1.5%, up from 1.1% on Thursday. Price/Moving Average Price of 1.07 and positive MACD:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Alliant Energy Corporation is 1.07. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $71.51 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $66.88, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $66.88, a third bullish indicator.

- The Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicator of 12-day Exponential Moving Average (EMA) of 72.09 minus the 26-day EMA of 72.09 is positive, suggesting a bullish signal.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high ten times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

- The shares had their best quarter in three quarters since the quarter ended August 08, 2025.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.7. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 18.3% in the last 3 years. This is considered superior and suggests a high margin of safety.
