urn:contentItem:6JHM-XJ53-RSYN-92T9-00000-00CARGILL INCORPORATED - KEY PEER AND SECTOR DEVELOPMENTS: RECENT UPDATES2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisSTOCKCARGILL INCORPORATED - KEY PEER AND SECTOR DEVELOPMENTS: RECENT UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CARGILL

PermID4296555324Websitehttp://www.cargill.comIndustryAgribusiness & Food ProcessingAddressPO Box 9300 Minneapolis, Minnesota 55440-9300 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

+ CARGILL [3 News That Matters]

+ CARGILL AGRI PURINA LAUNCHES TENDER FOR 140,000 MT OF CORN AMID ASIA DEMAND SURGE

HAMBURG — The date of the announcement is 4th May 2026. South Korean importer Cargill Agri Purina has issued an international tender to purchase around 140,000 metric tons of animal-feed corn, European traders said on Tuesday. The corn is sought from South America or South Africa in two consignments ranging from 55,000 to 70,000 tons each, with arrivals scheduled for August in South Korea. The price-offer deadline was set for Tuesday, 28th April.The first consignment is expected to arrive around 15th August, with shipments from South America planned between 12th June and 1st July or from South Africa between 27th June and 16th July. The second consignment is slated for around 20th August, with South American shipments between 17th June and 6th July and South Africa shipments between 2nd July and 21st July.Offers are requested on an outright c&f basis or as a premium over the Chicago July 2026 corn contract. Traders say only one of the two consignments may be purchased, within the 55,000 to 70,000-ton range. The tender reflects brisk demand for corn in Asia amid geopolitical tensions, and Taiwan's MFIG purchasing group has also issued an international tender for up to 65,000 tons from the United States, Argentina, Brazil or South Africa.

+ RSGBL COMPLETES CARGILL ASSET ACQUISITION IN DAVANGERE TO BROADEN SOUTHERN INDIAN FOOTPRINT

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd (RSGBL) has completed the acquisition of a corn milling plant and related assets in Davangere, Karnataka from Cargill. The deal, finalized on 28th April 2026 under an Asset Purchase Agreement, includes land, production facilities, warehouses and corn silos, expanding RSGBL's southern India footprint.The Davangere plant, in operation since 2016, processes about 300,000 metric tons annually. The acquisition broadens RSGBL's capacity to serve the food, pharmaceutical and industrial sectors while underscoring the company's commitment to sustainable manufacturing practices, including greater use of renewable energy sources.Executive Director Siddharth Chowdhary said, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals."

+ CARGILL INVESTS RP38.8 BILLION TO BOOST INDONESIAN COCOA FARMERS VIA RAINFOREST ALLIANCE

Cargill announced an investment of Rp38.8 billion (US$2.25 million) to support more than 7,800 cocoa farmers in Indonesia through the Rainforest Alliance certification program, disclosed on 26th of April 2026. The move underscores Cargill's commitment to sustainable sourcing and farmer empowerment in the cocoa sector.The premium payments total about Rp35 billion (US$2.02 million) to farmers in Central Sulawesi and Rp3.8 billion to those in East Nusa Tenggara. The payments supplement farmers' income from cocoa bean sales and are conditional on meeting globally recognized sustainability standards, reflecting Cargill's focus on ethical and responsible sourcing.In Central Sulawesi, more than 5,600 farmers participate, including over 600 women farmers. From May 2023 to February 2026, roughly 16,000 metric tonnes of Rainforest Alliance-certified cocoa beans were sourced from Parigi Moutong, Poso and North Morowali. In East Nusa Tenggara, about 2,200 farmers contributed close to 2,000 metric tonnes from Ende, Sikka, East Manggarai and East Flores. Brook Chang, Director of Sustainability for Food APAC at Cargill, said that investing in training, certification and premium payments linked to verified sustainability standards helps farmers raise income and productivity while strengthening traceability and supply reliability for customers and partners.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ INGREDION [3 News That Matters]

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSTS LATEST PROTEIN AND SUGAR SOLUTIONS AT VITAFOODS EUROPE 2026

Manchester, UK – Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) will showcase its latest advances in protein fortification, texture enhancement, and sugar reduction at Vitafoods Europe 2026 in Barcelona, helping brands meet EMEA consumers' rising expectations for great taste, appealing texture, and ingredient lists that support clean-label appeal. The date of this announcement is 23rd April 2026.The integrated portfolio aims to deliver simpler, more recognisable components while enhancing nutrition characteristics such as protein enrichment and reduced sugar, trends that consumers increasingly prioritise. Visitors to Ingredion's stand will explore VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD, a cutting-edge pea protein ideal for cold-pressed applications that maintains softness throughout its shelf life while enhancing texture and providing effective protein fortification. VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD also demonstrates versatility in bakery applications, enabling protein enrichment without compromising quality or taste.Beyond Vitafoods, Ingredion continues to advance its strategic partnership with Lantmannen, first announced in 2024, underscoring a commitment to European-sourced, plant-based protein solutions and stronger regional sourcing for customers in Europe. To discover more about Ingredion's ingredient solutions, visit stand number #3D252 at Vitafoods Europe in Barcelona, Spain.

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSITIONED TO BENEFIT FROM AI-DRIVEN PIVOT

Barclays Reports On AI's Impact Across the Agribusiness Sector in the Americas, noting Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) is well positioned to benefit in the near term. The bank says Ingredion is shifting from a commodity-focused model to a solutions-oriented approach, leveraging its library of ingredients and formulations to meet unmet customer needs. The pivot could enable faster monetization of AI opportunities, particularly for companies with solid balance sheets, Barclays writes in the report published on 22nd April 2026. In the medium term, Barclays identifies seeds and crop chemicals as sectors likely to see AI-driven advancements, with implications for faster product development cycles, improved pricing power, and structural efficiency. Ingredion and Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) are highlighted as frontrunners in AI adoption, while FMC (NYSE:FMC) may face execution delays given internal restructuring. Barclays also notes potential gains in protein processing from AI-enabled automation and yield improvements, which could reduce recalls and boost profitability. Overall, the bank argues AI will reinforce market leaders by driving innovation, lowering costs, and enhancing capital efficiency across the agriculture landscape. Investors will monitor how Ingredion’s strategy unfolds as AI transforms agricultural practices

+ INGREDION NAMES JASON PAYANT INTERIM CFO AS LEADERSHIP TRANSITION ADVANCES

Ingredion Incorporated has announced the appointment of Jason Payant as Interim Chief Financial Officer, effective April 1, following the resignation of James D. Gray as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, which will take effect on 31st March 2026. Payant, 55, has been with Ingredion since 2012 and most recently served as Vice President, Finance, Global Texture & Healthful Solutions since November 2025; he will continue to fulfill that role alongside his new CFO responsibilities.The company confirmed there are no arrangements between Payant and other persons regarding the appointment, and no transactions involving Payant or his immediate family have any material interest. Payant will receive a monthly cash compensation of $25,000 in addition to his current compensation and benefits. In other business, Ingredion declared a quarterly dividend of $0.82 per share payable on 21st April 2026 to stockholders of record as of 1st April 2026, and announced a research and development collaboration with Shiru aimed at using Shiru's AI discovery platform to develop functional proteins and create prebiotics from natural sources for nutritional products.Ingredion also named Univar Solutions LLC as the exclusive distributor for Ingredion Pharma Solutions in the United States and Canada, covering a range of pharmaceutical starches and excipients. The company also announced governance changes, with Gregory B. Kenny retiring after more than 21 years on the board and Siobhan Talbot joining the board effective 1st April 2026.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KOCH [3 News That Matters]

+ KOCH UNVEILS PLAN FOR NEW DOWNTOWN CONCERT VENUE TO ANCHOR ARTS DISTRICT

Koch has announced plans to build a new concert venue in the downtown arts district, positioning it between Wave and the Orpheum as of 5th May 2026.Construction is scheduled to begin next year, with an opening date targeted for 2029, underscoring Koch's commitment to strengthening the local arts scene and providing a platform for artists. The project is expected to complement existing entertainment options and could boost foot traffic and tourism as the area deepens its cultural footprint.

+ KOCH ACHIEVES WHC CERTIFICATIONS ACROSS 16 FACILITIES FOR BIODIVERSITY EFFORTS

Koch has earned Wildlife Habitat Council Certifications across 16 facilities, reinforcing its leadership in corporate biodiversity and conservation education. The WHC is the world's only voluntary sustainability standard for biodiversity on corporate lands, and Koch’s recognition as of 29th April 2026 underscores an ongoing commitment to environmental stewardship.The WHC Certification process requires ongoing, documented conservation work rather than a one-off submission. Across Koch’s sites, projects focus on protecting ecosystems, safeguarding local water systems, and supporting pollinator populations, with several initiatives earning recognition from Tandem Global, formerly Wildlife Habitat Council, for their effectiveness.Among the certified locations, Flint Hills Resources' Pine Bend and Cottage Grove stand out as Minnesota’s only Gold-certified sites and the first in the Twin Cities metro. Koch’s Wichita, Kansas headquarters also earned Gold recertification for native landscapes and wildlife stewardship. Other Koch brands—Georgia-Pacific, INVISTA, and Molex—have pursued varied levels of certification, including Molex’s Lisle site, which converted more than 17,000 square feet of manicured lawn into native gardens to support local wildlife. Koch’s Stewardship Vision and Principle Based Management framework guide these efforts, signaling a broader commitment to responsible operations across the company.

+ KOCH FERTILIZER SECURES ENERGY STAR CERTIFICATIONS FOR FIVE PLANTS

Koch Fertilizer has earned ENERGY STAR certifications for five plants, a recognition from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and Natural Resources Canada. The certification was announced on 23rd April 2026, underscoring the company's commitment to environmental stewardship and energy efficiency.The five facilities—Enid, Oklahoma; Wever, Iowa; Fort Dodge, Iowa; Beatrice, Nebraska; and Brandon, Manitoba—have collectively earned 25 ENERGY STAR certifications since 2019, signaling sustained progress in energy management. Each site operates in the top 25% for energy efficiency, establishing a benchmark within the fertilizer industry.These certifications reflect Koch Fertilizer's ongoing focus on sustainable operations and responsible resource use across its manufacturing network.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) LAUNCHES SNACK NA SWAK INITIATIVE TO PROMOTE MINDFUL FILIPINO SNACKING

Mondelez International Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) launched its Snack na Swak initiative on Apr. 27, 2026, aiming to reshape snacking habits among Filipinos by encouraging mindful choices within a recommended 250-calorie range advised by local health authorities. The campaign treats snacking as a cultural touchpoint, from quick merienda between meetings to shared family moments, and promotes mindful snacking as an opportunity to balance portions, nutrition, and the overall experience. An interactive session at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist Jo Sebastian guiding participants through the Portion Puzzle, stressing the importance of nutrition labels for confident choices. Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, called for clearer labeling and more portion-controlled options to aid healthier decisions. Mondelez International also notes its role in shaping consumption patterns and has introduced portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier options, reframing familiar snacks such as Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VICE PRESIDENT – MARKETING

On 22nd April 2026, Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing, with Viswanathan having spent nearly eight years at Mondelez and most recently serving as Senior Director of Marketing. In his new role, he will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, including brand equity, innovation, and activation, and will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with India and Global Technology Leadership. Viswanathan began his career as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business at Cadbury India in 2008, later serving as VP Marketing – Chocolates at Cadbury Kraft India from 2011 to 2013. He joined Mondelez International in 2014, relocated to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, and returned to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA, eventually becoming Senior Director of Marketing in 2020. The promotion comes as Mondelez International focuses on strengthening its market position in India amid a digital-forward consumer landscape and ongoing emphasis on innovative marketing strategies

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) GETS UPGRADED BY BOFA ON POSITIVE MANAGEMENT TALKS

Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) attracted investor attention after Bank of America raised its price target on the snack maker to $65 from $62 and kept a Buy rating, citing positive management updates from meetings in March. On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterated its constructive stance and flagged ongoing discussions with European chocolate retailers as a potential growth avenueUBS trimmed its price target on Mondelez to $62 from $63 and maintained a Neutral rating, with the bank expecting first-quarter results across consumer staples to show some improvement while warning that inflation could weigh on earnings in the second half of 2026Mondelez, which produces chocolate, biscuits and baked snacks, also has exposure to gum, candy, cheese, grocery items and powdered beverages. The stock is among 13 NASDAQ names with the highest dividends, adding a yield-oriented angle for investors evaluating the name

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TYSON FOODS [3 News That Matters]

+ TYSON FOODS, INC. (NYSE:TSN) POSTS FREE HOT MEALS FOR FLOOD VICTIMS IN NEW LONDON

Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE:TSN) mobilizes its Meals That Matter disaster relief team to provide free, hot meals to residents affected by flooding in New London. From 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. through Wednesday, April 22, the team will operate at the company’s facility located at 3620 County Road D, in the lower parking lot. The New London facility employs over 400 local residents, and Tyson Foods expects the Meals That Matter crew to serve between 2,000 and more than 10,000 meals daily, depending on demandMichelle Mullins, director of food business operations, food loss and waste at Tyson Foods, said, 'When communities are facing hardship, we believe one of the most powerful things we can do is show up with a hot meal.' Since its inception in 2012, the Meals That Matter initiative has been deployed to over 30 natural disaster sites, helping to provide food assistance during hurricanes, floods, and tornadoes. Tyson Foods continues to support communities affected by natural disasters through this programAs New London develops its response, Tyson Foods remains committed to aiding those in need and reinforcing its role as a community partner dedicated to relief during challenging times

+ TYSON FOODS, INC. (NYSE:TSN) EXPANDS GAME TIME OFFERING WITH THREE NEW SPORTS-THEMED CHICKEN NUGGETS

Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE:TSN) Releases three new sports-themed chicken nuggets shaped as a star, a trophy and a versatile ball to capture the energy of the summer sports season. On 20th April 2026, Tyson released its latest lineup, noting the nuggets are made from 100% all-natural white meat chicken, lightly breaded, and fully cooked, with 20 grams of protein per serving.The Game Time lineup broadens beyond last year’s NFL licensed shapes, which featured team silhouettes for fans of franchises like the Denver Broncos and the Dallas Cowboys, to appeal to a wider range of sports enthusiasts. Available now at select retailers nationwide, the nuggets offer a convenient option for game-day snacking and complement Tyson’s existing lines such as Restaurant Style Waffle Flavored Breaded Chicken Breast Bites and Chicken Dip Wedges.Tyson positions the launch to align with the summer sports calendar, including the 2026 FIFA World Cup, MLB games and the U.S. Open, enabling fans to show team spirit while enjoying a protein-rich, easy-to-prepare snack

+ TYSON FOODS EXPANDS PARTNERSHIP WITH ARKANSAS RAZORBACKS, INTRODUCING JERSEY PATCH ACROSS 19 PROGRAMS

Tyson Foods, Inc. announced a landmark expansion of its partnership with the University of Arkansas Department of Intercollegiate Athletics. Beginning in the 2026-2027 athletic season, Tyson Foods will be featured on the jerseys of all 19 Razorbacks varsity teams, marking one of the first jersey patch sponsorships in collegiate athletics. This announcement was made on the 4th of March, 2026.Under the multi-year agreement, Tyson will be designated Official Protein of the Razorbacks, reinforcing the company's commitment to supporting student-athletes with nutritious protein for peak performance. John Tyson, chairman of Tyson Foods, noted that the company has a long history of supporting the Razorbacks and that the partnership aims to develop champions today and leaders for tomorrow in the state they have called home for more than 90 years.The partnership is described as a broad and enduring engagement, granting Tyson extensive brand integration across Razorback Athletics, including jersey patches, logo visibility on fields and courts, and media backdrops at press conferences. The agreement is being facilitated through Learfield’s Razorback Sports Properties and envisions brand ambassador programs that engage Arkansas student-athletes to reinforce the shared commitment to community and excellence.

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (SINGAPORE:F34) FACES RENEWED SCRUTINY OVER PALM OIL PERMIT ALLEGATIONS

Wilmar International Limited (SINGAPORE:F34) is facing renewed scrutiny as Indonesian prosecutors revisit a prior acquittal related to alleged misconduct in securing palm oil export permits. The appeal by authorities has revived questions about compliance and corporate governance, even after the previous acquittal cleared Wilmar of wrongdoing. On 4th May 2026, analysts and industry observers assess potential implications for Wilmar’s reputation and financial standingThe case centers on Wilmar’s dealings with Indonesian government officials accused of facilitating improper gains in the issuance of export permits for palm oil. Wilmar has consistently maintained its innocence and argued that it operates within applicable rules, but the renewed action may pose challenges for regulatory relationships and investor sentimentIndustry observers note that Wilmar is among the world’s largest palm oil producers, and any perceived deviation from sustainability commitments could affect relationships with consumers and business partners as ESG standards become more prominent in investment decisions. The company’s response and transparency will be watched as the proceedings could influence its long term strategy and governance in a rapidly evolving market

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (SGX:F34) NAVIGATES INDIRECT IMPACTS FROM MIDDLE EAST CONFLICT MAINTAINS ROBUST STRUCTURE

Wilmar International Limited (SGX:F34) Posts Indirect Impacts From Middle East Conflict, Yet Maintains Robust Corporate Structure. The announcement dated 21st April 2026 shows Wilmar anticipates indirect operational effects from the Middle East conflict, while saying its corporate structure remains robust to navigate market fluctuations. The group reports direct exposure to the region is limited, with regional contributions representing a single-digit percentage of annual revenue, and identifies indirect consequences including higher costs for insurance, freight, and fertilizers, as well as volatility in global commodity prices.The firm notes it has managed the shocks in recent years and does not expect to overhaul its framework. It highlights Asia and Africa as core regions for investments and operations, and says long-term capital allocation decisions have not changed given the uncertainty over how long the conflict will last. The company plans to address investors at its annual general meeting on Apr. 23, following inquiries from the Securities Investors Association (Singapore).On legal matters, Wilmar reiterates its commitment to internal discipline and legal compliance. The group cites a 2025 Chinese court ruling involving contract fraud at a local unit and separate charges against a general manager in Indonesia, stating that strong disciplinary action will apply to any staff found to breach instructions. Shares closed at S$3.84 on Friday, down 0.3% before the information was made public.

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL SECURES $1.5 BILLION SYNDICATED LOAN WITH GREENSHOE OPTION

Wilmar International Limited has signed a $1.5 billion syndicated loan facility, announced on 31st March 2026, with a greenshoe option that provides additional financing flexibility for the company’s strategic initiatives.The loan carries a competitive interest rate and reflects Wilmar's strong market presence and robust financial health, with favorable ratings across fundamentals, valuation and visibility that underscore investor confidence.The greenshoe option could be used for strategic acquisitions or investments in new technologies that align with the company's sustainability goals, while supporting ongoing expansion. Wilmar has also been recognized for its ESG practices, with an MSCI ESG score indicating responsible business conduct.

+ GENERAL MILLS [3 News That Matters]

+ GENERAL MILLS, INC. (NYSE:GIS) EXPANDS OMNICHANNEL PROGRAM TO STRENGTHEN GROWTH TRAJECTORY

General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) is positioning omnichannel execution as a central pillar of its Remarkable Experience framework, underscoring its role in driving more consistent consumer engagement across digital and physical retail environments. Apr. 14, 2026 marked a demonstration that its focus on online and offline integration is delivering tangible results.A primary driver has been General Mills' ability to leverage its portfolio scale and high-impact marketing events to achieve differential retail execution. The company has ramped up investments in retail media to capture consumers who prioritize convenience alongside value. This targeted approach to winning online has yielded a tripling of its e-commerce growth across its top five e-commerce retail partners. The omnichannel momentum is corroborated by seven of the top ten North America retail categories showing rising household penetration through the fiscal third quarter.

+ GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) POSTS €1.7 BILLION JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) Posts €1.7 Billion in Junior Subordinated Notes Offering to Strengthen Capital Flexibility. The €1.7 billion issue comprises €1.0 billion of Series A notes at 4.750% fixed until 16 July 2031, after which the rate resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points, and €700 million of Series B notes at 5.250% fixed until 16 July 2034, with resets tied to the same swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series use fixed-to-fixed rate structures designed to provide a predictable income stream for investors while giving General Mills flexibility in financing plans. The offering was made public on 14th April 2026. Interest payments are scheduled annually on 16th July, beginning in 2026. The notes include step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin five years after the first reset date, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset date. General Mills also reserves the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years, with any deferred interest accruing at the applicable rate. The company may redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. Classified as junior subordinated debt, the notes carry a lower claim on assets in liquidation but provide a strategic means to strengthen capital structure and support growth initiatives.

+ GENERAL MILLS REFRESHES CEREAL LINE TO ALIGN WITH CONSUMER TRENDS

General Mills on 7th April 2026 rolled out two cereal innovations, Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney's Moana, part of a broader cereal refresh that aims to remove artificial colors and broaden flavor options through brand collaborations.General Mills traded at 37.67 dollars, about 7.9 percent below the consensus target of 40.89, with recent results showing weakness in the stock, including a 14.9 percent drop over the last 30 days and a 51.2 percent decline over three years. The cereal launches are described as part of a longer term strategy to rebuild brand relevance and consumer engagement, even as earnings are forecast to fall about 6.3 percent annually. The company also confronts valuation questions, as shares sit well below estimated fair value with a price-to-earnings ratio near 9.1 and debt levels that raise questions about cash-flow sufficiency.

+ CONAGRA BRANDS [3 News That Matters]

+ CONAGRA BRANDS, INC. (NYSE:CAG) EXPANDS IRAPUATO PLANT TO BOOST MEXICO PRODUCTION CAPACITY

Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE:CAG) Posts a capacity expansion and upgrade for its Irapuato manufacturing facility in the central Bajio region, investing 550 million pesos (approximately $31.9 million) to upgrade production lines, particularly packaging technologies, and to optimize processes to raise capacity to meet rising demand as of Apr. 22, 2026.The Irapuato plant serves as a strategic pillar for Conagra Brands Mexico, producing ACT II, Del Monte and Hunt’s products and accounting for 94% of the company’s total sales volume in Mexico. Founded in 1962 and acquired by Conagra in 2000, the facility employs more than 800 people and benefits from proximity to key raw materials such as corn, potatoes and carrots.Alberto Cavia, chief executive officer of Conagra Brands Mexico, said, "Mexico is a key market for Conagra Brands, and this investment reaffirms our confidence in the country and its talent." He added that the expansion strengthens production capabilities and reinforces the company’s long-term commitment to sustainable growth, innovation and the development of the food sector in Mexico.

+ CONAGRA BRANDS TO EXPAND FAYETTEVILLE FACILITY, AIMING TO INCREASE CHICKEN PRODUCTION

Conagra Brands is expanding its Fayetteville, Ark., manufacturing facility to significantly increase chicken production capacity. The expansion is part of a $220 million multi-year investment announced in a release dated 6th March 2026 by the Arkansas Economic Development Commission (AEDC).The project is expected to create about 100 new jobs over the next five years, providing a boost to the local economy and workforce.Construction is slated to begin in late 2026 as Conagra works to bolster its protein portfolio and strengthen its foothold in the Fayetteville market.

+ CONAGRA INTRODUCES BANQUET MEGA BREAKFAST BOWLS FEATURING 30G PROTEIN PER BOWL

On 11th February 2026, Conagra Brands announced the launch of Banquet MEGA Breakfast Bowls, a protein-focused entry targeting the $626 million breakfast bowls category. Each bowl provides 30 grams of protein, catering to health-conscious consumers seeking convenient morning options.The bowls feature tray-in-tray steaming technology that keeps ingredients fresh during cooking. The frozen design separates the sauce from other components, delivering fluffy eggs, tender potatoes, and juicy protein; after heating, the sauce is stirred in for a full-flavored meal.Four varieties are available: Meat Lovers with scrambled eggs, cooked sausage, roasted potatoes, ham, bacon, and cheddar cheese sauce; Bacon with scrambled eggs, roasted potatoes, bacon, and cheddar cheese sauce; Sausage Bowl with scrambled eggs, cooked sausage, roasted potatoes, and cheddar cheese sauce; and Sausage & Gravy with scrambled eggs, cooked sausage, roasted potatoes, country gravy, and shredded cheese. Banquet MEGA Breakfast Bowls are rolling out to grocery and mass retail stores throughout February.

+ PERDUE FARMS INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ PERDUE FARMS DONATES PROTEIN TO OHIO COUNTY HEALTHCARE WORKERS

HENDERSON, Ky. — On April 27, 2026, Perdue Farms delivered a truckload of chicken products to Ohio County Healthcare, providing nourishment for 1,000 healthcare workers and first responders. Officials say the delivery is a gesture of appreciation for their service and commitment to the community.\n\nPerdue Farms' Director of Operations, Allen Pavlovich, said, \"These individuals ensure the health and safety of their communities every day and are the backbone of Ohio County. This is a small way to show our gratitude for their tireless service and help nourish them and their families.\" \n\nThe effort is part of Perdue Farms' Delivering Hope To Our Neighbors outreach program, which aims to improve quality of life and strengthen communities where associates live, work, and raise their families. The focus on community support aligns with the growing link between agriculture and health.\n\nShellie Shouse, CEO of Ohio County Healthcare, praised the long-standing relationship with Perdue Farms, noting, \"Ohio County Healthcare has worked alongside Perdue Farms for more than 30 years to provide outstanding care to the communities we serve. As a proud supporter of the statewide Food Is Medicine initiative, OCH values the alignment between healthcare and agriculture in expanding access to nutritious food.\" Shouse added, \"Perdue’s generous donation of a healthy protein source to each OCH employee and local first responders is a meaningful way to say thank you to the men and women who answer the call without hesitation and care for our community when it matters most.\"

+ PERDUE FOODS NAMES ANDY URBAN CHIEF COMMERCIAL OFFICER TO LEAD GROWTH

Perdue Foods announced that Andy Urban will become chief commercial officer, effective 8th April 2026, with responsibility for sales, marketing, customer development, revenue management and innovation to drive disciplined growth and strengthen customer partnerships. Urban brings more than 35 years of leadership in multi-channel consumer packaged goods across retail, food service and international markets. He has been with Perdue since 2009 in senior roles, most recently leading the Transformation Office to streamline operations and improve execution. Kevin McAdams, Perdue Farms president and CEO, described Urban as a proven commercial leader with a deep understanding of the business, customers and culture, noting his track record in driving growth and building high-performing teams. "As CCO, Urban will lead our commercial organization to deliver value for customers and the company," he said. "I'm honored to step into this role at a pivotal time for Perdue," Urban added. As CCO, Urban aims to accelerate customer-centric growth and strengthen collaboration across the organization.

+ PERDUE FARMS DONATES $100,000 TO DELVAL POULTRY SCIENCE CENTER

Delaware Valley University has received a $100,000 gift from Perdue Farms and the Franklin P. and Arthur W. Perdue Foundation to fund a new Poultry Science Center on the university main campus. The gift, announced on 9th February 2026, aligns with Perdue Delivering Hope To Our Neighbors initiative that supports community well being and agricultural education.The grant was presented to DelVal President Dr. Benjamin E. Rusiloski and Dean of Agriculture and Environmental Science Dr. Broc Sandelin during a ceremony on Wednesday. Sandelin noted that the poultry industry is expanding and that the center will provide hands on learning and support the introduction of a dedicated Poultry Science academic program at the university.The Poultry Science Center will include a broiler building, a layer building and a pen research trials building, as well as laboratories, classrooms and demonstration spaces. In recognition of the Perdue ongoing support, a study room in the new center will bear the company name, reflecting its role in advancing poultry science education. Mike Levengood, Chief Animal Care Officer at Perdue, said that investing in education strengthens hands on learning and helps prepare students for careers in the poultry industry.

+ ARCHER DANIELS MIDLAND [3 News That Matters]

+ ADM AEROPORTS DE MONTREAL (NYSE:ADM) PARTNERS WITH VERIFORCE CANADA TO STRENGTHEN CONTRACTOR COMPLIANCE

MONTREAL, 15th April 2026 /CNW/ ADM Aeroports de Montreal Posts a strategic partnership with Veriforce Canada to bolster contractor compliance and training management across its operations. Veriforce Canada Partners With ADM Aeroports de Montreal to deploy SafeContractor Canada, a centralized platform designed to streamline contractor requirements and strengthen governance across multiple projects. The SafeContractor platform will grant ADM real-time visibility into contractor qualifications, certifications, and training records, enabling informed decision-making while reducing onboarding and site-access administration. Jean Charron, Deputy Director of Occupational Health and Safety (Construction) at ADM, notes that rigorous adherence to compliance standards is fundamental to the management of projects, the protection of airport users and workers, and the integrity of operations. Henriette Bichai, President of Veriforce Canada, emphasizes consistency and accountability in regulated environments like airports. Veriforce Canada, with 1,800 employees globally, including 300 in Canada, specializes in helping organizations mitigate risk and enhance governance through its technology and health and safety expertise. This partnership aligns with ADM Aeroports de Montreal's ongoing efforts to elevate compliance standards within the aviation sector

+ ADM EXPANDS BOARD TO STRENGTHEN SOUTH AMERICAN GROWTH STRATEGY

Archer-Daniels-Midland Co (NYSE:ADM) has expanded its board by appointing Michael McMurray, a veteran with more than 30 years in the industry, to bolster its South American growth push. The decision was announced on 31st March 2026 as the company seeks to deepen its operations in a region it views as central to its future performance. McMurray previously held senior roles at LyondellBasell Industries and Royal Dutch Shell, bringing governance, financial management and strategic leadership experience to ADM's board.CEO and board chair Juan Luciano said McMurray brings a proven track record in business strategy and mergers and acquisitions that will support ADM as it executes its plans in South America. In tandem with the appointment, the company has been engaging with investors and industry stakeholders to outline its strategic initiatives, including participation in the Bank of America 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference, where executives highlighted opportunities and challenges facing the agricultural sector and reaffirmed ADM's commitment to transparency and collaboration.

+ ADM PLANS GLOBAL HEADQUARTERS AND CUSTOMER CENTER TO STRENGTHEN CUSTOMER FOCUS

ADM announced on 30th March 2026 that it is exploring the establishment of a new global headquarters and customer center to enhance its global presence and customer-centric operations. The decision was revealed by Patricia Woertz, ADM's chairman and chief executive officer, who emphasized the need for a central location to facilitate efficient engagement with customers and employees worldwide.The company is evaluating potential locations and engaging with public officials and advisors, with specific sites not disclosed. The plan includes relocating a small corporate team to the new global center, involving about 100 job transfers, and establishing a new IT tech center that is expected to create around 100 positions in coming years. Woertz stressed that there will be no layoffs.ADM will maintain its 4,400-employee Decatur workforce, and its current Decatur office will be designated as the North American headquarters. The company also announced multi-year commitments to support the Decatur community, including $250,000 annually for three years to support a unified marketing campaign through the Economic Development Corporation of Decatur &Macon County, $500,000 per year for five years to Decatur Public School District 61, and $1 million per year in community support for at least the next decade. Looking ahead, Woertz noted optimism about a strong North American harvest and the GrainCorp acquisition, with the new global center positioned to sustain growth in the agricultural sector as ADM transforms crops into products for global markets.

+ BUNGE GLOBAL [3 News That Matters]

+ BUNGE GLOBAL SA (NYSE:BG) RAISES SECURITIZATION CAPACITY TO $2 BILLION WITH FINACITY GUIDANCE

Bunge Global SA (NYSE:BG) raises its Accounts Receivable Securitization Program to $2 billion from $1.5 billion, with Finacity guiding the expansion, on April 13th, 2026. The move expands capacity and strengthens Bunge's ability to manage receivables as it adapts to evolving agribusiness needs. The expansion also integrates Viterra Limited into the securitization structure, increasing capacity and reinforcing the company's operating framework. With about 34,000 employees and a footprint in more than 50 countries, Bunge oversees grain origination, storage, distribution and oilseed processing. The company maintains a commitment to sustainability and food security through innovative solutions for farmers and consumers worldwide. Finacity, a White Oak Global Advisors Company, structures efficient capital market solutions and has supported over $200 billion in annual receivables volume. Its global reach and expertise in off-balance-sheet securitizations under US GAAP and IFRS position it as a key partner for Bunge as it navigates the agricultural markets.

+ BUNGE GLOBAL UPGRADED AS GEOPOLITICAL TENSIONS BOOST OUTLOOK

Bunge Global SA (NYSE: BG) has received a rating upgrade, framed by a positive outlook tied to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The conflict has helped push oil prices higher, supporting margins for soybean oil and other key commodities, and boosting demand for soy oil used in biodiesel.An analysis dated 26th March 2026 notes the current environment could act as a near term catalyst, while the company continues to pursue long term execution strategies. The analysis points to an EPS target of $15 and reports that analysts have upgraded the stock from Hold to Buy, with potential upside estimates from 74% to 185%.Bunge Global emphasizes its focus on sourcing efficiency and operational synergies to capitalize on market dynamics and volatility in commodity prices. The rating upgrade underpins an investment case that combines favorable conditions with disciplined execution as the company positions for growth.

+ BOFA RAISES BUNGE TARGET AFTER POSITIVE INVESTOR DAY

On 26th March 2026, Bank of America raised its price target for Bunge Global SA to $130 from $125 and reiterated a Buy rating, citing Investor Day showing higher-than-expected midcycle EPS projections of $13 to $15, along with a broader synergy target and additional margin-enhancing opportunities. The firm said the Investor Day addressed investor fears of a disappointing outlook and signaled a path to further earnings upside.On the same day, BMO Capital raised its price target to $135 from $130 and kept an Outperform rating, noting confidence in Bunge's multi-year earnings trajectory after management outlined a plan to hit the $15 mid-cycle EPS target by 2030 during the event.Beyond the upgrades, Bunge operates as a global agribusiness and food company with products ranging from canned vegetables to branded items such as Green Giant and Mrs. Dash, positioning it to capture shifting consumer demand and realize efficiency gains from its platform. While some investors weigh AI stocks for higher near-term upside, Bunge remains a defensive option with potential longer-term upside as its strategy unfolds.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-R103-SD9Y-N269-00000-00CARGILL INCORPORATED - PEER SECTOR NEWS UPDATES2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisSTOCKCARGILL INCORPORATED - PEER SECTOR NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CARGILL

PermID4296555324Websitehttp://www.cargill.comIndustryAgribusiness & Food ProcessingAddressPO Box 9300 Minneapolis, Minnesota 55440-9300 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

+ CARGILL [3 News That Matters]

+ CARGILL AGRI PURINA LAUNCHES TENDER FOR 140,000 MT OF CORN AMID ASIA DEMAND SURGE

HAMBURG — The date of the announcement is 4th May 2026. South Korean importer Cargill Agri Purina has issued an international tender to purchase around 140,000 metric tons of animal-feed corn, European traders said on Tuesday. The corn is sought from South America or South Africa in two consignments ranging from 55,000 to 70,000 tons each, with arrivals scheduled for August in South Korea. The price-offer deadline was set for Tuesday, 28th April.The first consignment is expected to arrive around 15th August, with shipments from South America planned between 12th June and 1st July or from South Africa between 27th June and 16th July. The second consignment is slated for around 20th August, with South American shipments between 17th June and 6th July and South Africa shipments between 2nd July and 21st July.Offers are requested on an outright c&f basis or as a premium over the Chicago July 2026 corn contract. Traders say only one of the two consignments may be purchased, within the 55,000 to 70,000-ton range. The tender reflects brisk demand for corn in Asia amid geopolitical tensions, and Taiwan's MFIG purchasing group has also issued an international tender for up to 65,000 tons from the United States, Argentina, Brazil or South Africa.

+ RSGBL COMPLETES CARGILL ASSET ACQUISITION IN DAVANGERE TO BROADEN SOUTHERN INDIAN FOOTPRINT

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd (RSGBL) has completed the acquisition of a corn milling plant and related assets in Davangere, Karnataka from Cargill. The deal, finalized on 28th April 2026 under an Asset Purchase Agreement, includes land, production facilities, warehouses and corn silos, expanding RSGBL's southern India footprint.The Davangere plant, in operation since 2016, processes about 300,000 metric tons annually. The acquisition broadens RSGBL's capacity to serve the food, pharmaceutical and industrial sectors while underscoring the company's commitment to sustainable manufacturing practices, including greater use of renewable energy sources.Executive Director Siddharth Chowdhary said, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals."

+ CARGILL INVESTS RP38.8 BILLION TO BOOST INDONESIAN COCOA FARMERS VIA RAINFOREST ALLIANCE

Cargill announced an investment of Rp38.8 billion (US$2.25 million) to support more than 7,800 cocoa farmers in Indonesia through the Rainforest Alliance certification program, disclosed on 26th of April 2026. The move underscores Cargill's commitment to sustainable sourcing and farmer empowerment in the cocoa sector.The premium payments total about Rp35 billion (US$2.02 million) to farmers in Central Sulawesi and Rp3.8 billion to those in East Nusa Tenggara. The payments supplement farmers' income from cocoa bean sales and are conditional on meeting globally recognized sustainability standards, reflecting Cargill's focus on ethical and responsible sourcing.In Central Sulawesi, more than 5,600 farmers participate, including over 600 women farmers. From May 2023 to February 2026, roughly 16,000 metric tonnes of Rainforest Alliance-certified cocoa beans were sourced from Parigi Moutong, Poso and North Morowali. In East Nusa Tenggara, about 2,200 farmers contributed close to 2,000 metric tonnes from Ende, Sikka, East Manggarai and East Flores. Brook Chang, Director of Sustainability for Food APAC at Cargill, said that investing in training, certification and premium payments linked to verified sustainability standards helps farmers raise income and productivity while strengthening traceability and supply reliability for customers and partners.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ INGREDION [3 News That Matters]

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSTS LATEST PROTEIN AND SUGAR SOLUTIONS AT VITAFOODS EUROPE 2026

Manchester, UK – Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) will showcase its latest advances in protein fortification, texture enhancement, and sugar reduction at Vitafoods Europe 2026 in Barcelona, helping brands meet EMEA consumers' rising expectations for great taste, appealing texture, and ingredient lists that support clean-label appeal. The date of this announcement is 23rd April 2026.The integrated portfolio aims to deliver simpler, more recognisable components while enhancing nutrition characteristics such as protein enrichment and reduced sugar, trends that consumers increasingly prioritise. Visitors to Ingredion's stand will explore VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD, a cutting-edge pea protein ideal for cold-pressed applications that maintains softness throughout its shelf life while enhancing texture and providing effective protein fortification. VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD also demonstrates versatility in bakery applications, enabling protein enrichment without compromising quality or taste.Beyond Vitafoods, Ingredion continues to advance its strategic partnership with Lantmannen, first announced in 2024, underscoring a commitment to European-sourced, plant-based protein solutions and stronger regional sourcing for customers in Europe. To discover more about Ingredion's ingredient solutions, visit stand number #3D252 at Vitafoods Europe in Barcelona, Spain.

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSITIONED TO BENEFIT FROM AI-DRIVEN PIVOT

Barclays Reports On AI's Impact Across the Agribusiness Sector in the Americas, noting Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) is well positioned to benefit in the near term. The bank says Ingredion is shifting from a commodity-focused model to a solutions-oriented approach, leveraging its library of ingredients and formulations to meet unmet customer needs. The pivot could enable faster monetization of AI opportunities, particularly for companies with solid balance sheets, Barclays writes in the report published on 22nd April 2026. In the medium term, Barclays identifies seeds and crop chemicals as sectors likely to see AI-driven advancements, with implications for faster product development cycles, improved pricing power, and structural efficiency. Ingredion and Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) are highlighted as frontrunners in AI adoption, while FMC (NYSE:FMC) may face execution delays given internal restructuring. Barclays also notes potential gains in protein processing from AI-enabled automation and yield improvements, which could reduce recalls and boost profitability. Overall, the bank argues AI will reinforce market leaders by driving innovation, lowering costs, and enhancing capital efficiency across the agriculture landscape. Investors will monitor how Ingredion’s strategy unfolds as AI transforms agricultural practices

+ INGREDION NAMES JASON PAYANT INTERIM CFO AS LEADERSHIP TRANSITION ADVANCES

Ingredion Incorporated has announced the appointment of Jason Payant as Interim Chief Financial Officer, effective April 1, following the resignation of James D. Gray as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, which will take effect on 31st March 2026. Payant, 55, has been with Ingredion since 2012 and most recently served as Vice President, Finance, Global Texture & Healthful Solutions since November 2025; he will continue to fulfill that role alongside his new CFO responsibilities.The company confirmed there are no arrangements between Payant and other persons regarding the appointment, and no transactions involving Payant or his immediate family have any material interest. Payant will receive a monthly cash compensation of $25,000 in addition to his current compensation and benefits. In other business, Ingredion declared a quarterly dividend of $0.82 per share payable on 21st April 2026 to stockholders of record as of 1st April 2026, and announced a research and development collaboration with Shiru aimed at using Shiru's AI discovery platform to develop functional proteins and create prebiotics from natural sources for nutritional products.Ingredion also named Univar Solutions LLC as the exclusive distributor for Ingredion Pharma Solutions in the United States and Canada, covering a range of pharmaceutical starches and excipients. The company also announced governance changes, with Gregory B. Kenny retiring after more than 21 years on the board and Siobhan Talbot joining the board effective 1st April 2026.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KOCH [3 News That Matters]

+ KOCH UNVEILS PLAN FOR NEW DOWNTOWN CONCERT VENUE TO ANCHOR ARTS DISTRICT

Koch has announced plans to build a new concert venue in the downtown arts district, positioning it between Wave and the Orpheum as of 5th May 2026.Construction is scheduled to begin next year, with an opening date targeted for 2029, underscoring Koch's commitment to strengthening the local arts scene and providing a platform for artists. The project is expected to complement existing entertainment options and could boost foot traffic and tourism as the area deepens its cultural footprint.

+ KOCH ACHIEVES WHC CERTIFICATIONS ACROSS 16 FACILITIES FOR BIODIVERSITY EFFORTS

Koch has earned Wildlife Habitat Council Certifications across 16 facilities, reinforcing its leadership in corporate biodiversity and conservation education. The WHC is the world's only voluntary sustainability standard for biodiversity on corporate lands, and Koch’s recognition as of 29th April 2026 underscores an ongoing commitment to environmental stewardship.The WHC Certification process requires ongoing, documented conservation work rather than a one-off submission. Across Koch’s sites, projects focus on protecting ecosystems, safeguarding local water systems, and supporting pollinator populations, with several initiatives earning recognition from Tandem Global, formerly Wildlife Habitat Council, for their effectiveness.Among the certified locations, Flint Hills Resources' Pine Bend and Cottage Grove stand out as Minnesota’s only Gold-certified sites and the first in the Twin Cities metro. Koch’s Wichita, Kansas headquarters also earned Gold recertification for native landscapes and wildlife stewardship. Other Koch brands—Georgia-Pacific, INVISTA, and Molex—have pursued varied levels of certification, including Molex’s Lisle site, which converted more than 17,000 square feet of manicured lawn into native gardens to support local wildlife. Koch’s Stewardship Vision and Principle Based Management framework guide these efforts, signaling a broader commitment to responsible operations across the company.

+ KOCH FERTILIZER SECURES ENERGY STAR CERTIFICATIONS FOR FIVE PLANTS

Koch Fertilizer has earned ENERGY STAR certifications for five plants, a recognition from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and Natural Resources Canada. The certification was announced on 23rd April 2026, underscoring the company's commitment to environmental stewardship and energy efficiency.The five facilities—Enid, Oklahoma; Wever, Iowa; Fort Dodge, Iowa; Beatrice, Nebraska; and Brandon, Manitoba—have collectively earned 25 ENERGY STAR certifications since 2019, signaling sustained progress in energy management. Each site operates in the top 25% for energy efficiency, establishing a benchmark within the fertilizer industry.These certifications reflect Koch Fertilizer's ongoing focus on sustainable operations and responsible resource use across its manufacturing network.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) LAUNCHES SNACK NA SWAK INITIATIVE TO PROMOTE MINDFUL FILIPINO SNACKING

Mondelez International Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) launched its Snack na Swak initiative on Apr. 27, 2026, aiming to reshape snacking habits among Filipinos by encouraging mindful choices within a recommended 250-calorie range advised by local health authorities. The campaign treats snacking as a cultural touchpoint, from quick merienda between meetings to shared family moments, and promotes mindful snacking as an opportunity to balance portions, nutrition, and the overall experience. An interactive session at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist Jo Sebastian guiding participants through the Portion Puzzle, stressing the importance of nutrition labels for confident choices. Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, called for clearer labeling and more portion-controlled options to aid healthier decisions. Mondelez International also notes its role in shaping consumption patterns and has introduced portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier options, reframing familiar snacks such as Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VICE PRESIDENT – MARKETING

On 22nd April 2026, Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing, with Viswanathan having spent nearly eight years at Mondelez and most recently serving as Senior Director of Marketing. In his new role, he will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, including brand equity, innovation, and activation, and will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with India and Global Technology Leadership. Viswanathan began his career as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business at Cadbury India in 2008, later serving as VP Marketing – Chocolates at Cadbury Kraft India from 2011 to 2013. He joined Mondelez International in 2014, relocated to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, and returned to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA, eventually becoming Senior Director of Marketing in 2020. The promotion comes as Mondelez International focuses on strengthening its market position in India amid a digital-forward consumer landscape and ongoing emphasis on innovative marketing strategies

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) GETS UPGRADED BY BOFA ON POSITIVE MANAGEMENT TALKS

Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) attracted investor attention after Bank of America raised its price target on the snack maker to $65 from $62 and kept a Buy rating, citing positive management updates from meetings in March. On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterated its constructive stance and flagged ongoing discussions with European chocolate retailers as a potential growth avenueUBS trimmed its price target on Mondelez to $62 from $63 and maintained a Neutral rating, with the bank expecting first-quarter results across consumer staples to show some improvement while warning that inflation could weigh on earnings in the second half of 2026Mondelez, which produces chocolate, biscuits and baked snacks, also has exposure to gum, candy, cheese, grocery items and powdered beverages. The stock is among 13 NASDAQ names with the highest dividends, adding a yield-oriented angle for investors evaluating the name

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TYSON FOODS [3 News That Matters]

+ TYSON FOODS, INC. (NYSE:TSN) POSTS FREE HOT MEALS FOR FLOOD VICTIMS IN NEW LONDON

Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE:TSN) mobilizes its Meals That Matter disaster relief team to provide free, hot meals to residents affected by flooding in New London. From 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. through Wednesday, April 22, the team will operate at the company’s facility located at 3620 County Road D, in the lower parking lot. The New London facility employs over 400 local residents, and Tyson Foods expects the Meals That Matter crew to serve between 2,000 and more than 10,000 meals daily, depending on demandMichelle Mullins, director of food business operations, food loss and waste at Tyson Foods, said, 'When communities are facing hardship, we believe one of the most powerful things we can do is show up with a hot meal.' Since its inception in 2012, the Meals That Matter initiative has been deployed to over 30 natural disaster sites, helping to provide food assistance during hurricanes, floods, and tornadoes. Tyson Foods continues to support communities affected by natural disasters through this programAs New London develops its response, Tyson Foods remains committed to aiding those in need and reinforcing its role as a community partner dedicated to relief during challenging times

+ TYSON FOODS, INC. (NYSE:TSN) EXPANDS GAME TIME OFFERING WITH THREE NEW SPORTS-THEMED CHICKEN NUGGETS

Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE:TSN) Releases three new sports-themed chicken nuggets shaped as a star, a trophy and a versatile ball to capture the energy of the summer sports season. On 20th April 2026, Tyson released its latest lineup, noting the nuggets are made from 100% all-natural white meat chicken, lightly breaded, and fully cooked, with 20 grams of protein per serving.The Game Time lineup broadens beyond last year’s NFL licensed shapes, which featured team silhouettes for fans of franchises like the Denver Broncos and the Dallas Cowboys, to appeal to a wider range of sports enthusiasts. Available now at select retailers nationwide, the nuggets offer a convenient option for game-day snacking and complement Tyson’s existing lines such as Restaurant Style Waffle Flavored Breaded Chicken Breast Bites and Chicken Dip Wedges.Tyson positions the launch to align with the summer sports calendar, including the 2026 FIFA World Cup, MLB games and the U.S. Open, enabling fans to show team spirit while enjoying a protein-rich, easy-to-prepare snack

+ TYSON FOODS EXPANDS PARTNERSHIP WITH ARKANSAS RAZORBACKS, INTRODUCING JERSEY PATCH ACROSS 19 PROGRAMS

Tyson Foods, Inc. announced a landmark expansion of its partnership with the University of Arkansas Department of Intercollegiate Athletics. Beginning in the 2026-2027 athletic season, Tyson Foods will be featured on the jerseys of all 19 Razorbacks varsity teams, marking one of the first jersey patch sponsorships in collegiate athletics. This announcement was made on the 4th of March, 2026.Under the multi-year agreement, Tyson will be designated Official Protein of the Razorbacks, reinforcing the company's commitment to supporting student-athletes with nutritious protein for peak performance. John Tyson, chairman of Tyson Foods, noted that the company has a long history of supporting the Razorbacks and that the partnership aims to develop champions today and leaders for tomorrow in the state they have called home for more than 90 years.The partnership is described as a broad and enduring engagement, granting Tyson extensive brand integration across Razorback Athletics, including jersey patches, logo visibility on fields and courts, and media backdrops at press conferences. The agreement is being facilitated through Learfield’s Razorback Sports Properties and envisions brand ambassador programs that engage Arkansas student-athletes to reinforce the shared commitment to community and excellence.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (SI:F34) MAINTAINS DOW JONES BEST-IN-CLASS STATUS FOR FIFTH CONSECUTIVE YEAR

Wilmar International Limited (SI:F34) has secured its position in the Dow Jones Best-in-Class World Index for the fifth consecutive year, reinforcing its standing as the only Singapore-based Food Products company featured in both the Dow Jones Best-in-Class World Index and the Asia Pacific Index. The milestone was announced on 4 May 2026, underscoring Wilmar's ongoing commitment to sustainability. Group Chief Sustainability Officer for Wilmar, Jeremy Goon, said the recognition reflects the long-term investments across the company's global operations. "This consistent recognition is a testament to the long-term commitment of our teams across our global operations," he stated, adding that sustainability is an ongoing responsibility toward the environment, communities and stakeholders. In S&P Global CSA 2025, Wilmar earned an ESG score of 68 out of 100, placing it in the 92nd percentile of the Food Products category. The group outperformed peers across Environmental (79), Social (61) and Governance (60) dimensions, compared with an industry ESG average of 31. Beyond index recognition, Wilmar has advanced its sustainability program. The company is the only agricultural entity listed in Singapore to have both near- and long-term Science-Based Targets validated by the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi). It has conserved about 33,000 hectares on its oil palm and sugar plantations, including efforts to restore riparian zones to bolster ecosystem resilience. CSA disclosures reached 93 percent, with S&P Global rating the group's performance as very high relative to peers. Wilmar's ongoing focus on children's rights and gender diversity further reinforces its leadership in the agribusiness sector.

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (SINGAPORE:F34) FACES RENEWED SCRUTINY OVER PALM OIL PERMIT ALLEGATIONS

Wilmar International Limited (SINGAPORE:F34) is facing renewed scrutiny as Indonesian prosecutors revisit a prior acquittal related to alleged misconduct in securing palm oil export permits. The appeal by authorities has revived questions about compliance and corporate governance, even after the previous acquittal cleared Wilmar of wrongdoing. On 4th May 2026, analysts and industry observers assess potential implications for Wilmar’s reputation and financial standingThe case centers on Wilmar’s dealings with Indonesian government officials accused of facilitating improper gains in the issuance of export permits for palm oil. Wilmar has consistently maintained its innocence and argued that it operates within applicable rules, but the renewed action may pose challenges for regulatory relationships and investor sentimentIndustry observers note that Wilmar is among the world’s largest palm oil producers, and any perceived deviation from sustainability commitments could affect relationships with consumers and business partners as ESG standards become more prominent in investment decisions. The company’s response and transparency will be watched as the proceedings could influence its long term strategy and governance in a rapidly evolving market

+ WILMAR INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (SGX:F34) NAVIGATES INDIRECT IMPACTS FROM MIDDLE EAST CONFLICT MAINTAINS ROBUST STRUCTURE

Wilmar International Limited (SGX:F34) Posts Indirect Impacts From Middle East Conflict, Yet Maintains Robust Corporate Structure. The announcement dated 21st April 2026 shows Wilmar anticipates indirect operational effects from the Middle East conflict, while saying its corporate structure remains robust to navigate market fluctuations. The group reports direct exposure to the region is limited, with regional contributions representing a single-digit percentage of annual revenue, and identifies indirect consequences including higher costs for insurance, freight, and fertilizers, as well as volatility in global commodity prices.The firm notes it has managed the shocks in recent years and does not expect to overhaul its framework. It highlights Asia and Africa as core regions for investments and operations, and says long-term capital allocation decisions have not changed given the uncertainty over how long the conflict will last. The company plans to address investors at its annual general meeting on Apr. 23, following inquiries from the Securities Investors Association (Singapore).On legal matters, Wilmar reiterates its commitment to internal discipline and legal compliance. The group cites a 2025 Chinese court ruling involving contract fraud at a local unit and separate charges against a general manager in Indonesia, stating that strong disciplinary action will apply to any staff found to breach instructions. Shares closed at S$3.84 on Friday, down 0.3% before the information was made public.

+ GENERAL MILLS [3 News That Matters]

+ GENERAL MILLS, INC. (NYSE:GIS) EXPANDS OMNICHANNEL PROGRAM TO STRENGTHEN GROWTH TRAJECTORY

General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) is positioning omnichannel execution as a central pillar of its Remarkable Experience framework, underscoring its role in driving more consistent consumer engagement across digital and physical retail environments. Apr. 14, 2026 marked a demonstration that its focus on online and offline integration is delivering tangible results.A primary driver has been General Mills' ability to leverage its portfolio scale and high-impact marketing events to achieve differential retail execution. The company has ramped up investments in retail media to capture consumers who prioritize convenience alongside value. This targeted approach to winning online has yielded a tripling of its e-commerce growth across its top five e-commerce retail partners. The omnichannel momentum is corroborated by seven of the top ten North America retail categories showing rising household penetration through the fiscal third quarter.

+ GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) POSTS €1.7 BILLION JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) Posts €1.7 Billion in Junior Subordinated Notes Offering to Strengthen Capital Flexibility. The €1.7 billion issue comprises €1.0 billion of Series A notes at 4.750% fixed until 16 July 2031, after which the rate resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points, and €700 million of Series B notes at 5.250% fixed until 16 July 2034, with resets tied to the same swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series use fixed-to-fixed rate structures designed to provide a predictable income stream for investors while giving General Mills flexibility in financing plans. The offering was made public on 14th April 2026. Interest payments are scheduled annually on 16th July, beginning in 2026. The notes include step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin five years after the first reset date, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset date. General Mills also reserves the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years, with any deferred interest accruing at the applicable rate. The company may redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. Classified as junior subordinated debt, the notes carry a lower claim on assets in liquidation but provide a strategic means to strengthen capital structure and support growth initiatives.

+ GENERAL MILLS REFRESHES CEREAL LINE TO ALIGN WITH CONSUMER TRENDS

General Mills on 7th April 2026 rolled out two cereal innovations, Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney's Moana, part of a broader cereal refresh that aims to remove artificial colors and broaden flavor options through brand collaborations.General Mills traded at 37.67 dollars, about 7.9 percent below the consensus target of 40.89, with recent results showing weakness in the stock, including a 14.9 percent drop over the last 30 days and a 51.2 percent decline over three years. The cereal launches are described as part of a longer term strategy to rebuild brand relevance and consumer engagement, even as earnings are forecast to fall about 6.3 percent annually. The company also confronts valuation questions, as shares sit well below estimated fair value with a price-to-earnings ratio near 9.1 and debt levels that raise questions about cash-flow sufficiency.

+ CONAGRA BRANDS [3 News That Matters]

+ CONAGRA BRANDS, INC. (NYSE:CAG) RELEASES FISCAL 2025 CITIZENSHIP REPORT DETAILING SUSTAINABILITY MILESTONES

On 4th May 2026, Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE:CAG) released its Fiscal 2025 Citizenship Report detailing progress across the four pillars Good Food, Responsible Sourcing, Better Planet and Stronger Communities, with a focus on employees, communities and environmental health. The report shows how citizenship is integrated into the company's operations and growth strategy.Key highlights include Good Food with new labeling on select products to provide better access to nutritional information and full Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) certification for production facilities. About 7,500 SmartLabel pages are live, covering more than 3,600 unique items. Under Responsible Sourcing, Conagra launched a priority-ingredients assessment in collaboration with the Kellogg School of Management to map social and environmental risks in its supply chain. The company also joined the Circular Action Alliance as a founding member, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable packaging.Under Better Planet, the company updated its 2035 climate targets in line with Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) guidelines. It diverted about 90% of solid waste from landfills and earned Zero Waste Champion recognition for 19 production facilities. In Stronger Communities, employees donated over 19 million pounds of food and logged more than 10,000 volunteer hours. For more details, the complete report is available on Conagra Brands' official website.

+ CONAGRA BRANDS, INC. (NYSE:CAG) EXPANDS IRAPUATO PLANT TO BOOST MEXICO PRODUCTION CAPACITY

Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE:CAG) Posts a capacity expansion and upgrade for its Irapuato manufacturing facility in the central Bajio region, investing 550 million pesos (approximately $31.9 million) to upgrade production lines, particularly packaging technologies, and to optimize processes to raise capacity to meet rising demand as of Apr. 22, 2026.The Irapuato plant serves as a strategic pillar for Conagra Brands Mexico, producing ACT II, Del Monte and Hunt’s products and accounting for 94% of the company’s total sales volume in Mexico. Founded in 1962 and acquired by Conagra in 2000, the facility employs more than 800 people and benefits from proximity to key raw materials such as corn, potatoes and carrots.Alberto Cavia, chief executive officer of Conagra Brands Mexico, said, "Mexico is a key market for Conagra Brands, and this investment reaffirms our confidence in the country and its talent." He added that the expansion strengthens production capabilities and reinforces the company’s long-term commitment to sustainable growth, innovation and the development of the food sector in Mexico.

+ CONAGRA BRANDS TO EXPAND FAYETTEVILLE FACILITY, AIMING TO INCREASE CHICKEN PRODUCTION

Conagra Brands is expanding its Fayetteville, Ark., manufacturing facility to significantly increase chicken production capacity. The expansion is part of a $220 million multi-year investment announced in a release dated 6th March 2026 by the Arkansas Economic Development Commission (AEDC).The project is expected to create about 100 new jobs over the next five years, providing a boost to the local economy and workforce.Construction is slated to begin in late 2026 as Conagra works to bolster its protein portfolio and strengthen its foothold in the Fayetteville market.

+ PERDUE FARMS INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ PERDUE FARMS DONATES PROTEIN TO OHIO COUNTY HEALTHCARE WORKERS

HENDERSON, Ky. — On April 27, 2026, Perdue Farms delivered a truckload of chicken products to Ohio County Healthcare, providing nourishment for 1,000 healthcare workers and first responders. Officials say the delivery is a gesture of appreciation for their service and commitment to the community.\n\nPerdue Farms' Director of Operations, Allen Pavlovich, said, \"These individuals ensure the health and safety of their communities every day and are the backbone of Ohio County. This is a small way to show our gratitude for their tireless service and help nourish them and their families.\" \n\nThe effort is part of Perdue Farms' Delivering Hope To Our Neighbors outreach program, which aims to improve quality of life and strengthen communities where associates live, work, and raise their families. The focus on community support aligns with the growing link between agriculture and health.\n\nShellie Shouse, CEO of Ohio County Healthcare, praised the long-standing relationship with Perdue Farms, noting, \"Ohio County Healthcare has worked alongside Perdue Farms for more than 30 years to provide outstanding care to the communities we serve. As a proud supporter of the statewide Food Is Medicine initiative, OCH values the alignment between healthcare and agriculture in expanding access to nutritious food.\" Shouse added, \"Perdue’s generous donation of a healthy protein source to each OCH employee and local first responders is a meaningful way to say thank you to the men and women who answer the call without hesitation and care for our community when it matters most.\"

+ PERDUE FOODS NAMES ANDY URBAN CHIEF COMMERCIAL OFFICER TO LEAD GROWTH

Perdue Foods announced that Andy Urban will become chief commercial officer, effective 8th April 2026, with responsibility for sales, marketing, customer development, revenue management and innovation to drive disciplined growth and strengthen customer partnerships. Urban brings more than 35 years of leadership in multi-channel consumer packaged goods across retail, food service and international markets. He has been with Perdue since 2009 in senior roles, most recently leading the Transformation Office to streamline operations and improve execution. Kevin McAdams, Perdue Farms president and CEO, described Urban as a proven commercial leader with a deep understanding of the business, customers and culture, noting his track record in driving growth and building high-performing teams. "As CCO, Urban will lead our commercial organization to deliver value for customers and the company," he said. "I'm honored to step into this role at a pivotal time for Perdue," Urban added. As CCO, Urban aims to accelerate customer-centric growth and strengthen collaboration across the organization.

+ PERDUE FARMS DONATES $100,000 TO DELVAL POULTRY SCIENCE CENTER

Delaware Valley University has received a $100,000 gift from Perdue Farms and the Franklin P. and Arthur W. Perdue Foundation to fund a new Poultry Science Center on the university main campus. The gift, announced on 9th February 2026, aligns with Perdue Delivering Hope To Our Neighbors initiative that supports community well being and agricultural education.The grant was presented to DelVal President Dr. Benjamin E. Rusiloski and Dean of Agriculture and Environmental Science Dr. Broc Sandelin during a ceremony on Wednesday. Sandelin noted that the poultry industry is expanding and that the center will provide hands on learning and support the introduction of a dedicated Poultry Science academic program at the university.The Poultry Science Center will include a broiler building, a layer building and a pen research trials building, as well as laboratories, classrooms and demonstration spaces. In recognition of the Perdue ongoing support, a study room in the new center will bear the company name, reflecting its role in advancing poultry science education. Mike Levengood, Chief Animal Care Officer at Perdue, said that investing in education strengthens hands on learning and helps prepare students for careers in the poultry industry.
