urn:contentItem:6JGY-VSX3-S55N-03T0-00000-00CARGILL INCORPORATED - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisSTOCKCARGILL INCORPORATED - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: CARGILL

PermID4296555324Websitehttp://www.cargill.comIndustryAgribusiness & Food ProcessingAddressPO Box 9300 Minneapolis, Minnesota 55440-9300 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

+ CARGILL [3 News That Matters]

+ RIDDHI SIDDHI GLUCO BIOLS LTD EXPANDS OPERATIONS WITH CARGILL ASSET ACQUISITION

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd. (RSGBL) has successfully completed the acquisition of a corn milling plant and related assets in Davangere, Karnataka from Cargill. This strategic transaction was finalized on 28th April 2026, in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement signed earlier. The acquisition includes an integrated manufacturing facility, encompassing land, production infrastructure, warehouses, and corn silos, thereby enhancing RSGBL's operational footprint in southern India.The Davangere facility, operational since 2016, boasts an annual processing capacity of 300,000 metric tons. This significant acquisition marks a pivotal step in RSGBL's growth journey, expanding its capacity to cater to the food, pharmaceutical, and industrial sectors while reaffirming its commitment to sustainable and efficient manufacturing practices. Mr. Siddharth Chowdhary, Executive Director of RSGBL, expressed optimism regarding the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals."The strategic location of the Davangere facility will enhance RSGBL's ability to serve both domestic and export markets efficiently. The company is also focused on sustainable operations at the facility, including increased utilization of renewable energy sources, reflecting its commitment to operational excellence. This acquisition will enable RSGBL to create long-term value for stakeholders while setting new benchmarks within the industry.Cargill India has been present in India since 1987 and operates across various sectors, including refined oils, food ingredients, and industrial specialties. Mr. Dheeraj Talreja, Vice President & Managing Director of Food South Asia at Cargill, expressed confidence in RSGBL's capability, stating, "We wish RSGBL success and are confident that this asset will support their growth as they expand in the starch industry."With over three decades of experience in starch and derivatives, Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd's acquisition of Cargill's assets further solidifies its position in India's starch industry. The seamless integration of this large-scale asset showcases RSGBL’s ability to identify and capitalize on high-value opportunities, reinforcing its reputation as a resilient player in the sector.

+ CARGILL'S US$2.25M COCOA INVESTMENT AIMS TO TRANSFORM INDONESIAN FARMING

Cargill has announced an investment of approximately Rp38.8 billion (US$2.25M) to support over 7,800 cocoa farmers in Indonesia through the Rainforest Alliance certification program. This move, revealed on the 26th of April 2026, underscores Cargill's ongoing commitment to sustainable sourcing and farmer empowerment in the cocoa industry.The premium payments include roughly Rp35 billion (US$2.02M) directed towards farmers in Central Sulawesi and Rp3.8 billion allocated to those in East Nusa Tenggara. These payments, which supplement farmers' income from cocoa bean sales, are contingent upon adherence to globally recognized sustainability standards, reflecting Cargill's dedication to ethical and responsible sourcing practices.Cargill’s initiative is part of a more extensive cocoa sourcing program designed to enhance productivity, ensure quality, and improve market access for farmers. Brook Chang, Director of Sustainability for Food APAC at Cargill, emphasized the importance of supporting farmers in building a resilient supply chain. He stated, "By investing in training, certification, and premium payments linked to verified sustainability standards, we are helping farmers improve income and productivity while strengthening traceability and supply reliability for our customers and partners."In Central Sulawesi, over 5,600 farmers are engaged in the program, including more than 600 women farmers. From May 2023 to February 2026, approximately 16,000 metric tonnes of Rainforest Alliance-certified cocoa beans were sourced from regions such as Parigi Moutong, Poso, and North Morowali. Meanwhile, in East Nusa Tenggara, about 2,200 farmers contributed close to 2,000 metric tonnes of certified cocoa beans from Ende, Sikka, EastManggarai, and East Flores regencies during the same timeframe.The certified cocoa sourced from these farmers integrates into Cargill's global supply chain, benefiting a variety of food and beverage manufacturers. The Rainforest Alliance certification framework promotes transparency through detailed record-keeping and traceability systems, enabling farmers to meet international market standards. Lukmansyah, Team Manager for Cocoa at Rainforest Alliance Indonesia, noted that certification offers farmers a structured approach to agricultural practices and traceability, thereby enhancing the overall cocoa production process.

+ CARGILL'S REGINA CANOLA FACILITY SETS NEW STANDARDS FOR DOMESTIC PROCESSING

Cargill has officially opened its new canola processing facility in Regina, Saskatchewan, significantly enhancing the local agricultural landscape. This development, announced on 22nd April 2026 at 16:36:58.001272, aims to bolster market access for western Canadian farmers while addressing the surging global demand for food and renewable fuel alternatives.With an impressive capacity to process 1 million metric tons of canola annually, Cargill's Regina facility is poised to increase local demand and facilitate the domestic processing of canola into high-value products. These include oil for both food consumption and renewable fuels, as well as high-protein meal for animal feed. The facility is strategically located to serve growers across Saskatchewan and western Manitoba.Jeff Vassart, president of Cargill Canada, emphasized the importance of the new facility, stating, "This facility strengthens our ability to connect Canadian farmers to growing global demand for food and renewable fuels." He underscored that expanding processing capacity in Saskatchewan creates more opportunities for farmers and helps Canada maintain its competitive edge in rapidly evolving global markets.As the demand for low-carbon energy solutions rises, canola is increasingly recognized as a vital renewable feedstock for biofuels. Cargill's investment in the Regina facility will not only meet this demand but also reduce the reliance on exporting raw canola seed, thereby enabling more value creation within Canada and improving supply chain efficiency for farmers and customers alike.Situated at Saskatchewan's Global Transportation Hub, Cargill's facility offers efficient rail access to both domestic and export markets. The site is expected to create over 100 jobs in the region, contributing to economic growth across transportation, logistics, and service sectors in Regina and its surrounding communities. By continuing to invest in Canada's agriculture sector, Cargill aims to strengthen supply chains and support local farmers, with existing processing facilities already operational in Camrose, Alberta, and Clavet, Saskatchewan.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ INGREDION [3 News That Matters]

+ INGREDION TO UNVEIL INNOVATIVE PROTEIN AND SUGAR SOLUTIONS AT VITAFOODS

Manchester, UK – Ingredion Incorporated will showcase its latest advances in protein fortification, texture enhancement, and sugar reduction at Vitafoods Europe 2026 in Barcelona, helping brands meet EMEA consumers’ rising expectations for great taste, appealing texture, and ingredient lists that support clean-label appeal. The date of this announcement is 23rd April 2026.As demand increases for simpler, more recognisable components, Ingredion will demonstrate how its integrated ingredient portfolio assists brands in delivering these preferences while enhancing positive nutrition characteristics, such as protein enrichment and reduced sugar, which consumers increasingly prioritise. The showcase will feature innovative products designed to elevate the overall consumer experience.Visitors to Ingredion's stand will have the opportunity to explore VITESSENCE® Pea 100 HD, a cutting-edge pea protein ideal for cold-pressed applications. The product maintains softness throughout its shelf life while enhancing texture and providing effective protein fortification. VITESSENCE® Pea 100 HD also demonstrates versatility in bakery applications, enabling protein enrichment without compromising quality or taste.According to Ingredion's insights, texture plays a crucial role in consumer satisfaction, with many consumers likely to recommend products based solely on their texture. This trend highlights the importance for brands to elevate texture to secure consumer preference and loyalty in a discerning marketplace. Additionally, sugar reduction continues to be a significant focus in product development across EMEA, with many consumers prioritising sugar-reduction claims when selecting packaged foods and beverages.Beyond the innovations presented at Vitafoods, Ingredion continues to advance its strategic partnership with Lantm nnen, which was first announced in 2024. This collaboration underscores Ingredion's commitment to European-sourced, plant-based protein solutions, enhancing regional sourcing capabilities and supporting the development of sustainable protein ingredients for customers in Europe. To discover more about Ingredion’s ingredient solutions, visit stand number #3D252 at Vitafoods Europe in Barcelona, Spain.

+ BARCLAYS HIGHLIGHTS INGREDION'S STRATEGIC SHIFT TOWARDS AI SOLUTIONS

Barclays has released a report analyzing the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) across the agribusiness sector in the Americas, noting that Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) is particularly well-positioned to benefit in the near term. The report, published on 22nd April 2026, highlights how Ingredion is transitioning its business model from a commodity-focused approach to a solutions-oriented strategy.The investment bank asserts that Ingredion can leverage its extensive library of ingredients and formulations to meet unmet customer needs effectively. This strategic pivot is expected to enable the company to capitalize on emerging AI technologies more rapidly than its competitors. Barclays emphasized that companies like Ingredion that possess strong balance sheets are better positioned to monetize AI opportunities in the current environment.Looking towards the medium term, Barclays identified seeds and crop chemicals as sectors likely to see significant AI-driven advancements. These developments are anticipated to lead to faster product development cycles, enhanced pricing power, and improved structural efficiencies. While Ingredion and Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) are highlighted as frontrunners in AI adoption, other companies in the sector, such as FMC (NYSE:FMC), may face delays due to internal restructuring challenges.In the realm of protein processing, Barclays noted that AI could enable processors to extract greater value from livestock through automation and yield improvements. This could also result in fewer product recalls, further enhancing profitability in the sector. Overall, the bank suggests that AI will reinforce market leaders by driving innovation, reducing costs, and improving capital efficiency across the agriculture landscape.Barclays' analysis underscores the critical role that AI is expected to play in shaping the future of agribusiness. As companies like Ingredion transition to more innovative models, they are likely to gain a competitive edge in this evolving market. Investors will be closely watching how these developments unfold, particularly in light of the significant potential for AI to transform agricultural practices.

+ INGREDION PARTNERS WITH CIBO TO ENHANCE SUSTAINABLE SOURCING

Ingredion Incorporated, a leading global ingredient solutions provider, has announced a strategic partnership with CIBO Technologies, a prominent independent data and analytics platform for agriculture. This collaboration aims to advance regenerative agriculture across supply chains, marking a significant step in Ingredion’s commitment to sustainability. The announcement was made on 1st April 2026.CIBO's partnership with Ingredion is noteworthy as it represents the company's fourth strategic collaboration in 2026, reflecting its rapid growth in the climate-tech sector within agriculture and food industries. Ingredion is recognized for its sustainability efforts, having sourced 97% of its Tier 1 priority crops responsibly. The partnership aims to enhance the scale and effectiveness of Ingredion’s sustainability programs, which are essential for improving agricultural supply chain resilience.Under this partnership, Ingredion plans to utilize CIBO's advanced platform to increase farmer participation in its sustainability initiatives across Iowa and Illinois. CIBO's Trusted Advisor Network will facilitate the enrollment of farmers into the program, offering crucial technical and agronomic support as they adopt new regenerative practices. This collaboration is designed to streamline enrollment processes and enhance program management, thereby engaging a broader base of farmers.To further its goal of sustainably sourcing 100% of Tier 1 and 2 priority crops by 2030, Ingredion will rely on data provided by farmers to assess on-farm sustainability. CIBO will assist by providing essential program and storage reporting data, utilizing its computer vision and remote sensing capabilities. This technology will enable Ingredion to monitor practice adoption rates and meet compliance requirements efficiently, ensuring a consistent sustainability measurement across all participating farms.Brian Nash, Vice President of Corporate Sustainability at Ingredion, emphasized the importance of this partnership in demonstrating how responsible sourcing can yield long-term benefits for growers, partners, and the environment. CIBO’s capabilities will empower Ingredion to scale its responsible sourcing initiatives effectively, reinforcing its commitment to building climate-resilient supply chains through data-driven insights and robust support for farmers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KOCH [3 News That Matters]

+ KOCH INTRODUCES CENTURO A-PRO: A GAME-CHANGER FOR NITROGEN STABILIZATION

Koch Agronomic Services has unveiled its latest innovation, Centuro A-PRO, aimed at enhancing the efficiency of nitrogen stabilization in agriculture. This new product is designed to stabilize anhydrous ammonia and UAN, ensuring that nitrogen remains in the ammonium form for a longer duration. This announcement was made on the 28th of April 2026, at a time when farmers are seeking more efficient ways to manage their nitrogen inputs.According to Cody Hornaday, technical agronomist at Koch Agronomic Services, the development of Centuro A-PRO was driven by customer feedback that highlighted the need for a more concentrated formula. "Koch is big on customer voice," he stated, emphasizing that the feedback led to a formulation with a higher concentration of active ingredients, resulting in a significant reduction in the required use rate. The new Centuro A-PRO requires only 1.61 gallons per ton of anhydrous ammonia, a stark decrease from the 5 gallons needed for the original Centuro formulation, representing a 67% reduction in product volume handled.The advantages of Centuro A-PRO extend beyond mere efficiency. Retailers will benefit from reduced storage needs and improved inventory management due to the lower volume of product. Farmers, on the other hand, will experience quicker turnaround times when filling tanks, allowing them to apply nitrogen more effectively during critical application windows. Hornaday remarked, "It’s a win-win for retailers and growers alike," highlighting the mutual benefits of this innovation.Early trials conducted by Koch Agronomic Services in corn crops have demonstrated promising results, showing an increase of up to 18 bushels per acre compared to untreated anhydrous ammonia applications at a nitrogen application rate of 180 pounds per acre. The company anticipates a full product launch in time for the fall 2026 application season, reinforcing its commitment to providing effective nitrogen management solutions.As nitrogen stabilizers become an increasingly important tool for farmers aiming to maximize their nitrogen efficiency, Koch Agronomic Services continues to innovate in this space. Hornaday stated, "We want to ensure that Mother Nature doesn’t take away any of that through volatilization or denitrification or leaching," emphasizing the financial implications of nitrogen management for growers operating on tight margins. This ongoing product development reflects the company's understanding of the challenges faced by farmers and its dedication to supporting their agricultural practices.

+ KOCH FERTILIZER ACHIEVES ENERGY STAR CERTIFICATIONS ACROSS FIVE PLANTS

Koch Fertilizer has made headlines by securing ENERGY STAR certifications for five of its plants, a recognition awarded for superior energy performance by the US Environmental Protection Agency and Natural Resources Canada. This achievement, announced on 23rd April 2026, underscores Koch Fertilizer's dedication to environmental stewardship and energy efficiency.The facilities located in Enid, Oklahoma; Wever, Iowa; Fort Dodge, Iowa; Beatrice, Nebraska; and Brandon, Manitoba, have collectively earned a total of 25 ENERGY STAR certifications since 2019, showcasing their ongoing commitment to energy management. Each plant has demonstrated its ability to operate within the top 25% for energy efficiency, setting a benchmark in the fertilizer industry.Koch Fertilizer's approach to environmental stewardship emphasizes responsible resource management and continuous operational improvement. Jason Stowell, Plant Manager at the Beatrice facility, highlighted that this recognition is a testament to the teamwork and dedication of the people behind Koch Fertilizer. "Achieving this level of performance requires strong site ownership, disciplined execution, and a culture that values both operational excellence and environmental stewardship," Stowell noted.Ev Shishkin, the plant manager in Wever, echoed this sentiment, stating that ENERGY STAR certification goes beyond merely meeting benchmarks. It reflects a serious commitment to improving efficiency year over year and operating with discipline. "We focus on reducing waste, improving reliability, and investing in upgrades that help us produce essential products while being mindful of our footprint," Shishkin explained.These certifications serve as a clear indicator of Koch Fertilizer's focus on meaningful results, empowering its workforce to strive for reduced resource consumption and waste. The ENERGY STAR program recognizes facilities that meet stringent energy performance standards, and Koch Fertilizer's accomplishments demonstrate its unwavering resolve to integrate sustainability into its operations.

+ CPH2 EXPLORES HYDROGEN LICENSING AGREEMENT WITH KOCH MODULAR

CPH2 (AIM:CPH2), the developer of the innovative Membrane-Free Electrolyser (MFE) technology for producing high-purity hydrogen and medical-grade oxygen, has announced a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Koch Modular Process Systems LLC. This strategic partnership aims to explore the feasibility of manufacturing and licensing CPH2's proprietary hydrogen technology across North America. The announcement was made on 14th April 2026.The MoU, effective for an initial period of 24 months, sets the groundwork for both parties to assess the technical, commercial, and operational aspects of the proposed collaboration. This includes evaluating supply chain logistics and localization requirements as they work towards a legally binding agreement for up to 100 megawatts (MW) of CPH2's modular hydrogen technology. The target markets for this technology include Mexico, the USA, and Canada.Jon Duffy, Chief Executive Officer of CPH2, expressed optimism regarding the partnership, indicating that discussions will encompass joint supply chain assessments, feasibility studies, and potential manufacturing proposals. He believes that Koch Modular's expertise in process and project delivery positions them as an ideal partner for licensing CPH2's technology throughout North America. Further updates on the progression towards a binding agreement are expected in the near future.Brian Loftus, Vice President of Koch Modular, also highlighted the significance of this collaboration, noting the unique advantages of CPH2's patented MFE technology. He anticipates that the partnership will leverage Koch Modular's design and manufacturing capabilities to effectively penetrate the North American hydrogen market.CPH2 serves as the holding company for Clean Power Hydrogen Group Limited, which has accumulated a decade of research and development experience, leading to several global patents in advanced hydrogen and oxygen production technologies. The group aims to achieve the lowest lifetime Levelised Cost of Hydrogen (LCoH) in the market, targeting decentralized applications in sectors such as wastewater treatment, curtailed wind power capture, data center backup, medical and life sciences, and heavy-duty mobility.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDEL Z INTERNATIONAL'S 'SNACK NA SWAK' INITIATIVE AIMS TO TRANSFORM FILIPINO SNACKING

Mondel z International has launched its 'Snack na Swak' initiative, aiming to reshape snacking habits among Filipinos. As daily life becomes increasingly fast-paced and demanding, the campaign invites consumers to slow down, tune in, and make more intentional choices — transforming what is often a mindless habit into a mindful act of self-care. This initiative was officially announced on 27th April 2026.At the heart of the 'Snack na Swak' campaign is the recognition that snacking is deeply embedded in Filipino culture. Whether it’s a quick merienda between meetings or a shared moment with family, these small pauses carry significant emotional weight. By promoting mindful snacking, Mondel z International aims to position this practice not as a restriction but as an opportunity for awareness regarding portions, nutrition, and the overall snacking experience.The initiative emphasizes education and accessibility, encouraging consumers to better understand nutrition labels and manage portion sizes within a recommended 250-calorie range, as advised by local health authorities. Caitlin Nina Punzalan, Corporate and Government Affairs Lead at Mondel z International, highlighted the campaign's inspiration, noting that snacks are often linked to concerns around obesity and diabetes, and emphasized the importance of a balanced diet that includes indulgences when consumed mindfully.An interactive session held at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist-dietitian Jo Sebastian, who guided participants through activities like the 'Portion Puzzle.' She stressed that mindful snacking begins with understanding nutrition labels, allowing individuals to make confident choices about what they eat. This message was echoed by Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, who pointed out the need for clearer labeling and portion-controlled options to aid in healthier decision-making.Through 'Snack na Swak,' Mondel z International not only aims to empower consumers to make informed choices but also addresses broader public health concerns. The company recognizes its role in influencing consumption habits and has introduced portion-controlled products to make healthier options more accessible. By reframing familiar snacks like Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle, Mondel z International is promoting a conscious approach to snacking that can be both enjoyable and health-conscious.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VP - MARKETING

Mondelez International has promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing. On 22nd April 2026, the company announced that Viswanathan, who has been with Mondelez for nearly eight years, previously served as Senior Director of Marketing.In his new role, Viswanathan will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, which includes brand equity, innovation, and activation. He will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with both India and Global Technology Leadership.Viswanathan began his career as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business at Cadbury India in 2008. He played a crucial role in evolving the business strategy at Cadbury Kraft India, where he served as VP Marketing – Chocolates from 2011 to 2013.Joining Mondelez International in 2014, he relocated to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, leading chocolate innovation initiatives. His trajectory continued upward when he returned to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA, eventually becoming Senior Director of Marketing in 2020.The promotion comes at a time when Mondelez International is focusing on enhancing its market position in India, particularly in the fast-evolving consumer landscape emphasizing digital engagement and innovative marketing strategies.

+ BOFA UPGRADES MONDELEZ (MDLZ) AMID POSITIVE MANAGEMENT INSIGHTS

Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) has recently caught the attention of investors as BofA analyst Peter Galbo raised the firm's price recommendation on the snack giant to $65 from $62. This upgrade comes after positive feedback from management during meetings held in March, with BofA maintaining a Buy rating on Mondelez shares. The announcement has generated interest, especially given the company's inclusion among the 13 NASDAQ stocks with the highest dividends.On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterated its favorable stance on Mondelez, citing a seemingly positive update regarding ongoing negotiations with European chocolate retailers. These discussions are critical for Mondelez, as they represent potential growth opportunities in key markets. The firm has expressed confidence in Mondelez's prospects within the packaged food and protein sector, an area that is expected to witness steady demand amid evolving consumer preferences.In contrast, UBS has taken a more cautious approach, lowering its price target on Mondelez to $62 from $63 while maintaining a Neutral rating. The firm anticipates that the first quarter results for many companies in the consumer staples sector, including Mondelez, will show some improvement, with organic revenue growth stabilizing. However, UBS raises concerns about inflationary pressures that could affect earnings in the latter half of 2026, which adds an element of uncertainty to the overall market outlook.Mondelez International specializes in producing and selling a diverse range of snack products, including chocolate, biscuits, and baked snacks. Additionally, the company has interests in related categories, such as gum, candy, cheese, grocery items, and powdered beverages. This broad portfolio positions Mondelez strategically to capitalize on changing consumer habits and preferences, which are increasingly leaning towards convenience and indulgent snacking.As investors weigh their options, some analysts suggest that while Mondelez presents a compelling case for investment, certain artificial intelligence stocks may offer greater upside potential with less downside risk. Notably, there is growing interest in stocks that could benefit from geopolitical trends such as onshoring and tariff impacts. Investors are encouraged to conduct thorough research before making any investment decisions, particularly in light of evolving market conditions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TYSON FOODS [3 News That Matters]

+ TYSON FOODS STEPS UP WITH DISASTER RELIEF MEALS FOR FLOOD VICTIMS

Tyson Foods, Inc. is set to provide free, hot meals to community members affected by recent flooding in New London. On 20th April 2026, the company's Meals That Matter disaster relief team will serve meals from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. through Wednesday, April 22, at its facility located at 3620 County Road D, in the lower parking lot.The New London facility, which employs over 400 local residents, will see company volunteers on-site to assist with meal distribution. Tyson Foods has indicated that during such deployments, its Meals That Matter team can serve between 2,000 and more than 10,000 meals daily, depending on the needs of the community.Michelle Mullins, director of food business operations, food loss and waste at Tyson Foods, emphasized the importance of providing meals in times of hardship. 'When communities are facing hardship, we believe one of the most powerful things we can do is show up with a hot meal,' Mullins stated. 'It’s our way of feeding people like family — offering comfort, connection, and hope when it’s needed most.'Since its inception in 2012, the Meals That Matter initiative has been deployed to over 30 natural disaster sites, helping to provide food assistance during hurricanes, floods, and tornadoes. Tyson Foods continues to play a pivotal role in supporting communities affected by natural disasters through this program.As the situation in New London develops, Tyson Foods remains committed to aiding those in need and reinforcing its presence as a community partner dedicated to providing relief during challenging times.

+ TYSON FOODS EXPANDS GAME DAY OFFERINGS WITH NEW CHICKEN NUGGETS

Tyson Foods, Inc. is making a bold move in the frozen foods aisle with the introduction of three new sports-themed chicken nuggets. The company aims to capitalize on the excitement surrounding the summer sports season, which includes high-profile events such as the 2026 FIFA World Cup, MLB games, and the U.S. Open. On 20th April 2026, Tyson announced its latest lineup, featuring nuggets in the shapes of a star, a trophy, and a versatile ball that can represent various sports.Last year, Tyson made a significant impact with its NFL-licensed chicken nuggets, which featured team-specific shapes for fans of franchises like the Denver Broncos and the Dallas Cowboys. This year, the company is broadening its approach, moving beyond just football to cater to a wider array of sports enthusiasts. Tyson's Game Time nuggets are crafted from 100% all-natural white meat chicken, lightly breaded, and fully cooked, making them a convenient option for game-day snacking.Each serving of the new nuggets boasts 20 grams of protein, aligning with the growing consumer demand for high-protein snack options. While Tyson Foods is leading the savory snack segment, other brands are also stepping up their game. Nestl Toll House has recently launched soccer ball-shaped chocolate morsels, while Pillsbury and Chips Ahoy! are rolling out limited-edition cookies to celebrate the World Cup. Tyson, however, remains firmly positioned to dominate the savory side of the market.Available now at select retailers nationwide, the Game Time nuggets are poised to become a staple for those gathering to watch summer sports. In addition to these new offerings, Tyson Foods continues to innovate with products like its Restaurant Style Waffle Flavored Breaded Chicken Breast Bites and Chicken Dip Wedges, ensuring a diverse range of options for consumers.As the summer sports season approaches, Tyson Foods is not just providing a snack but an experience for fans. With its new line of chicken nuggets, the company is set to enhance the game-day atmosphere, making it easier for consumers to show their team spirit while enjoying convenient and delicious food options.

+ TYSON FOODS EXPANDS PARTNERSHIP WITH ARKANSAS RAZORBACKS IN MAJOR ATHLETICS SPONSORSHIP

Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE: TSN) has announced a landmark partnership with the University of Arkansas Department of Intercollegiate Athletics, significantly expanding its relationship with the Razorbacks. + GENERAL MILLS MOVES TO STRENGTHEN CAPITAL STRUCTURE WITH €1.7 BILLION NOTE OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) has announced the issuance of €1.7 billion in junior subordinated notes, designed to bolster its capital structure and provide financial flexibility. The offering, made public on 14th April 2026, includes €1 billion of 4.750% Series A notes and €700 million of 5.250% Series B notes, both of which feature fixed-to-fixed reset rate structures.The Series A notes will maintain a fixed interest rate of 4.750% until 16th July 2031, after which the rate will reset based on the Five-Year Swap Rate plus an initial margin of 202.4 basis points. The Series B notes will carry a 5.250% rate until 16th July 2034, with subsequent resets tied to the same swap rate plus an initial margin of 239.0 basis points. This structured approach aims to provide a predictable income stream for investors while offering General Mills a flexible financing option.Both series of notes come with step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin beginning five years after their respective first reset dates, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset dates. The interest payments for these notes are scheduled to occur annually on 16th July, commencing in 2026, which aligns with the company's cash flow management strategy.General Mills retains the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years during any deferral period, with any deferred payments accruing additional interest at the applicable rate. This feature provides the company with a buffer in case of unforeseen financial pressures, allowing it to maintain operational stability.Additionally, the company has the option to redeem the notes at any time under the terms outlined in the prospectus supplement. The classification of these notes as junior subordinated debt indicates a lower claim on assets in the event of liquidation, but it also suggests a strategic move by General Mills to leverage its capital structure for future growth initiatives.

Bunge Global SA (NYSE:BG) has garnered renewed attention from analysts following a positive outlook from Bank of America (BofA), which has raised its price target for the agribusiness giant to $130 from $125. On 26th March 2026, BofA reiterated its Buy rating on the shares, citing the company's recent Investor Day as a key factor in its decision. The firm noted that the event revealed higher-than-expected midcycle earnings per share (EPS) projections of $13 to $15, along with an increased synergy target and discussions about additional margin-enhancing opportunities. BofA characterized the Investor Day as a significant hurdle that investors had previously feared might yield a disappointing outlook, but with this concern now alleviated, the firm believes the stock has the potential to gain further traction.In addition to BofA's bullish stance, Bunge Global SA also received an upgrade from BMO Capital on the same day. BMO raised its price target to $135 from $130, maintaining an Outperform rating for the stock. The firm expressed increased confidence in Bunge's multi-year earnings trajectory, especially after the management laid out a roadmap to achieve the $15 mid-cycle EPS target by 2030 during the Investor Day presentation. This dual endorsement from prominent financial institutions underscores a growing optimism about Bunge Global SA's future performance in the agribusiness sector.Bunge Global SA operates as a leading global agribusiness and food company, offering a diverse array of products, including canned and frozen vegetables, spices, and various food items marketed under well-known brands such as Green Giant and Mrs. Dash. The company's expansive portfolio positions it favorably in the marketplace, allowing it to capitalize on evolving consumer preferences and demand for food products. Analysts believe that Bunge's ability to leverage its combined platform and technology will enhance its operational efficiencies and profitability in the coming years.While Bunge Global SA's stock is seen as a solid investment opportunity, some analysts caution that there are other sectors, particularly in artificial intelligence, that may offer higher potential returns in a shorter timeframe. Investors are encouraged to evaluate their portfolios carefully, and while Bunge presents a defensive option, the allure of AI stocks with significant growth potential cannot be overlooked. As the market continues to evolve, the comparative analysis between traditional agribusiness stocks and emerging tech stocks will remain a critical consideration for investors.In conclusion, Bunge Global SA is positioned for growth as it navigates through positive analyst sentiments and strategic initiatives. The recent upgrades from BofA and BMO Capital reflect a broader confidence in the company's ability to enhance its earnings trajectory and deliver value to shareholders. urn:contentItem:6JH6-TKT3-S1N0-G0DH-00000-00CARGILL INCORPORATED - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisSTOCKCARGILL INCORPORATED - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: CARGILL

PermID4296555324Websitehttp://www.cargill.comIndustryAgribusiness & Food ProcessingAddressPO Box 9300 Minneapolis, Minnesota 55440-9300 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

+ CARGILL [3 News That Matters]

+ RSGBL COMPLETES CARGILL ASSET ACQUISITION IN DAVANGERE TO BROADEN SOUTHERN INDIAN FOOTPRINT

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd (RSGBL) has completed the acquisition of a corn milling plant and related assets in Davangere, Karnataka from Cargill. The deal, finalized on 28th April 2026 under an Asset Purchase Agreement, includes land, production facilities, warehouses and corn silos, expanding RSGBL's southern India footprint.The Davangere plant, in operation since 2016, processes about 300,000 metric tons annually. The acquisition broadens RSGBL's capacity to serve the food, pharmaceutical and industrial sectors while underscoring the company's commitment to sustainable manufacturing practices, including greater use of renewable energy sources.Executive Director Siddharth Chowdhary said, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals."

+ CARGILL INVESTS RP38.8 BILLION TO BOOST INDONESIAN COCOA FARMERS VIA RAINFOREST ALLIANCE

Cargill announced an investment of Rp38.8 billion (US$2.25 million) to support more than 7,800 cocoa farmers in Indonesia through the Rainforest Alliance certification program, disclosed on 26th of April 2026. The move underscores Cargill's commitment to sustainable sourcing and farmer empowerment in the cocoa sector.The premium payments total about Rp35 billion (US$2.02 million) to farmers in Central Sulawesi and Rp3.8 billion to those in East Nusa Tenggara. The payments supplement farmers' income from cocoa bean sales and are conditional on meeting globally recognized sustainability standards, reflecting Cargill's focus on ethical and responsible sourcing.In Central Sulawesi, more than 5,600 farmers participate, including over 600 women farmers. From May 2023 to February 2026, roughly 16,000 metric tonnes of Rainforest Alliance-certified cocoa beans were sourced from Parigi Moutong, Poso and North Morowali. In East Nusa Tenggara, about 2,200 farmers contributed close to 2,000 metric tonnes from Ende, Sikka, East Manggarai and East Flores. Brook Chang, Director of Sustainability for Food APAC at Cargill, said that investing in training, certification and premium payments linked to verified sustainability standards helps farmers raise income and productivity while strengthening traceability and supply reliability for customers and partners.

+ CARGILL OPENS REGINA CANOLA PLANT, EXPANDING DOMESTIC PROCESSING CAPACITY

Cargill has opened its new canola processing facility in Regina, Saskatchewan, designed to bolster market access for western Canadian farmers and meet rising global demand for food and renewable fuels. The project was announced on Apr. 22 and features an annual processing capacity of about 1 million metric tons.The plant will process canola into oil for food and renewable fuels and high-protein meal for animal feed, positioning Regina as a key node for western Canadian supply chains with rail access to domestic and export markets from the Global Transportation Hub."This facility strengthens our ability to connect Canadian farmers to growing global demand for food and renewable fuels," said Jeff Vassart, president of Cargill Canada. The expansion, which complements existing facilities in Camrose, Alberta, and Clavet, Saskatchewan, is expected to create more than 100 local jobs and boost value creation within Canada by reducing reliance on exporting raw seed.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ INGREDION [3 News That Matters]

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSTS LATEST PROTEIN AND SUGAR SOLUTIONS AT VITAFOODS EUROPE 2026

Manchester, UK – Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) will showcase its latest advances in protein fortification, texture enhancement, and sugar reduction at Vitafoods Europe 2026 in Barcelona, helping brands meet EMEA consumers' rising expectations for great taste, appealing texture, and ingredient lists that support clean-label appeal. The date of this announcement is 23rd April 2026.The integrated portfolio aims to deliver simpler, more recognisable components while enhancing nutrition characteristics such as protein enrichment and reduced sugar, trends that consumers increasingly prioritise. Visitors to Ingredion's stand will explore VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD, a cutting-edge pea protein ideal for cold-pressed applications that maintains softness throughout its shelf life while enhancing texture and providing effective protein fortification. VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD also demonstrates versatility in bakery applications, enabling protein enrichment without compromising quality or taste.Beyond Vitafoods, Ingredion continues to advance its strategic partnership with Lantmannen, first announced in 2024, underscoring a commitment to European-sourced, plant-based protein solutions and stronger regional sourcing for customers in Europe. To discover more about Ingredion's ingredient solutions, visit stand number #3D252 at Vitafoods Europe in Barcelona, Spain.

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSITIONED TO BENEFIT FROM AI-DRIVEN PIVOT

Barclays Reports On AI's Impact Across the Agribusiness Sector in the Americas, noting Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) is well positioned to benefit in the near term. The bank says Ingredion is shifting from a commodity-focused model to a solutions-oriented approach, leveraging its library of ingredients and formulations to meet unmet customer needs. The pivot could enable faster monetization of AI opportunities, particularly for companies with solid balance sheets, Barclays writes in the report published on 22nd April 2026. In the medium term, Barclays identifies seeds and crop chemicals as sectors likely to see AI-driven advancements, with implications for faster product development cycles, improved pricing power, and structural efficiency. Ingredion and Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) are highlighted as frontrunners in AI adoption, while FMC (NYSE:FMC) may face execution delays given internal restructuring. Barclays also notes potential gains in protein processing from AI-enabled automation and yield improvements, which could reduce recalls and boost profitability. Overall, the bank argues AI will reinforce market leaders by driving innovation, lowering costs, and enhancing capital efficiency across the agriculture landscape. Investors will monitor how Ingredion’s strategy unfolds as AI transforms agricultural practices

+ INGREDION NAMES JASON PAYANT INTERIM CFO AS LEADERSHIP TRANSITION ADVANCES

Ingredion Incorporated has announced the appointment of Jason Payant as Interim Chief Financial Officer, effective April 1, following the resignation of James D. Gray as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, which will take effect on 31st March 2026. Payant, 55, has been with Ingredion since 2012 and most recently served as Vice President, Finance, Global Texture & Healthful Solutions since November 2025; he will continue to fulfill that role alongside his new CFO responsibilities.The company confirmed there are no arrangements between Payant and other persons regarding the appointment, and no transactions involving Payant or his immediate family have any material interest. Payant will receive a monthly cash compensation of $25,000 in addition to his current compensation and benefits. In other business, Ingredion declared a quarterly dividend of $0.82 per share payable on 21st April 2026 to stockholders of record as of 1st April 2026, and announced a research and development collaboration with Shiru aimed at using Shiru's AI discovery platform to develop functional proteins and create prebiotics from natural sources for nutritional products.Ingredion also named Univar Solutions LLC as the exclusive distributor for Ingredion Pharma Solutions in the United States and Canada, covering a range of pharmaceutical starches and excipients. The company also announced governance changes, with Gregory B. Kenny retiring after more than 21 years on the board and Siobhan Talbot joining the board effective 1st April 2026.

+ KOCH [3 News That Matters]

+ CPH2 AND KOCH MODULAR SIGN MOU TO EXPLORE NORTH AMERICAN LICENSING OF MFE HYDROGEN TECH

CPH2 (AIM:CPH2) announced on 14th April 2026 a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding with Koch Modular Process Systems LLC to explore the feasibility of manufacturing and licensing CPH2's Membrane-Free Electrolyser (MFE) technology across North America. The MoU is for an initial period of 24 months and will assess technical, commercial, and operational aspects, including supply-chain localization, with the aim of developing a binding agreement for up to 100 MW of modular hydrogen technology. + STAUT CLINCHES FOURTH GT3 CUP PRO-AM WIN AS KOCH MOTORSPORTS SHINES

Barber Sprint Challenge delivered high-octane action at Barber Motorsports Park over the weekend of Mar. 30, 2026, with 15-year-old Belgian Karel Staut and rising American Ford Koch starring for Koch Motorsports as the Porsche Sprint Challenge North America by Yokohama supported the Children's of Alabama Indy Grand Prix, marking the series return to Barber after a hiatus.Staut, who made his Sprint Challenge debut earlier this month, extended his GT3 Cup Pro-Am lead by taking pole for both races and building a commanding advantage in each event, finishing 15 seconds ahead of the runner-up in Race 1 and six seconds in Race 2.Koch made a strong impression in the Cayman GT4 RS Clubsport class, claiming a flag-to-flag victory in Race 1 after joining the field late and then briefly surrendering the lead in Race 2 before reclaiming it and finishing two seconds clear of his nearest rival. Mondelez International Inc. An interactive session at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist Jo Sebastian guiding participants through the Portion Puzzle, stressing the importance of nutrition labels for confident choices. Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, called for clearer labeling and more portion-controlled options to aid healthier decisions. Mondelez International also notes its role in shaping consumption patterns and has introduced portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier options, reframing familiar snacks such as Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VICE PRESIDENT – MARKETING

On 22nd April 2026, Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing, with Viswanathan having spent nearly eight years at Mondelez and most recently serving as Senior Director of Marketing. In his new role, he will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, including brand equity, innovation, and activation, and will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with India and Global Technology Leadership. + WILMAR INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (SGX:F34) NAVIGATES INDIRECT IMPACTS FROM MIDDLE EAST CONFLICT MAINTAINS ROBUST STRUCTURE

Wilmar International Limited (SGX:F34) Posts Indirect Impacts From Middle East Conflict, Yet Maintains Robust Corporate Structure. The announcement dated 21st April 2026 shows Wilmar anticipates indirect operational effects from the Middle East conflict, while saying its corporate structure remains robust to navigate market fluctuations. The group reports direct exposure to the region is limited, with regional contributions representing a single-digit percentage of annual revenue, and identifies indirect consequences including higher costs for insurance, freight, and fertilizers, as well as volatility in global commodity prices.The firm notes it has managed the shocks in recent years and does not expect to overhaul its framework. It highlights Asia and Africa as core regions for investments and operations, and says long-term capital allocation decisions have not changed given the uncertainty over how long the conflict will last. The company plans to address investors at its annual general meeting on Apr. 23, following inquiries from the Securities Investors Association (Singapore).On legal matters, Wilmar reiterates its commitment to internal discipline and legal compliance. The group cites a 2025 Chinese court ruling involving contract fraud at a local unit and separate charges against a general manager in Indonesia, stating that strong disciplinary action will apply to any staff found to breach instructions. Shares closed at S$3.84 on Friday, down 0.3% before the information was made public.

+ WILMAR REPORTS $150 MILLION LOSS LINKED TO UNITY FOODS INVESTMENT

+ GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) POSTS €1.7 BILLION JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) Posts €1.7 Billion in Junior Subordinated Notes Offering to Strengthen Capital Flexibility. The €1.7 billion issue comprises €1.0 billion of Series A notes at 4.750% fixed until 16 July 2031, after which the rate resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points, and €700 million of Series B notes at 5.250% fixed until 16 July 2034, with resets tied to the same swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series use fixed-to-fixed rate structures designed to provide a predictable income stream for investors while giving General Mills flexibility in financing plans. The offering was made public on 14th April 2026. Interest payments are scheduled annually on 16th July, beginning in 2026. The notes include step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin five years after the first reset date, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset date. General Mills also reserves the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years, with any deferred interest accruing at the applicable rate. The company may redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. Classified as junior subordinated debt, the notes carry a lower claim on assets in liquidation but provide a strategic means to strengthen capital structure and support growth initiatives.

urn:contentItem:6JH1-0XR3-RRT2-X0X2-00000-00CARGILL INCORPORATED - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisSTOCKCARGILL INCORPORATED - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CARGILL

PermID4296555324Websitehttp://www.cargill.comIndustryAgribusiness & Food ProcessingAddressPO Box 9300 Minneapolis, Minnesota 55440-9300 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

+ CARGILL [3 News That Matters]

+ RSGBL COMPLETES CARGILL ASSET ACQUISITION IN DAVANGERE TO BROADEN SOUTHERN INDIAN FOOTPRINT

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd (RSGBL) has completed the acquisition of a corn milling plant and related assets in Davangere, Karnataka from Cargill. The deal, finalized on 28th April 2026 under an Asset Purchase Agreement, includes land, production facilities, warehouses and corn silos, expanding RSGBL's southern India footprint.The Davangere plant, in operation since 2016, processes about 300,000 metric tons annually. The acquisition broadens RSGBL's capacity to serve the food, pharmaceutical and industrial sectors while underscoring the company's commitment to sustainable manufacturing practices, including greater use of renewable energy sources.Executive Director Siddharth Chowdhary said, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals."

+ CARGILL INVESTS RP38.8 BILLION TO BOOST INDONESIAN COCOA FARMERS VIA RAINFOREST ALLIANCE

Cargill announced an investment of Rp38.8 billion (US$2.25 million) to support more than 7,800 cocoa farmers in Indonesia through the Rainforest Alliance certification program, disclosed on 26th of April 2026. The move underscores Cargill's commitment to sustainable sourcing and farmer empowerment in the cocoa sector.The premium payments total about Rp35 billion (US$2.02 million) to farmers in Central Sulawesi and Rp3.8 billion to those in East Nusa Tenggara. The payments supplement farmers' income from cocoa bean sales and are conditional on meeting globally recognized sustainability standards, reflecting Cargill's focus on ethical and responsible sourcing.In Central Sulawesi, more than 5,600 farmers participate, including over 600 women farmers. From May 2023 to February 2026, roughly 16,000 metric tonnes of Rainforest Alliance-certified cocoa beans were sourced from Parigi Moutong, Poso and North Morowali. In East Nusa Tenggara, about 2,200 farmers contributed close to 2,000 metric tonnes from Ende, Sikka, East Manggarai and East Flores. Brook Chang, Director of Sustainability for Food APAC at Cargill, said that investing in training, certification and premium payments linked to verified sustainability standards helps farmers raise income and productivity while strengthening traceability and supply reliability for customers and partners.

+ CARGILL SECURES EDISON BRONZE FOR CARVE AI SYSTEM IN MEAT PROCESSING

WAYZATA, MINN. — Cargill Inc. recently announced one of its latest innovations received recognition from the 2026 Edison Award in the Consumer Food Innovations and Sustainable Industry Solutions category. On the 22nd of April 2026, the company accepted a Bronze award for its CarVe computer vision system, which incorporates artificial intelligence (AI) to measure red meat yield in real time.The CarVe system not only enhances the accuracy of beef-cutting but also provides video footage that helps reduce food waste and improve efficiency across Cargill's protein operations. Florian Schattenmann, chief technology officer at Cargill, emphasized the company's commitment to innovation, stating, "By combining deep food expertise with innovation and new technologies, we are reimagining how food is made at scale. This recognition reflects the talent of our teams and the meaningful progress we’re delivering with our partners and customers." The Edison Awards are known for acknowledging innovative products and business leaders, with winners selected based on their concept, value, and impact by a panel of peers from various sectors including industry, academia, and innovation. Earlier this year, Cargill was also honored with a 2026 BIG AI Award, which highlighted its AI technology applications across operations, with CarVe noted as a key example in protein processing. Cargill has already implemented the CarVe system at its Fort Morgan, Colorado plant, and plans to roll it out at its High River facility in Ontario, Canada. This initiative is part of Cargill's broader strategy to integrate advanced technologies in its operations, aiming to enhance productivity while promoting sustainability in the food supply chain.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ INGREDION [3 News That Matters]

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSTS LATEST PROTEIN AND SUGAR SOLUTIONS AT VITAFOODS EUROPE 2026

Manchester, UK – Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) will showcase its latest advances in protein fortification, texture enhancement, and sugar reduction at Vitafoods Europe 2026 in Barcelona, helping brands meet EMEA consumers' rising expectations for great taste, appealing texture, and ingredient lists that support clean-label appeal. The date of this announcement is 23rd April 2026.The integrated portfolio aims to deliver simpler, more recognisable components while enhancing nutrition characteristics such as protein enrichment and reduced sugar, trends that consumers increasingly prioritise. Visitors to Ingredion's stand will explore VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD, a cutting-edge pea protein ideal for cold-pressed applications that maintains softness throughout its shelf life while enhancing texture and providing effective protein fortification. VITESSENCE\u00AE Pea 100 HD also demonstrates versatility in bakery applications, enabling protein enrichment without compromising quality or taste.Beyond Vitafoods, Ingredion continues to advance its strategic partnership with Lantmannen, first announced in 2024, underscoring a commitment to European-sourced, plant-based protein solutions and stronger regional sourcing for customers in Europe. To discover more about Ingredion's ingredient solutions, visit stand number #3D252 at Vitafoods Europe in Barcelona, Spain.

+ INGREDION INCORPORATED (NYSE:INGR) POSITIONED TO BENEFIT FROM AI-DRIVEN PIVOT

Barclays Reports On AI's Impact Across the Agribusiness Sector in the Americas, noting Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) is well positioned to benefit in the near term. The bank says Ingredion is shifting from a commodity-focused model to a solutions-oriented approach, leveraging its library of ingredients and formulations to meet unmet customer needs. The pivot could enable faster monetization of AI opportunities, particularly for companies with solid balance sheets, Barclays writes in the report published on 22nd April 2026. In the medium term, Barclays identifies seeds and crop chemicals as sectors likely to see AI-driven advancements, with implications for faster product development cycles, improved pricing power, and structural efficiency. Ingredion and Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) are highlighted as frontrunners in AI adoption, while FMC (NYSE:FMC) may face execution delays given internal restructuring. Barclays also notes potential gains in protein processing from AI-enabled automation and yield improvements, which could reduce recalls and boost profitability. Overall, the bank argues AI will reinforce market leaders by driving innovation, lowering costs, and enhancing capital efficiency across the agriculture landscape. Investors will monitor how Ingredion’s strategy unfolds as AI transforms agricultural practices

+ INGREDION NAMES JASON PAYANT INTERIM CFO AS LEADERSHIP TRANSITION ADVANCES

Ingredion Incorporated has announced the appointment of Jason Payant as Interim Chief Financial Officer, effective April 1, following the resignation of James D. Gray as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, which will take effect on 31st March 2026. Payant, 55, has been with Ingredion since 2012 and most recently served as Vice President, Finance, Global Texture & Healthful Solutions since November 2025; he will continue to fulfill that role alongside his new CFO responsibilities.The company confirmed there are no arrangements between Payant and other persons regarding the appointment, and no transactions involving Payant or his immediate family have any material interest. Payant will receive a monthly cash compensation of $25,000 in addition to his current compensation and benefits. In other business, Ingredion declared a quarterly dividend of $0.82 per share payable on 21st April 2026 to stockholders of record as of 1st April 2026, and announced a research and development collaboration with Shiru aimed at using Shiru's AI discovery platform to develop functional proteins and create prebiotics from natural sources for nutritional products.Ingredion also named Univar Solutions LLC as the exclusive distributor for Ingredion Pharma Solutions in the United States and Canada, covering a range of pharmaceutical starches and excipients. The company also announced governance changes, with Gregory B. Kenny retiring after more than 21 years on the board and Siobhan Talbot joining the board effective 1st April 2026.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KOCH [3 News That Matters]

+ KOCH FERTILIZER SECURES ENERGY STAR CERTIFICATIONS FOR FIVE PLANTS

Koch Fertilizer has earned ENERGY STAR certifications for five plants, a recognition from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and Natural Resources Canada. The certification was announced on 23rd April 2026, underscoring the company's commitment to environmental stewardship and energy efficiency.The five facilities—Enid, Oklahoma; Wever, Iowa; Fort Dodge, Iowa; Beatrice, Nebraska; and Brandon, Manitoba—have collectively earned 25 ENERGY STAR certifications since 2019, signaling sustained progress in energy management. Each site operates in the top 25% for energy efficiency, establishing a benchmark within the fertilizer industry.These certifications reflect Koch Fertilizer's ongoing focus on sustainable operations and responsible resource use across its manufacturing network.

+ CPH2 AND KOCH MODULAR SIGN MOU TO EXPLORE NORTH AMERICAN LICENSING OF MFE HYDROGEN TECH

CPH2 (AIM:CPH2) announced on 14th April 2026 a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding with Koch Modular Process Systems LLC to explore the feasibility of manufacturing and licensing CPH2's Membrane-Free Electrolyser (MFE) technology across North America. The MoU is for an initial period of 24 months and will assess technical, commercial, and operational aspects, including supply-chain localization, with the aim of developing a binding agreement for up to 100 MW of modular hydrogen technology. Target markets are Mexico, the United States, and Canada.CEO Jon Duffy said the collaboration will involve joint supply-chain assessments, feasibility studies, and potential manufacturing proposals, with Koch Modular's process and project delivery expertise positioned to support licensing of CPH2's technology across North America. Koch Modular Vice President Brian Loftus highlighted the distinctive advantages of the patented MFE technology and said the partnership could leverage Koch Modular's design and manufacturing capabilities to help bring the technology to market in North America.

+ STAUT CLINCHES FOURTH GT3 CUP PRO-AM WIN AS KOCH MOTORSPORTS SHINES

Barber Sprint Challenge delivered high-octane action at Barber Motorsports Park over the weekend of Mar. 30, 2026, with 15-year-old Belgian Karel Staut and rising American Ford Koch starring for Koch Motorsports as the Porsche Sprint Challenge North America by Yokohama supported the Children's of Alabama Indy Grand Prix, marking the series return to Barber after a hiatus.Staut, who made his Sprint Challenge debut earlier this month, extended his GT3 Cup Pro-Am lead by taking pole for both races and building a commanding advantage in each event, finishing 15 seconds ahead of the runner-up in Race 1 and six seconds in Race 2.Koch made a strong impression in the Cayman GT4 RS Clubsport class, claiming a flag-to-flag victory in Race 1 after joining the field late and then briefly surrendering the lead in Race 2 before reclaiming it and finishing two seconds clear of his nearest rival. The weekend also featured standout runs in the GT3 Cup Masters from Alain Scalzo and Mark Boden, with Scalzo moving up to the win in Race 1 from fifth and Boden returning to the winner's circle in Race 2 on his birthday. The team now looks ahead to Sonoma Raceway, where action resumes Apr. 10-12.

Mondelez International Inc. An interactive session at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist Jo Sebastian guiding participants through the Portion Puzzle, stressing the importance of nutrition labels for confident choices. Mondelez International also notes its role in shaping consumption patterns and has introduced portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier options, reframing familiar snacks such as Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

The announcement dated 21st April 2026 shows Wilmar anticipates indirect operational effects from the Middle East conflict, while saying its corporate structure remains robust to navigate market fluctuations. The group reports direct exposure to the region is limited, with regional contributions representing a single-digit percentage of annual revenue, and identifies indirect consequences including higher costs for insurance, freight, and fertilizers, as well as volatility in global commodity prices.The firm notes it has managed the shocks in recent years and does not expect to overhaul its framework. It highlights Asia and Africa as core regions for investments and operations, and says long-term capital allocation decisions have not changed given the uncertainty over how long the conflict will last. The group cites a 2025 Chinese court ruling involving contract fraud at a local unit and separate charges against a general manager in Indonesia, stating that strong disciplinary action will apply to any staff found to breach instructions. + GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) POSTS €1.7 BILLION JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) Posts €1.7 Billion in Junior Subordinated Notes Offering to Strengthen Capital Flexibility. The €1.7 billion issue comprises €1.0 billion of Series A notes at 4.750% fixed until 16 July 2031, after which the rate resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points, and €700 million of Series B notes at 5.250% fixed until 16 July 2034, with resets tied to the same swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series use fixed-to-fixed rate structures designed to provide a predictable income stream for investors while giving General Mills flexibility in financing plans. The offering was made public on 14th April 2026. Interest payments are scheduled annually on 16th July, beginning in 2026. The notes include step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin five years after the first reset date, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset date. General Mills also reserves the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years, with any deferred interest accruing at the applicable rate. The company may redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. Classified as junior subordinated debt, the notes carry a lower claim on assets in liquidation but provide a strategic means to strengthen capital structure and support growth initiatives.
