urn:contentItem:6JGH-XDD3-RRRY-T35M-00000-00CARGILL INCORPORATED - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM INGREDION INCORPORATED AND PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisSTOCKCARGILL INCORPORATED - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM INGREDION INCORPORATED AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CARGILL

PermID4296555324Websitehttp://www.cargill.comIndustryAgribusiness & Food ProcessingAddressPO Box 9300 Minneapolis, Minnesota 55440-9300 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

+ CARGILL [3 News That Matters]

+ RIDDHI SIDDHI GLUCO BIOLS LTD EXPANDS OPERATIONS WITH CARGILL ASSET ACQUISITION

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd. (RSGBL) has successfully completed the acquisition of a corn milling plant and related assets in Davangere, Karnataka from Cargill. This strategic transaction was finalized on 28th April 2026, in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement signed earlier. The acquisition includes an integrated manufacturing facility, encompassing land, production infrastructure, warehouses, and corn silos, thereby enhancing RSGBL's operational footprint in southern India.The Davangere facility, operational since 2016, boasts an annual processing capacity of 300,000 metric tons. This significant acquisition marks a pivotal step in RSGBL's growth journey, expanding its capacity to cater to the food, pharmaceutical, and industrial sectors while reaffirming its commitment to sustainable and efficient manufacturing practices. Mr. Siddharth Chowdhary, Executive Director of RSGBL, expressed optimism regarding the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals."The strategic location of the Davangere facility will enhance RSGBL's ability to serve both domestic and export markets efficiently. The company is also focused on sustainable operations at the facility, including increased utilization of renewable energy sources, reflecting its commitment to operational excellence. This acquisition will enable RSGBL to create long-term value for stakeholders while setting new benchmarks within the industry.Cargill India has been present in India since 1987 and operates across various sectors, including refined oils, food ingredients, and industrial specialties. Mr. Dheeraj Talreja, Vice President & Managing Director of Food South Asia at Cargill, expressed confidence in RSGBL's capability, stating, "We wish RSGBL success and are confident that this asset will support their growth as they expand in the starch industry."With over three decades of experience in starch and derivatives, Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd's acquisition of Cargill's assets further solidifies its position in India's starch industry. The seamless integration of this large-scale asset showcases RSGBL’s ability to identify and capitalize on high-value opportunities, reinforcing its reputation as a resilient player in the sector.

+ CARGILL'S US$2.25M COCOA INVESTMENT AIMS TO TRANSFORM INDONESIAN FARMING

Cargill has announced an investment of approximately Rp38.8 billion (US$2.25M) to support over 7,800 cocoa farmers in Indonesia through the Rainforest Alliance certification program. This move, revealed on the 26th of April 2026, underscores Cargill's ongoing commitment to sustainable sourcing and farmer empowerment in the cocoa industry.The premium payments include roughly Rp35 billion (US$2.02M) directed towards farmers in Central Sulawesi and Rp3.8 billion allocated to those in East Nusa Tenggara. These payments, which supplement farmers' income from cocoa bean sales, are contingent upon adherence to globally recognized sustainability standards, reflecting Cargill's dedication to ethical and responsible sourcing practices.Cargill’s initiative is part of a more extensive cocoa sourcing program designed to enhance productivity, ensure quality, and improve market access for farmers. Brook Chang, Director of Sustainability for Food APAC at Cargill, emphasized the importance of supporting farmers in building a resilient supply chain. He stated, "By investing in training, certification, and premium payments linked to verified sustainability standards, we are helping farmers improve income and productivity while strengthening traceability and supply reliability for our customers and partners."In Central Sulawesi, over 5,600 farmers are engaged in the program, including more than 600 women farmers. From May 2023 to February 2026, approximately 16,000 metric tonnes of Rainforest Alliance-certified cocoa beans were sourced from regions such as Parigi Moutong, Poso, and North Morowali. Meanwhile, in East Nusa Tenggara, about 2,200 farmers contributed close to 2,000 metric tonnes of certified cocoa beans from Ende, Sikka, EastManggarai, and East Flores regencies during the same timeframe.The certified cocoa sourced from these farmers integrates into Cargill's global supply chain, benefiting a variety of food and beverage manufacturers. The Rainforest Alliance certification framework promotes transparency through detailed record-keeping and traceability systems, enabling farmers to meet international market standards. Lukmansyah, Team Manager for Cocoa at Rainforest Alliance Indonesia, noted that certification offers farmers a structured approach to agricultural practices and traceability, thereby enhancing the overall cocoa production process.

+ CARGILL'S REGINA CANOLA FACILITY SETS NEW STANDARDS FOR DOMESTIC PROCESSING

Cargill has officially opened its new canola processing facility in Regina, Saskatchewan, significantly enhancing the local agricultural landscape. This development, announced on 22nd April 2026 at 16:36:58.001272, aims to bolster market access for western Canadian farmers while addressing the surging global demand for food and renewable fuel alternatives.With an impressive capacity to process 1 million metric tons of canola annually, Cargill's Regina facility is poised to increase local demand and facilitate the domestic processing of canola into high-value products. These include oil for both food consumption and renewable fuels, as well as high-protein meal for animal feed. The facility is strategically located to serve growers across Saskatchewan and western Manitoba.Jeff Vassart, president of Cargill Canada, emphasized the importance of the new facility, stating, "This facility strengthens our ability to connect Canadian farmers to growing global demand for food and renewable fuels." He underscored that expanding processing capacity in Saskatchewan creates more opportunities for farmers and helps Canada maintain its competitive edge in rapidly evolving global markets.As the demand for low-carbon energy solutions rises, canola is increasingly recognized as a vital renewable feedstock for biofuels. Cargill's investment in the Regina facility will not only meet this demand but also reduce the reliance on exporting raw canola seed, thereby enabling more value creation within Canada and improving supply chain efficiency for farmers and customers alike.Situated at Saskatchewan's Global Transportation Hub, Cargill's facility offers efficient rail access to both domestic and export markets. The site is expected to create over 100 jobs in the region, contributing to economic growth across transportation, logistics, and service sectors in Regina and its surrounding communities. By continuing to invest in Canada's agriculture sector, Cargill aims to strengthen supply chains and support local farmers, with existing processing facilities already operational in Camrose, Alberta, and Clavet, Saskatchewan.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ INGREDION [3 News That Matters]

+ INGREDION TO UNVEIL INNOVATIVE PROTEIN AND SUGAR SOLUTIONS AT VITAFOODS

Manchester, UK – Ingredion Incorporated will showcase its latest advances in protein fortification, texture enhancement, and sugar reduction at Vitafoods Europe 2026 in Barcelona, helping brands meet EMEA consumers’ rising expectations for great taste, appealing texture, and ingredient lists that support clean-label appeal. The date of this announcement is 23rd April 2026.As demand increases for simpler, more recognisable components, Ingredion will demonstrate how its integrated ingredient portfolio assists brands in delivering these preferences while enhancing positive nutrition characteristics, such as protein enrichment and reduced sugar, which consumers increasingly prioritise. The showcase will feature innovative products designed to elevate the overall consumer experience.Visitors to Ingredion's stand will have the opportunity to explore VITESSENCE® Pea 100 HD, a cutting-edge pea protein ideal for cold-pressed applications. The product maintains softness throughout its shelf life while enhancing texture and providing effective protein fortification. VITESSENCE® Pea 100 HD also demonstrates versatility in bakery applications, enabling protein enrichment without compromising quality or taste.According to Ingredion's insights, texture plays a crucial role in consumer satisfaction, with many consumers likely to recommend products based solely on their texture. This trend highlights the importance for brands to elevate texture to secure consumer preference and loyalty in a discerning marketplace. Additionally, sugar reduction continues to be a significant focus in product development across EMEA, with many consumers prioritising sugar-reduction claims when selecting packaged foods and beverages.Beyond the innovations presented at Vitafoods, Ingredion continues to advance its strategic partnership with Lantm nnen, which was first announced in 2024. This collaboration underscores Ingredion's commitment to European-sourced, plant-based protein solutions, enhancing regional sourcing capabilities and supporting the development of sustainable protein ingredients for customers in Europe. To discover more about Ingredion’s ingredient solutions, visit stand number #3D252 at Vitafoods Europe in Barcelona, Spain.

+ BARCLAYS HIGHLIGHTS INGREDION'S STRATEGIC SHIFT TOWARDS AI SOLUTIONS

Barclays has released a report analyzing the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) across the agribusiness sector in the Americas, noting that Ingredion Incorporated (NYSE:INGR) is particularly well-positioned to benefit in the near term. The report, published on 22nd April 2026, highlights how Ingredion is transitioning its business model from a commodity-focused approach to a solutions-oriented strategy.The investment bank asserts that Ingredion can leverage its extensive library of ingredients and formulations to meet unmet customer needs effectively. This strategic pivot is expected to enable the company to capitalize on emerging AI technologies more rapidly than its competitors. Barclays emphasized that companies like Ingredion that possess strong balance sheets are better positioned to monetize AI opportunities in the current environment.Looking towards the medium term, Barclays identified seeds and crop chemicals as sectors likely to see significant AI-driven advancements. These developments are anticipated to lead to faster product development cycles, enhanced pricing power, and improved structural efficiencies. While Ingredion and Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) are highlighted as frontrunners in AI adoption, other companies in the sector, such as FMC (NYSE:FMC), may face delays due to internal restructuring challenges.In the realm of protein processing, Barclays noted that AI could enable processors to extract greater value from livestock through automation and yield improvements. This could also result in fewer product recalls, further enhancing profitability in the sector. Overall, the bank suggests that AI will reinforce market leaders by driving innovation, reducing costs, and improving capital efficiency across the agriculture landscape.Barclays' analysis underscores the critical role that AI is expected to play in shaping the future of agribusiness. As companies like Ingredion transition to more innovative models, they are likely to gain a competitive edge in this evolving market. Investors will be closely watching how these developments unfold, particularly in light of the significant potential for AI to transform agricultural practices.

+ INGREDION PARTNERS WITH CIBO TO ENHANCE SUSTAINABLE SOURCING

Ingredion Incorporated, a leading global ingredient solutions provider, has announced a strategic partnership with CIBO Technologies, a prominent independent data and analytics platform for agriculture. This collaboration aims to advance regenerative agriculture across supply chains, marking a significant step in Ingredion’s commitment to sustainability. The announcement was made on 1st April 2026.CIBO's partnership with Ingredion is noteworthy as it represents the company's fourth strategic collaboration in 2026, reflecting its rapid growth in the climate-tech sector within agriculture and food industries. + GENERAL MILLS MOVES TO STRENGTHEN CAPITAL STRUCTURE WITH €1.7 BILLION NOTE OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) has announced the issuance of €1.7 billion in junior subordinated notes, designed to bolster its capital structure and provide financial flexibility. The offering, made public on 14th April 2026, includes €1 billion of 4.750% Series A notes and €700 million of 5.250% Series B notes, both of which feature fixed-to-fixed reset rate structures.The Series A notes will maintain a fixed interest rate of 4.750% until 16th July 2031, after which the rate will reset based on the Five-Year Swap Rate plus an initial margin of 202.4 basis points. The Series B notes will carry a 5.250% rate until 16th July 2034, with subsequent resets tied to the same swap rate plus an initial margin of 239.0 basis points. This structured approach aims to provide a predictable income stream for investors while offering General Mills a flexible financing option.Both series of notes come with step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin beginning five years after their respective first reset dates, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset dates. The interest payments for these notes are scheduled to occur annually on 16th July, commencing in 2026, which aligns with the company's cash flow management strategy.General Mills retains the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years during any deferral period, with any deferred payments accruing additional interest at the applicable rate. This feature provides the company with a buffer in case of unforeseen financial pressures, allowing it to maintain operational stability.Additionally, the company has the option to redeem the notes at any time under the terms outlined in the prospectus supplement. The classification of these notes as junior subordinated debt indicates a lower claim on assets in the event of liquidation, but it also suggests a strategic move by General Mills to leverage its capital structure for future growth initiatives.

On 11th February 2026, the company announced that these new offerings will provide 30 grams of protein per bowl, catering to the growing trend in the $626 million breakfast bowls category.The Banquet MEGA Breakfast Bowls aim to deliver a hearty and satisfying breakfast experience. With protein being the top attribute sought by consumers, Conagra Brands has strategically developed this product to ensure that it resonates with health-conscious individuals looking for convenient meal solutions. Each bowl is designed to be ready in minutes, providing an accessible option for busy mornings.Equipped with innovative tray-in-tray steaming technology, these breakfast bowls ensure that ingredients maintain their quality during the cooking process. The design separates the sauce from the other components while frozen, allowing for a perfect steaming experience that keeps eggs fluffy, potatoes tender, and proteins juicy. Once heated, consumers can easily mix the sauce with the other ingredients for a flavorful meal.The Banquet MEGA Breakfast Bowls come in four enticing varieties: the Meat Lovers option features scrambled eggs, cooked sausage, roasted potatoes, ham, bacon, and cheddar cheese sauce; the Bacon option includes scrambled eggs, roasted potatoes, bacon, and cheddar cheese sauce; the Sausage Bowl contains scrambled eggs, cooked sausage, roasted potatoes, and cheddar cheese sauce; and the Sausage & Gravy variety combines scrambled eggs, cooked sausage, roasted potatoes, country gravy, and shredded cheese.As a market leader in single-serve frozen meals, breakfast pot pies, and frozen cooked sausage links, Conagra Brands continues to innovate and expand its product offerings. The Banquet MEGA Breakfast Bowls will begin rolling out to grocery and mass retail stores throughout February, positioning Conagra Brands to capitalize on the growing consumer preference for protein-packed breakfast options.

+ PERDUE FARMS INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ PERDUE FARMS ACKNOWLEDGES OHIO COUNTY CARE WORKERS WITH PROTEIN DONATION

HENDERSON, Ky. — Perdue Farms is showcasing their appreciation to healthcare workers and first responders in Ohio County. On the 27th of April 2026, the organization delivered a truckload of chicken products to 1,000 healthcare workers and first responders to Ohio County Healthcare. Officials with the company say it is a show of appreciation for their service and commitment to the community.Perdue Farms' Director of Operations, Allen Pavlovich, emphasized the importance of healthcare professionals and first responders, stating, "These individuals ensure the health and safety of their communities every day and are the backbone of Ohio County. This is a small way to show our gratitude for their tireless service and help nourish them and their families."This initiative is part of Perdue Farms' Delivering Hope To Our Neighbors outreach program, which aims to improve quality of life and build strong communities where associates live, work, and raise their families. The focus on community support aligns with the increasing emphasis on the intersection of agriculture and health.Shellie Shouse, CEO of Ohio County Healthcare, praised the long-standing relationship between her organization and Perdue Farms, noting, "Ohio County Healthcare has worked alongside Perdue Farms for more than 30 years to provide outstanding care to the communities we serve. As a proud supporter of the statewide Food Is Medicine initiative, OCH values the alignment between healthcare and agriculture in expanding access to nutritious food."Shouse added, "Perdue’s generous donation of a healthy protein source to each OCH employee and local first responders is a meaningful way to say thank you to the men and women who answer the call without hesitation and care for our community when it matters most." This gesture not only reinforces Perdue Farms' commitment to local communities but also highlights the vital role of nutritious food in supporting healthcare professionals.

+ PERDUE FARMS APPOINTS ANDY URBAN AS CHIEF COMMERCIAL OFFICER

Perdue Foods has announced the appointment of Andy Urban as chief commercial officer, a strategic move aimed at enhancing its commercial operations. As of 8th April 2026, Urban will oversee the company’s sales, marketing, customer development, revenue management, and innovation efforts, all designed to drive disciplined growth and strengthen customer partnerships.With over 35 years of experience in leading complex, multi-channel consumer packaged goods businesses, Urban has a wealth of expertise across various sectors including retail, food service, and international markets. He has been with Perdue since 2009, during which he has held multiple senior leadership roles. Most recently, he served as senior vice president of the Transformation Office, where he spearheaded initiatives to streamline operations and enhance execution throughout the organization.Kevin McAdams, president and CEO of Perdue Farms, expressed confidence in Urban’s capabilities, stating, “Andy is a proven commercial leader with a deep understanding of our business, our customers and consumers, and our culture.” McAdams emphasized that Urban’s track record in driving growth and building high-performing teams positions him well to lead Perdue Foods’ commercial organization in an increasingly competitive marketplace.Prior to his current role, Urban was senior vice president and general manager of Perdue Premium Meat Co., managing multi-billion-dollar portfolios with full P&L responsibility. His career at Perdue also includes senior sales leadership positions where he directed retail sales across all major channels. Before joining Perdue, Urban held commercial leadership roles at WhiteWave Foods and ConAgra Foods.As chief commercial officer, Urban aims to accelerate customer-centric growth, enhance commercial capabilities, and foster innovation. “I’m honored to step into this role at a pivotal time for Perdue,” Urban remarked, highlighting the company’s strong foundation and the importance of collaboration with customers and associates to drive sustainable growth and deliver value across the business.

+ PERDUE FARMS CONTRIBUTES $100K TO DELAWARE VALLEY UNIVERSITY'S POULTRY SCIENCE CENTER

Delaware Valley University (DelVal) has received a significant $100,000 contribution from Maryland-based Perdue Farms, along with its charitable partner, the Franklin P. and Arthur W. Perdue Foundation, to fund the construction of a new Poultry Science Center on its main campus. This generous gift, announced on 9th February 2026, underscores Perdue's commitment to nurturing the next generation of agricultural professionals while aligning with its Delivering Hope To Our Neighbors® initiative aimed at enhancing community well-being.The grant was presented to DelVal President Dr. Benjamin E. Rusiloski and Dean of Agriculture and Environmental Science Dr. Broc Sandelin during a ceremony on Wednesday. Dr. Sandelin expressed appreciation for the support, emphasizing the growing need for skilled professionals in the poultry industry. He noted that the new Poultry Science Center will not only provide hands-on learning opportunities but will also support the introduction of a dedicated Poultry Science academic program at the university.As the poultry sector continues to expand, DelVal anticipates an increased demand for trained professionals. The state-of-the-art Poultry Science Center will feature various facilities, including a broiler building, layer building, and pen research trials building, along with laboratories, classrooms, and demonstration spaces. In recognition of Perdue’s ongoing contributions, a study room within the new center will bear the company’s name, reflecting its pivotal role in advancing poultry science education.Mike Levengood, Chief Animal Care Officer and Farmer Relationship Advocate at Perdue, stated, 'Investing in education and workforce development is crucial for the future of agriculture.' He highlighted that this investment strengthens hands-on learning opportunities and prepares students for significant careers in the poultry industry, ensuring that future agricultural leaders possess the necessary knowledge and experience to innovate within the field.DelVal is actively engaging with potential partners in the poultry industry to finalize the project. Interested parties are encouraged to connect with the Office of Development and Alumni Affairs at development@delval.edu or by calling 215.489.2397. The Franklin P. and Arthur W. Perdue Foundation, established in 1957, supports community initiatives and grants on behalf of Perdue Farms, further reinforcing the company 's commitment to responsible food and agriculture.

+ ARCHER DANIELS MIDLAND [3 News That Matters]

+ ADM A ROPORTS DE MONTR AL ENHANCES CONTRACTOR COMPLIANCE WITH VERIFORCE CANADA PARTNERSHIP

MONTR AL, 15th April 2026 /CNW/ - ADM A roports de Montr al has announced a strategic partnership with Veriforce Canada, the country’s largest network of contractor and client relationships, to bolster contractor compliance and training management across its operations.Operating within a heavily regulated environment, ADM requires structured compliance processes to effectively manage contractors in the complex setting of an active airport. Through SafeContractor Canada, a product offered by Veriforce Canada, ADM aims to implement a centralized solution designed to streamline contractor requirements and enhance governance across various projects.The SafeContractor platform will grant ADM real-time visibility into contractor qualifications, certifications, and training records, facilitating informed decision-making while alleviating the administrative burdens associated with onboarding and site access. Jean Charron, Deputy Director of Occupational Health and Safety (Construction) at ADM, stated, "Rigorous adherence to compliance standards is fundamental to the management of our projects, the protection of our users, airport workers, and the integrity of our operations."Henriette Bichai, President of Veriforce Canada, emphasized the importance of consistency and accountability in highly regulated environments such as airports. She remarked, "Our solutions are built to support compliance in critical infrastructure settings. We are proud to work with ADM to strengthen contractor standards and support the safe and efficient delivery of its development initiatives."Veriforce Canada, with a workforce of 1,800 employees globally, including 300 in Canada, specializes in helping organizations mitigate risk and enhance governance through its innovative technology and health and safety expertise. This partnership with ADM A roports de Montr al marks a significant step towards achieving higher compliance standards within the aviation sector.

+ ARCHER-DANIELS-MIDLAND CO EXPANDS BOARD TO ENHANCE SOUTH AMERICAN STRATEGY

Archer-Daniels-Midland Co (NYSE:ADM) has announced a strategic move to strengthen its board of directors by appointing Michael McMurray, an industry veteran with over 30 years of experience. This key decision, made on 31st March 2026, comes as the company seeks to bolster its operations in South America, a region that is increasingly important to its overall growth strategy.McMurray's appointment is expected to bring invaluable expertise to Archer-Daniels-Midland Co, particularly in areas of financial management and strategic leadership. He has previously held senior roles at major companies such as LyondellBasell Industries and Royal Dutch Shell. Juan Luciano, the CEO and board chair of Archer-Daniels-Midland Co, emphasized McMurray's proven track record in business strategy and mergers and acquisitions, which are critical as the company navigates the complexities of expanding its footprint in South America.In addition to McMurray's appointment, Archer-Daniels-Midland Co is actively engaging with investors and industry stakeholders to outline its strategic initiatives. Representatives from the company recently participated in the Bank of America 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference, where they highlighted both opportunities and challenges facing the agricultural sector. This engagement underscores the company's commitment to transparency and collaboration as it works to enhance its market position.The South American market presents a range of opportunities for Archer-Daniels-Midland Co, particularly in agriculture and food production. As global demand for sustainable agricultural practices continues to rise, the company is well-positioned to leverage its resources and expertise to meet these needs. The addition of McMurray to the board is seen as a proactive step in aligning the company's leadership with its ambitious growth objectives in this critical region.Overall, Archer-Daniels-Midland Co's strategic board appointment reflects its dedication to strengthening governance and enhancing operational capabilities. As the company continues to focus on its South American operations, investors will be keenly watching how these developments unfold and the impact they may have on the company's long-term growth trajectory.

+ ADM REVEALS PLANS FOR NEW GLOBAL HEADQUARTERS TO ENHANCE CUSTOMER FOCUS

Archer Daniels Midland Company (NYSE: ADM) announced plans to explore the establishment of a new global headquarters and customer center, a move aimed at enhancing its global presence and customer-centric operations. The decision was revealed by Patricia Woertz, ADM's chairman and chief executive officer, who emphasized the necessity of a central location that facilitates efficient engagement with customers and employees globally. The announcement comes on 30th March 2026, highlighting ADM's commitment to growth and adaptability in an evolving market.As part of this initiative, ADM is currently evaluating potential locations and engaging with public officials and advisors, though details regarding specific sites remain undisclosed. The company plans to relocate a small corporate team to the new global center, which will involve approximately 100 job transfers, alongside the establishment of a new IT tech center that is expected to create an additional 100 positions in the coming years. Importantly, Woertz confirmed that this transition will not result in any layoffs.Despite the impending move, ADM reassured its commitment to its Decatur workforce, where 4,400 employees will continue to operate. To underscore its dedication to the local community, the company announced that its existing office in Decatur will be designated as the North American headquarters. This strategic positioning aims to maintain a strong presence in Decatur, which has been a key location for ADM's operations for over a century.In support of the Decatur community, ADM unveiled several multi-year financial commitments intended to bolster local economic development, enhance workforce training, and improve social services. This includes an annual investment of $250,000 for three years to support a unified marketing campaign through the Economic Development Corporation of Decatur &Macon County, as well as $500,000 per year for five years dedicated to Decatur Public School District 61. Additionally, the company will sustain its community support contributions at $1 million per year for at least the next decade.Looking ahead, Woertz expressed optimism about ADM's future, particularly with the anticipated large North American harvest and the completion of its acquisition of GrainCorp. The establishment of a new global center is part of ADM's strategy to position itself for sustained success and continued growth in the agricultural sector, as it remains committed to transforming crops into essential products for global markets.

+ BUNGE GLOBAL [3 News That Matters]

+ BUNGE GLOBAL SA EXPANDS SECURITIZATION PROGRAM TO $2 BILLION WITH FINACITY'S SUPPORT

Bunge Global SA has successfully expanded its Accounts Receivable Securitization Program to $2 billion, up from a previous commitment of $1.5 billion, thanks to the facilitation by Finacity Corporation. This announcement was made on April 13th, 2026, and highlights the ongoing collaboration between the two companies in enhancing financial strategies tailored to the evolving needs of the agribusiness sector.As part of the expansion, Finacity also supported the integration of Viterra Limited, a recently merged entity, into the securitization program. This addition not only increases the capacity of the program but also strengthens Bunge's operational framework and its ability to manage receivables effectively. Bunge, a leader in the agribusiness industry, focuses on connecting farmers and consumers globally, delivering essential food, feed, and fuel.Bunge Global SA's mission is reinforced through this significant increase in commitment capacity. With a workforce of approximately 34,000 employees and a presence in over 50 countries, Bunge's operations encompass grain origination, storage, distribution, and oilseed processing. The company's commitment to sustainability and food security is supported by its innovative solutions that cater to evolving consumer demands.Finacity, a White Oak Global Advisors Company, specializes in structuring efficient capital market solutions and has successfully facilitated over $200 billion in annual receivables volume. With operations spanning the globe, its expertise in off-balance sheet securitizations under both US GAAP and IFRS positions it as a critical partner for Bunge as it navigates the complexities of the agricultural market.This enhanced capacity in Bunge Global SA's Accounts Receivable Securitization Program is expected to provide the company with greater flexibility and stability in financing its operations. As Bunge continues to innovate and expand its product offerings, the partnership with Finacity underscores its strategic approach to maintaining a competitive edge in the agribusiness landscape.

+ BUNGE GLOBAL SEES RATING UPGRADE AMID MIDDLE EAST TENSIONS

Bunge Global SA (NYSE: BG) has received a rating upgrade, reflecting a positive outlook driven by geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East. The conflict has triggered a surge in oil prices, resulting in improved margins for soybean oil and other essential commodities. This has positioned Bunge Global favorably within the agricultural sector, as demand for soy oil, a key component in biodiesel production, has increased significantly.As noted in an analysis dated 26th March 2026, Bunge Global anticipates that the current geopolitical climate will serve as a short-term catalyst for their performance. However, the company is equally focused on long-term execution strategies that leverage these external factors. The recent rise in soybean prices is indicative of the broader market dynamics influenced by the ongoing conflict, which Bunge Global aims to capitalize on.Bunge Global's strategic initiatives are expected to yield strong synergies, with projections indicating an earnings per share (EPS) of $15. This positive outlook has led analysts to upgrade the stock rating from Hold to Buy, with potential upside ranging from 74% to 185%. Investors are encouraged to consider this rating adjustment as the company navigates both immediate challenges and long-term growth opportunities.The agricultural sector's response to geopolitical shifts has historically been profound, and Bunge Global's positioning appears well-aligned to harness these market movements. The company's focus on innovation and efficiency in sourcing and production will be critical as they manage the impact of fluctuating commodity prices driven by external conflicts. Investors are advised to monitor Bunge Global closely as it embarks on this promising trajectory.In conclusion, Bunge Global is poised to benefit from current market conditions while executing its long-term strategies. With a favorable rating upgrade and a focus on enhancing operational synergies, the company presents a compelling case for investment as it positions itself to thrive amid a changing geopolitical landscape.

+ BUNGE GLOBAL SA'S PRICE TARGET RAISED BY BOFA AMID POSITIVE INVESTOR DAY INSIGHTS

Bunge Global SA (NYSE:BG) has garnered renewed attention from analysts following a positive outlook from Bank of America (BofA), which has raised its price target for the agribusiness giant to $130 from $125. On 26th March 2026, BofA reiterated its Buy rating on the shares, citing the company's recent Investor Day as a key factor in its decision. The firm noted that the event revealed higher-than-expected midcycle earnings per share (EPS) projections of $13 to $15, along with an increased synergy target and discussions about additional margin-enhancing opportunities. BofA characterized the Investor Day as a significant hurdle that investors had previously feared might yield a disappointing outlook, but with this concern now alleviated, the firm believes the stock has the potential to gain further traction.In addition to BofA's bullish stance, Bunge Global SA also received an upgrade from BMO Capital on the same day. BMO raised its price target to $135 from $130, maintaining an Outperform rating for the stock. The firm expressed increased confidence in Bunge's multi-year earnings trajectory, especially after the management laid out a roadmap to achieve the $15 mid-cycle EPS target by 2030 during the Investor Day presentation. This dual endorsement from prominent financial institutions underscores a growing optimism about Bunge Global SA's future performance in the agribusiness sector.Bunge Global SA operates as a leading global agribusiness and food company, offering a diverse array of products, including canned and frozen vegetables, spices, and various food items marketed under well-known brands such as Green Giant and Mrs. Dash. The company's expansive portfolio positions it favorably in the marketplace, allowing it to capitalize on evolving consumer preferences and demand for food products. Analysts believe that Bunge's ability to leverage its combined platform and technology will enhance its operational efficiencies and profitability in the coming years.While Bunge Global SA's stock is seen as a solid investment opportunity, some analysts caution that there are other sectors, particularly in artificial intelligence, that may offer higher potential returns in a shorter timeframe. Investors are encouraged to evaluate their portfolios carefully, and while Bunge presents a defensive option, the allure of AI stocks with significant growth potential cannot be overlooked. As the market continues to evolve, the comparative analysis between traditional agribusiness stocks and emerging tech stocks will remain a critical consideration for investors.In conclusion, Bunge Global SA is positioned for growth as it navigates through positive analyst sentiments and strategic initiatives. The recent upgrades from BofA and BMO Capital reflect a broader confidence in the company's ability to enhance its earnings trajectory and deliver value to shareholders. value.Title: April 28, 2026: Cargill asset sale to RSGBL strengthens starch operations with 300,000 t/yr Davangere facility

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-RVD3-RS9K-G52H-00000-00April 28, 2026: Cargill asset sale to RSGBL strengthens starch operations with 300,000 t/yr Davangere facility2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Cargill asset sale to RSGBL strengthens starch operations with 300,000 t/yr Davangere facility

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd (RSGBL) has completed the acquisition of Cargill's Davangere corn milling plant and related assets in Karnataka, finalizing the deal on 28th April 2026 under an Asset Purchase Agreement. The package covers land, production facilities, warehouses and silos, expanding RSGBL's southern India footprint.

The Davangere facility, in operation since 2016, processes about 300,000 metric tons annually, strengthening RSGBL's ability to serve food, pharmaceutical and industrial applications and supporting its sustainability drive with increased renewable energy use.

Executive Director Siddharth Chowdhary stated, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals." The company expects to leverage the asset to meet rising domestic and export demand and push efficiency improvements.

Cargill India, present since 1987, entrusted RSGBL with this asset in a bid to support its starch business; Dheeraj Talreja, Vice President & Managing Director of Food South Asia at Cargill, commented, "We wish RSGBL success and are confident that this asset will support their growth as they expand in the starch industry."

INDEX

SECTION 2 CARGILL TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 CARGILL PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Cargill Incorporated, founded in 1865 by William Wallace Cargill, is a global agribusiness leader headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 155,000 employees across 70 countries, it provides food, agriculture, and industrial products, generating $177 billion in 2023 revenue. Cargill processes crops, trades commodities, and produces animal nutrition, serving clients like food manufacturers and farmers. Its focus on sustainability includes regenerative agriculture and carbon reduction goals. Competing with ADM, Cargills family-owned structure and innovation in supply chain solutions maintain its dominance in global agriculture markets.

Mission and Vision

Mission:"To nourish the world in a safe, responsible, and sustainable way."

Vision:To be a trusted partner in food, agriculture, financial, and industrial sectors, connecting farmers to markets and customers to essential ingredients.

Core Values

Innovation:Merging over 158 years of expertise with cutting-edge solutions.

Responsibility:Upholding safe, ethical, and sustainable business practices.

Community Engagement:Strengthening local communities through economic development, partnerships, and volunteerism.

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd Expands Operations with Cargill Asset Acquisition

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-3VX3-RV59-42G9-00000-00April 28, 2026: Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd Expands Operations with Cargill Asset Acquisition2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd Expands Operations with Cargill Asset Acquisition

Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd. (RSGBL) has successfully completed the acquisition of a corn milling plant and related assets in Davangere, Karnataka from Cargill. This strategic transaction was finalized on 28th April 2026, in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement signed earlier. The acquisition includes an integrated manufacturing facility, encompassing land, production infrastructure, warehouses, and corn silos, thereby enhancing RSGBL's operational footprint in southern India.

The Davangere facility, operational since 2016, boasts an annual processing capacity of 300,000 metric tons. This significant acquisition marks a pivotal step in RSGBL's growth journey, expanding its capacity to cater to the food, pharmaceutical, and industrial sectors while reaffirming its commitment to sustainable and efficient manufacturing practices. Mr. Siddharth Chowdhary, Executive Director of RSGBL, expressed optimism regarding the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to mark the successful completion of this acquisition, which strengthens our manufacturing capabilities and expands our presence in food and pharmaceuticals."

The strategic location of the Davangere facility will enhance RSGBL's ability to serve both domestic and export markets efficiently. The company is also focused on sustainable operations at the facility, including increased utilization of renewable energy sources, reflecting its commitment to operational excellence. This acquisition will enable RSGBL to create long-term value for stakeholders while setting new benchmarks within the industry.

Cargill India has been present in India since 1987 and operates across various sectors, including refined oils, food ingredients, and industrial specialties. Mr. Dheeraj Talreja, Vice President & Managing Director of Food South Asia at Cargill, expressed confidence in RSGBL's capability, stating, "We wish RSGBL success and are confident that this asset will support their growth as they expand in the starch industry."

With over three decades of experience in starch and derivatives, Riddhi Siddhi Gluco Biols Ltd's acquisition of Cargill's assets further solidifies its position in India's starch industry. The seamless integration of this large-scale asset showcases RSGBL’s ability to identify and capitalize on high-value opportunities, reinforcing its reputation as a resilient player in the sector.

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

MADISON, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Alliant Energy Corporation has shown some weakness in recent days. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, dropped 1.0% during its previous trading session to close at $72.31.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 321 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Sets Sights on Data Center Demand Amid Utility Stability

Alliant Energy Corp. is strategically positioning itself to address the surging electricity demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure across the Midwest. As of 15th April 2026, the utility provider serves over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its primary subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company in Iowa and Wisconsin Power and Light Company in Wisconsin.

With a regulated utility model, Alliant Energy earns returns that are approved by state commissions, providing a buffer against volatile economic cycles. The company currently manages its energy generation through a balanced portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. Ongoing capital plans are focused on grid modernization and expanding renewable energy resources to comply with clean energy mandates and capitalize on federal decarbonization incentives.

The demand for steady power is projected to rise significantly, driven by an anticipated 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. According to industry experts, utilities like Alliant Energy anchor their portfolios by concentrating on essential infrastructure, particularly during periods of market uncertainty. The emergence of hyperscaler-driven data centers in the Upper Midwest necessitates reliable baseload power, a demand that aligns with Alliant Energy's regional capabilities.

This structural shift in energy demand supports Alliant Energy's disciplined capital approach, which aims for an annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. Analysts generally hold a favorable view of the company due to its rate base growth and a consistent track record of dividends. Despite concerns regarding interest rate sensitivity, Alliant Energy's low-leverage strategy is designed to outpace inflation while ensuring service reliability, further solidifying its position in the market.

As Alliant Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, its focus on data center growth not only enhances its utility stability but also positions the company for sustainable long-term success. With a commitment to meeting the energy needs of the future, Alliant Energy is poised to play a critical role in the region's economic development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 363 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's New Strategy Focuses on Powering Data Centers in the Midwest

Alliant Energy Corporation has announced a strategic pivot aimed at addressing the growing electricity demands of hyperscale data centers in the Midwest. On the 13th of April 2026, the company revealed plans to leverage its regulated utility model to ensure grid reliability while targeting an ambitious annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. This shift reflects a broader strategy to modernize the Midwest energy grid, aligning with the increasing demand for robust digital infrastructure.

Serving over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light and Wisconsin Power and Light, Alliant Energy is responding to a projected 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. This strategic adaptation allows Alliant Energy to utilize its regulated utility framework—earning state-approved returns—to mitigate economic volatility while fulfilling the heavy baseload power requirements of modern data centers. By managing a balanced generation portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal, the company aims to ensure a sustainable energy future.

The utility's focus on grid modernization is designed to align with both federal decarbonization mandates and the technical needs of high-capacity data facilities. Analysts view this move as positioning Alliant Energy as a defensive investment, offering shareholders predictable dividends amid significant transitions in the energy market. The company's commitment to integrating digital infrastructure into its utility model reflects a proactive approach to the evolving energy landscape.

By prioritizing data center integration, Alliant Energy seeks to influence regional energy pricing and service reliability positively. Reports suggest that by aligning its investment strategies with long-term power needs, Alliant Energy intends to adopt a disciplined capital approach aimed at achieving its targeted annual earnings growth. This strategy not only mitigates interest rate sensitivity but also capitalizes on the increasing demand for reliable, large-scale power in the Upper Midwest.

The shift towards integrating data centers into its utility operations represents a pivotal recalibration for Alliant Energy, indicating that traditional power providers are now recognizing digital infrastructure as a key driver of grid modernization and long-term financial stability. As the landscape of energy generation continues to evolve, Alliant Energy's strategic focus on these high-growth sectors positions it favorably for future developments in the industry.

The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $5.58.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 3.0% to 3.2%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in current ratio from 0.4 to 0.8.

But does not meet the following 4 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in gross margin.

- Improvement in asset turnover.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 315 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 363 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's New Strategy Focuses on Powering Data Centers in the Midwest

Alliant Energy Corporation has announced a strategic pivot aimed at addressing the growing electricity demands of hyperscale data centers in the Midwest. On the 13th of April 2026, the company revealed plans to leverage its regulated utility model to ensure grid reliability while targeting an ambitious annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. This shift reflects a broader strategy to modernize the Midwest energy grid, aligning with the increasing demand for robust digital infrastructure.

Serving over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light and Wisconsin Power and Light, Alliant Energy is responding to a projected 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. This strategic adaptation allows Alliant Energy to utilize its regulated utility framework—earning state-approved returns—to mitigate economic volatility while fulfilling the heavy baseload power requirements of modern data centers. By managing a balanced generation portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal, the company aims to ensure a sustainable energy future.

The utility's focus on grid modernization is designed to align with both federal decarbonization mandates and the technical needs of high-capacity data facilities. Analysts view this move as positioning Alliant Energy as a defensive investment, offering shareholders predictable dividends amid significant transitions in the energy market. The company's commitment to integrating digital infrastructure into its utility model reflects a proactive approach to the evolving energy landscape.

By prioritizing data center integration, Alliant Energy seeks to influence regional energy pricing and service reliability positively. Reports suggest that by aligning its investment strategies with long-term power needs, Alliant Energy intends to adopt a disciplined capital approach aimed at achieving its targeted annual earnings growth. This strategy not only mitigates interest rate sensitivity but also capitalizes on the increasing demand for reliable, large-scale power in the Upper Midwest.

The shift towards integrating data centers into its utility operations represents a pivotal recalibration for Alliant Energy, indicating that traditional power providers are now recognizing digital infrastructure as a key driver of grid modernization and long-term financial stability. As the landscape of energy generation continues to evolve, Alliant Energy's strategic focus on these high-growth sectors positions it favorably for future developments in the industry.

- Improvement in asset turnover.

value.Title: Data: 2025 wasn't great for clean energy, but could have been much worse

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-R203-SBYP-F0TN-00000-00Data: 2025 wasn't great for clean energy, but could have been much worse2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisData: 2025 wasn't great for clean energy, but could have been much worse

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. Newstex Authoritative Content is not "read and approved" before it is posted. Accordingly, neither Newstex nor its re-distributors make any claims, promises or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained therein or linked to from such content, nor do they take responsibility for any aspect of such content. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Brian Martucci

May 4th, 2026 ( Minnesota Reformer - Delivered by Newstex )

The July repeal of federal tax credits for most wind energy projects will likely affect business investment for years to come.(Photo by Robert Zullo/ States Newsroom)

Minnesota produced more of its own power in 2025 - most of it from carbon-free sources - as inflation-adjusted electric rates fell slightly from 2024 even as the national average electric bill jumped.

Those are some of the good-news tidbits in the latest Minnesota Energy Factsheet, an annual report from Clean Energy Economy Minnesota in partnership with BloombergNEF and the Business Council for Sustainable Energy.

Planet-warming emissions from power generation held steady in Minnesota last year, bucking a national uptick driven by a mini-boom in coal utilization that could continue into 2026 and perhaps beyond.

Since last May, the Trump administration has issued 'emergency' orders to force at least seven coal-, oil- and gas-fired power plants to keep running past their planned retirement dates. Those orders have cost U.S. ratepayers more than $230 million, the Sierra Club said in March.

'It was quite a turbulent year for clean energy (with) a number of sweeping policy changes, particularly from the federal government,' Trina White, a sustainable finance analyst with BloombergNEF, said in a media briefing ahead of the factsheet's recent release.

White said the July repeal of federal tax credits for electric vehicles and most wind and solar energy projects would affect consumer purchasing behavior and business investment in those industries for years to come.

Minnesota and the U.S. writ large saw a surge in electric vehicle registrations in the third quarter of 2025 ahead of the EV credits' Sept. 30 expiration. Minnesota now has nearly 100,000 EVs on the road, according to the factsheet. But new EV registrations are likely to fall in 2026 without federal tax incentives, White said.

(The recent spike in gas prices might juice EV sales, however.)

Analysts expect a similar surge in wind and solar energy development activity before the tax credits expire on July 4. The law repealing the credits gives developers until 2030 to complete construction on projects that begin by then, so 'it's really about getting projects online in the next few years,' White said.

Minnesota has dozens of large-scale wind and solar projects that have applied to connect to the regional power grid. It's unclear how many will come online, however. Over the past decade, nearly 70% of interconnection requests withdrew before getting built.

Another significant headwind for the clean energy economy last year was the Trump administration's chaotic rollout - and rollback - of tariffs on solar panels, battery components and critical minerals, among other products. BloombergNEF tallied more than 80 tariff policy changes 'that really impacted companies' decision-making over the course of the year' and pushed importers away from lower-cost supply chains controlled by China, White said.

The U.S. Supreme Courtstruck down Trump's broadest tariffs in February, though his administration is actively working to find alternatives that pass muster with the courts.

Still, the prevailing sentiment among the policy analysts, state officials and industry insiders who joined White in the briefing and CEEM officials at a Capitol Rotunda event two weeks earlier is that 2025 could have been worse for an industry that employs tens of thousands of Minnesotans. The carbon-free power law and other supportive state policies provide local tailwinds even as the federal government actively works to stymie wind energy, Pete Wyckoff, deputy commissioner of energy resources at the Minnesota Department of Commerce, said in the briefing.

'Renewables and clean energy are just going gangbusters globally(this) shows that Minnesota is part of the globe even if Washington doesn't want us to be,' he said.

Events half a world away have sent prices for oil, gasoline and other refined products skyward across much of the world, lending new urgency to 'affordability'-based arguments for clean power.

The effective closure of the narrow waterway linking the Persian Gulf and Indian Ocean coupled with significant damage to Qatar's biggest gas exporting terminal have left the world short about 10 million barrels of oil per day and taken around 20% of global liquefied natural gas supply offline. Experts say it will be months - and maybe longer - before markets for liquid fossil fuels recover, if and when vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz returns to prewar levels.

Paul Douglas, a Twin Cities meteorologist and environmental advocate who retired in 2024, told the Capitol audience that the advent of low-cost, locally produced renewable energy is a 'tectonic shift in the landscape.'

'At the end of the day, Americans vote with their wallets. We need more energy than before and we want it to be cheap and secure,' he said.

Link to the original story.

urn:contentItem:6JGY-HMB3-SD84-W3VV-00000-00ICYMI: Monday's Preview: Can Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), continue its recent gains?2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:57:03ZLexisNexisICYMI: Monday's Preview: Can Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), continue its recent gains?

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

MADISON, Friday, May 01 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Alliant Energy Corporation has shown some strength in recent days. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 0.9% during its previous trading session to close at $74.06.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 341 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 228 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Corporation Sets Sights on Infrastructure Expansion Amid Rising Demand

Alliant Energy Corporation is aligning its regulated utility model to capture the rising demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure in Iowa and Wisconsin. As of 13th April 2026, the company aims to provide stable investor returns through its essential electricity and natural gas services.

The utility provider operates primarily through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Alliant currently serves over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers across the Midwest region, highlighting its extensive footprint in a rapidly growing market.

Market data indicates that industrial technology growth is projected at a 6% compound annual growth rate through 2030. This expansion, largely driven by hyperscaler-led data center builds, is expected to significantly increase electricity needs within Alliant Energy's specific geographic footprint, creating opportunities for future revenue growth.

Alliant Energy maintains a diverse generation mix, which includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. This diversification helps reduce fuel cost risks while positioning the company to benefit from federal decarbonization incentives and grid-hardening programs, further enhancing its growth prospects.

Institutional analysts generally maintain hold to buy ratings on Alliant Energy stock, citing its track record of dividend increases and rate base growth. While interest rate sensitivity remains a factor for monitoring, the regulated model is viewed as a hedge against commodity price swings and broader market volatility, ensuring consistent returns for investors.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 315 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Other Bullish Signals

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.8. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 18.3% in the last 3 years. This is considered superior and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 11.0% is better than sector average of 10.7%. This is computed as net profit margin of 18.6% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.17 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.4.

Source: Alliant Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 20, 2026, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/52485/000035254126000007/lnt-20251231.htm. company (Alliant Energy Corporation), Ticker(LNT)

Source: Nasdaq Stock Exchange: https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/stocks/LNT, company (Alliant Energy Corporation), Ticker(LNT)

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Alliant Energy Corporation sees dividend rise for an eighth consecutive year

Alliant Energy Corporation reported dividends per share of $2.08 in the past year, up 5.6% from the previous year. This is the eighth consecutive dividend increase. In the past 8 years average annual compound growth rate of dividends was 6.1%.

3.2 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 10%, EBITDA margin is 34.5% and the sum of the two 44.1% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Alliant Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 20, 2026, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/52485/000035254126000007/lnt-20251231.htm. company (Alliant Energy Corporation), Ticker(LNT)

3.3 Past two-years

- The shares rose $11.49 (24.3%) a year ago which accelerated to $15.36 (26.2%) in the past year.

3.4 Created Market Value [CMV] past 18 yrs of $12.3 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $14.8 billion from $4.2 billion to $19 billion in the last 18 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2.6 billion and Created Market Value of $12.3 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $5.77.

3.5 Satisfies 5 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 3.0% to 3.2%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in current ratio from 0.4 to 0.8.

But does not meet the following 4 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in gross margin.

- Improvement in asset turnover.
